locations.title: Paljassaare Military Building 1
locations.description: A Soviet time military ammo storage building that was built as a part of the naval defence line. The building is in a surprisingly good state and the slowly overgrowing nature provides an interesting atmosphere. The way to this location is from the east side.

locations.title: Paljassaare Military Building 2
locations.description: A Soviet time military ammo storage building that was built as a part of the naval defence line. The building is in a surprisingly good state and the slowly overgrowing nature provides an interesting atmosphere. The way to this location is from the east side.

locations.title: Jesus' Rock
locations.description: A big boulder with "Jeesuse kivi" written on it, which translates into "Jesus' Rock". Just a nice landmark to mark these beautiful stone filled beaches of Paljassaare.

locations.title: Paljassaare Cliff
locations.description: The highest natural lookout point of Paljassaare providing a picturesque view over the sea. A very good location for photography or just to enjoy the view.

locations.title: Paljassaare Naval Defence 1
locations.description: The naval defence battery position was built in the year 1915 and consists of 4 individual artillery stands. The buildings are in a rather good state and offer a glimpse into the military past of the region.

locations.title: Paljassaare Naval Defence 2
locations.description: The naval defence battery position was built in the year 1915 and consists of 4 individual artillery stands. The buildings are in a rather good state and offer a glimpse into the military past of the region.

locations.title: Paljassaare Naval Defence 3
locations.description: The naval defence battery position was built in the year 1915 and consists of 4 individual artillery stands. The buildings are in a rather good state and offer a glimpse into the military past of the region.

locations.title: Paljassaare Birdwatching Tower
locations.description: This 6-meter tall observation tower is located right next to a swamp lake in the middle of Paljassaare which is home to numerous bird species throughout the year.

locations.title: Paljassaare Lookout Tower
locations.description: This 2-storey building that was initially built in the end of the 1980s as a coast guard station, is now used as a sightseeing or birdwatching tower. The lookout area is overlooking the bay of Tallinn and on the other side, the bay of Paljassaare.

locations.title: Paljassaare Wooden Walkway
locations.description: This is a wooden walkway which was built for enjoying the nature around the area at a slight elevation. It is a good alternative path to get to the lookout tower just a bit away from the end of the walkway.

locations.title: Katariina Kai
locations.description: Katariina Kai "The Katherine's Pier" is a historic pier from the Soviet era. The initial construction of the pier was planned in 1911 but it was never finished as planned, it reached a length of 500m at some point and currently stands at 300m long. The pier provides a scenic view over the city with a sandy beach next to it, which makes it a great place to check out.

locations.title: Pikakari Beach
locations.description: A good sand filled beach next to the Katherine's Pier. The beach has a workout ground, a playground, a basketball ground, a grilling area and clothing booths.

locations.title: Kopli Hill
locations.description: The highest hill around which opens a view to the city center, the whole of Paljassaare and you can even see over the landmass of Paljassaare towards the city of Viimsi. The hill is an old trash heap that has been buried and covered and now serves as a viewpoint.

locations.title: Kase Park
locations.description: "The Birch Park" is a nice renovated park with a skatepark, a calisthenics park, ping pong tables and dog parks. The park probably gets the name from having so many birch trees.

locations.title: The Pier of Meerus
locations.description: There used to be a pier here but since then the pier was re-located and after that the area has been for enjoying the view over the bay with a lot of sitting spots that have been built onto the huge boulders of the beach area.

locations.title: Stroomi Beach
locations.description: A widely known beach in Tallinn with a sandy coastline. The seabed is quite shallow and takes a while to get deeper. There are clothing booths on the beach and playgrounds located along the park next to the beach.

locations.title: Merimets
locations.description: A nice pine forest, right next to the Pelguranna area, that has a lot of trails going through it for running, walking, riding a bike.

locations.title: Kakumäe Surveilance Station
locations.description: The tip of the peninsula of Kakumäe has a lot of military structures that were built in 1940 and were used by the coast guard for technical surveilance. Many of the buildings are in ruins but the area is worth checking out, since there are a lot of buildings still standing and the area on it's own is already worth a hike through.

locations.title: Patarei Prison
locations.description: Patarei Prison is a former prison complex located in the Kalamaja district of Tallinn. The prison was in operation from the early 19th century until 2002 and was used by various occupying powers, including the Soviet Union. The site has been described as haunting and eerie, with many of the original prison features still intact.

locations.title: Linnahall
locations.description: Linnahall is a multi-purpose venue that was originally built for the 1980 Summer Olympics. Since then it has been used for different cultural events but now it stands in disrepair and is closed to the public. Currently it is used as a place to spend some time enjoying the nice view it provides over the sea from the roof of the building.

locations.title: Tallinn Port Promenade
locations.description: A promenade which was constructed by the year 2021. The walkway extends quite far onto the Bay of Tallinn and provides many sitting areas with great viewpoints.

locations.title: Järve Forest
locations.description: This is a pine forest with a lot of trails made for running, biking or walking. A good destination to enjoy natural surroundings.

locations.title: The Giant Boulder of Männiku
locations.description: A giant boulder on a small island in the middle of a lake. This is a peaceful area and it would be great for a picnic or for enjoying the scenery of the lake.

locations.title: Männiku Quarry Lake
locations.description: An old sand quarry that has now filled up with water. The lake is sometimes used for swimming or fishing though most of all it is a peaceful scenic area to enjoy.

locations.title: Männiku Hill
locations.description: This is a hill which has a great elevation in comparison to the surrounding landscape and provides a picturesque view over the multiple lakes that are around.

locations.title: Rätsepa Lake
locations.description: "The Taylor's lake" A lake surrounded by a bog. Getting to the lake is a bit difficult as the road is covered with treeroots and some areas are a bit moist but if you are looking for seclusion, then this is a good option.

locations.title: Männiku Forest
locations.description: A rather big pine forest with around 10-20 abandoned Soviet time structures scattered around the forest (mostly ruins). Perfect for a longer walk with the Pääsküla bog nearby as well.

locations.title: Pääsküla Lookout Platform
locations.description: A tall lookout platform which is positioned upon a hill next to the bog of Pääsküla. The tower provides an amazing view over the bog and is a great place for photography as well.

locations.title: Pääsküla Bog
locations.description: A beautiful bog with a lot of paths to take through the fresh air. There is a choice between wooden walkways and gravel roads but both provide a great experience.

locations.title: Park of Glehn
locations.description: This is a beautiful park with diverse nature, located in Tallinn. The park is home to Glehn Castle, several historic buildings, and statues.

locations.title: The Palm House of Glehn
locations.description: This is a unique structure that is located in the Park of Glehn, next to the Glehn Castle. The structure was initially built in the beginning of the 20th century and was renovated in the year 2006. The unique design is very interesting and it looks a bit like a medievil dungeon.

locations.title: Nõmme Viewpoint
locations.description: Nõmme Viewpoint is a good observation point that provides a panoramic view of the city and the surrounding area. The observation point is easily accessible by foot or bike, and there are several hiking trails in the area for those who want to explore the nature around it.

locations.title: Nõmme Sand Dunes
locations.description: The Nõmme sand dunes are a unique natural attraction located in Tallinn. The tall dunes offer a picturesque landscape that is perfect for hiking, walking, and exploring the Park of Sütiste.

locations.title: Park of Sütiste
locations.description: Sütiste park is a beautiful forest park, offering visitors a peaceful natural setting to enjoy various outdoor activities such as hiking, jogging, or cycling. The park features several trails and picnic areas.

locations.title: Harku Forest
locations.description: Harku forest is the largest forest in the vicinity of Tallinn, featuring a 10 km trail and numerous zigzagging paths throughout the area. The landscape, which undulates throughout the forest, adds to the already stunning scenery, making it a great spot for picnicking, cycling, walking or jogging.

locations.title: Harku Quarry Lake
locations.description: The Harku Forest encircles a former quarry turned into a serene lake with sandy beaches, equipped with grills and seating areas.

locations.title: Lasnamäe Cliff
locations.description: Lasnamäe pank is a limestone cliff located in the eastern part of Tallinn. It provides a picturesque view of the surrounding area and you can see the city center from here.

locations.title: Kadrioru Park
locations.description: Kadriorg Park is a vast and picturesque park located in the heart of Tallinn. The park features several walking trails, beautiful gardens, historic buildings, and fountains. It's a great place to relax, have a picnic, and enjoy the scenery. Some notable landmarks in the park include the Kadriorg Palace, Kumu Art Museum, and the Japanese Garden.

locations.title: Luigetiik
locations.description: Luigetiik is the most famous of the ponds in Kadriorg Park, initially planned in 1723 as a lower pond. A pavilion with a small island was added in 1741, and in the 1930s, the area underwent renovations to include grassy fields, flower beds, and hedges. It is known for its beautiful scenery and is great for strolling, picnicking, and bird watching.

locations.title: Pae Park
locations.description: Pae Park is a small but cozy park in the Lasnamäe district of Tallinn. The park offers a playground for children, a basketball court, and plenty of green space for picnics and relaxation. The highlight of the park is a small lake with a fountain, surrounded by benches for taking in the peaceful scenery.

locations.title: Iru Trenches
locations.description: These trenches were constructed in the year 1917 as a defence position for the Soviet Union. Now a lot of the trenches are overgrown by nature but it is an interesting sight to see.

locations.title: Pirita Forest
locations.description: Pirita Forest is one of the most highly rated forests within the vicinity of Tallinn located next to the Pirita River. The landscape varies and has an abundance of trails and is a popular forest for hiking, cycling and even skiing during the winter.

locations.title: Orbita Radar Station
locations.description: This is a former Soviet era radar station that was constructed around the 1950-1960. It is an abandoned facility that has been dug into a hill with 2 floors inside. The structure is in a rather good state, most of the metal has been looted but the locations is rather unique and is definitely a good point of interest.

locations.title: Pirita Beach
locations.description: Pirita beach is the biggest sand beach in Tallinn and is a favorite among the locals. The beach is around 2km in length and provides changing booths, workout grounds and cafes in the area.

locations.title: Lookout point of Lükati
locations.description: Right next to the Pirita river, the lookout point is positioned on a hill and provides a view over the nature filled scenery below. The location is easily accessible by car as well as on foot.

locations.title: Tondiraba Park
locations.description: Located in Lasnamäe, the park was renovated in 2021 and provides a lot of different amenities. The park includes a skatepark, multiple workout grounds, easy trails in the middle of the park for a more nature filled experience, a nice paved road around the park etc. A good option for any outdoor activities..

locations.title: Kivila Park
locations.description: Located next to the concrete jungles of Lasnamäe, this park offers a bit of greenery to the area. The park features a skatepark, a workout ground, playgrounds, dog parks and more.

locations.title: The End of Lasnamäe
locations.description: A bridge that roughly symbolizes the border of the city. Some street art and the river make it a nice stop if going around on foot or riding a bike. There are some areas nearby that are good for swimming in the river as well.

locations.title: Iru Hill
locations.description: Off the course of any regular trail but through a weed filled trail, you can reach the high-point of the hill which provides a view and at the foot of the hill there are a few spots to access the river as well.

locations.title: Pirita Suspension Bridge
locations.description: This is a suspension bridge for pedestrians to cross the Pirita River. The bridge is a valuable landmark since there are only a very limited number of crossings over the Pirita River. The bridge also provides a good view over the river and surrounding area.

locations.title: Laagri Former Military Base
locations.description: The entrance to the Soviet time abandoned military base. There are multiple bunker like structures here and some of those are connected to each other by tunnels. There are a lot of ruins here which are not mapped but the main points of interests are marked. The tunnels are filled with water up to around 0,5m deep and the state in which these structures are in may vary.

locations.title: Laagri Military Bunker 1
locations.description: A concrete bunker from the Soviet era with multiple rooms inside and a partially submerged tunnel. The tunnel with the most water.

locations.title: Laagri Military Bunker 2
locations.description: A concrete bunker from the Soviet era with multiple rooms inside and a partially submerged tunnel.

locations.title: Laagri Military Bunker 3
locations.description: A concrete bunker from the Soviet era with multiple rooms inside and a partially submerged tunnel. The entrance to the tunnel is a narrow hole in the ground, not recommended to enter the tunnel from here if you're planning on visiting the underground.

locations.title: Laagri Military Bunker 4
locations.description: A concrete bunker from the Soviet era with multiple rooms inside and a partially submerged tunnel. The outside area has a lot of trash around and the entrance is not very convinient.

locations.title: Viru Gate
locations.description: A part of the city wall that was constructed in the 1300s that has remained to this day, it now symbolizes the main entrance to the Old Town on the Viru street.

locations.title: Town Hall Square
locations.description: Raekoja plats is a town square located in Tallinn's Old Town in Estonia that dates back to the 14th century. Its purpose was to serve as a gathering place for citizens and merchants, as well as a venue for markets and public events. Today, Raekoja plats remains a vibrant public space, serving as a popular destination for tourists and locals alike, and hosting cultural events and festivals throughout the year.

locations.title: Freedom Square
locations.description: Vabaduse väljak, also known as Freedom Square, is a public square located in the center of Tallinn. It was originally built in the 19th century and was renamed after Estonia regained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991. Today, the square is a popular destination for tourists and locals, featuring notable landmarks and serving as an important symbol of Estonia's struggle for independence and commitment to democracy.

locations.title: Lindamäe Park
locations.description: Lindamäe Park is a public park located in Tallinn, Estonia, and was built in the 1970s. The park is characterized by its vast green spaces and winding paths, with several benches and picnic areas for visitors to enjoy.

locations.title: Kohtuotsa Viewing Platform
locations.description: Kohtuotsa Viewing Platform is a popular tourist attraction located on the northern side of Toompea Hill in Tallinn. From the platform, visitors can enjoy stunning panoramic views of the Old Town's red roofs, impressive churches, modern high-rises in the city center, and the Gulf of Finland, harbor, and Pirita in the distance.

locations.title: Patkuli Viewing Platform
locations.description: Patkuli Viewing Platform is a popular tourist attraction located in Tallinn. The platform was built in the 19th century and offers visitors breathtaking panoramic views of the Old Town's medieval buildings, towering spires, and the sea beyond.

locations.title: Piiskopi Viewing Platform
locations.description: Piiskopi Viewing Platform is a hidden gem located on the western side of Toompea Hill in Tallinn, Estonia. The small garden-like platform offers beautiful views of the Pelgulinn and Kalamaja neighborhoods, and visitors can also admire the medieval well and apple trees that were planted during the renovation of the garden.

locations.title: Toompark
locations.description: Toompark is the largest park in the Tallinn center and features the Snelli Pond as its central feature. The park is a landscape garden with walking paths, greenery, and a diverse selection of trees. Visitors can also see the Patkuli staircase, which connects Toompea with the city below and was completed in 1903.

locations.title: Rannamägi
locations.description: Rannamägi is a green area located next to the Old Town of Tallinn, between Rannamäe tee and Põhja puiestee. It was created from the Skoone bastion, which was built in the 17th century as part of Tallinn's defensive fortifications. The bastion was one of the 11 planned, but due to a lack of resources, only three were completed by 1710. After the Crimean War, the bastion was turned into a park, and in 1885, it was officially designed as a green area.

locations.title: Harku Forest Hut
locations.description: A small wooden hut in the middle of the large Harku forest. The location is a perfect place to have a break and enjoy the surrounding forest and the hill filled landscape.

locations.title: Kakumäe Beach
locations.description: Kakumäe rand is a beautiful sandy beach located in the Kakumäe district of Tallinn. It is a popular destination for swimming, sunbathing, and water sports, with clear water and stunning views of the Gulf of Finland. The beach is also surrounded by a scenic forest and has several amenities such as a playground and picnic areas.

locations.title: Tabasalu Cliff
locations.description: Tabasalu pank is a stunning cliff formation located on the northern coast of Estonia, overlooking the Gulf of Finland. It is characterized by its towering limestone cliffs and panoramic views of the sea, making it a popular destination for hiking and sightseeing.

locations.title: Suurupi Coastal Defence
locations.description: This is a former Coastal Defence Battery, that was built in 1916 initially, and has since then received some repairs and improvements. The defence unit consists of 4 concrete artillery stands that are connected through tunnels. The location is easy to access.

locations.title: Suurupi Statue of Freedom
locations.description: This is a statue of a woman, constructed in 2017. Located next to the sea, the statue should resemble the Estonians' thrive for freedom and their relations with the sea.

locations.title: Suurupi's Sea Cave
locations.description: An interesting cave carved by the sea into a sandy outcrop. The surrounding area is also very beautiful.

locations.title: Suurupi Siren Station
locations.description: Built in 1898 as a complex of sirens and service buildings, and currently, only the siren building, residential house, fuel shed, sauna, and a leaning mast remain. The complex was established to warn ships of dangerous reefs and shallow waters during foggy weather. However, the historical complex is now threatened by erosion caused by the stormy sea.

locations.title: Suurupi Comm. Station 1
locations.description: There used to be a military communications station here which consisted of several buildings. The station included missile stands, barracks, garages, the communications center and some more minor structures. Due to the distance between the buildings, the area is split into three sections.

locations.title: Suurupi Comm. Station 2
locations.description: There used to be a military communications station here which consisted of several buildings. The station included missile stands, barracks, garages, the communications center and some more minor structures. Due to the distance between the buildings, the area is split into three sections.

locations.title: Suurupi Comm. Station 3
locations.description: There used to be a military communications station here which consisted of several buildings. The station included missile stands, barracks, garages, the communications center and some more minor structures. Due to the distance between the buildings, the area is split into three sections.

locations.title: Vääna-Jõesuu Beach
locations.description: This is a well known sandy beach that is about 3 kilometers long and is surrounded by pine forests. Vääna-Jõesuu beach is a popular destination for swimming, sunbathing, and various water activities such as windsurfing, kitesurfing, and surfing.

locations.title: Vääna-Jõesuu bridge
locations.description: This is a bridge on the Vääna-Jõesuu beach that crosses the Vääna River. It is a suspension bridge made for pedestrians and it provides a nice view over the surrounding area as well.

locations.title: Türisalu Cliff
locations.description: This is a picturesque cliff located on the coast of the Baltic Sea in northern Estonia, about 30 kilometers west of Tallinn. The cliff is about 30 meters high and stretches for several kilometers along the coastline. It offers stunning panoramic views of the sea and the surrounding landscape.

locations.title: Türisalu Missile Base
locations.description: This used to be a missile base that was built around the 1950s and it used to be one of the biggest abandoned military bases in Estonia by the size of the landarea. It is recommended to take several hours to check out the area since buildings are scattered all around the base and many are hidden by the overgrown nature.

locations.title: Naage Cave
locations.description: This is a small naturally formed cavern in the Naage cliff. It is a cool little cave and do not disturb the bats if there are any inside.

locations.title: Keila Waterfall
locations.description: Keila Waterfall is a beautiful waterfall located in the town of Keila in northern Estonia, about 25 kilometers southwest of Tallinn. The waterfall is about 6 meters high and 60 meters wide, and it is formed by the Keila River cascading over a limestone cliff. Keila Juga is a popular tourist attraction and a great spot for nature lovers, hikers, and photographers. There is a walking trail along the river, which provides excellent views of the waterfall and the surrounding area.

locations.title: Laulasmaa Beach
locations.description: The beach is situated on the coast of the Gulf of Finland, and it is known for its long sandy shore and shallow waters, making it an ideal spot for swimming, sunbathing, and other recreational activities.  Laulasmaa beach is a great place to enjoy the scenic beauty of the Estonian coastline and to relax in a peaceful and natural environment.

locations.title: Nabe Island
locations.description: A small landmass resembling an island that you can walk onto from land. A bit hard to access since the trail is not the clearest.

locations.title: Treppoja Cascade
locations.description: The cascade consists of a hundred-meter-long area with a width of 2-15 meters and up to 1.5 meters high slopes. Due to its youth and relatively small water flow, the waterfall has not been able to shape a canyon.

locations.title: Käesalu Manor Ruins
locations.description: Käesalu Manor was founded in the mid-17th century, and has had several owners throughout its history. The manor's current main building is a two-story baroque structure with a triangular pediment in the center of the facade, and was likely built during the period when the von Gernet family owned the property. Today, the manor is privately owned and several outbuildings have been preserved.

locations.title: Klooga Manor Ruins
locations.description: The manor is situated on the eastern shore of Lake Klooga. The manor was first mentioned in 1504 and belonged to various families until it was acquired by Hans von Klugen in 1665. The manor house was completed in 1794, and today only ruins remain. After World War II, the Soviet Army occupied the manor complex until their departure in 1992.

locations.title: Klooga Military Campus
locations.description: The military base was used during the Soviet times as an ammo storage. There are buildings in various conditions, including hangars and multiple barracks. A lot of it is overgrown by nature but the area provides some interesting sights to see.

locations.title: Klooga Lake
locations.description: The lake is known for its recreational opportunities and natural beauty, and it is also the site of the former Klooga Manor.

locations.title: Leetse Missile Base
locations.description: There used to be a missile base here from the Soviet period that now is mostly in ruins and a lot of the buildings have been torn down. There are a few structures remaining that could still be of interest.

locations.title: Lahepere Cliff
locations.description: The Lahepere cliff is approximately 1.5 km long and 10-15 meters high, and includes several waterfalls. It provides a scenic view of the area.

locations.title: Leetse Camping Area 1
locations.description: Leetse camping area is a campsite located on the Pakri Peninsula in Estonia. It is situated in a beautiful pine forest along the Lahepere Bay and has five dispersed fireplaces that offer privacy. The camping area includes two open fireplaces, three covered fireplaces, picnic tables with benches, shelters for firewood, trash cans, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Leetse Camping Area 2
locations.description: Leetse camping area is a campsite located on the Pakri Peninsula in Estonia. It is situated in a beautiful pine forest along the Lahepere Bay and has five dispersed fireplaces that offer privacy. The camping area includes two open fireplaces, three covered fireplaces, picnic tables with benches, shelters for firewood, trash cans, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Leetse Camping Area 3
locations.description: Leetse camping area is a campsite located on the Pakri Peninsula in Estonia. It is situated in a beautiful pine forest along the Lahepere Bay and has five dispersed fireplaces that offer privacy. The camping area includes two open fireplaces, three covered fireplaces, picnic tables with benches, shelters for firewood, trash cans, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Pakri Lighthouse
locations.description: Pakri lighthouse is a lighthouse was built in 1889 and is 52 meters high, making it the tallest in Estonia. It is located on a rocky cliff and provides a panoramic view of the surrounding area. The lighthouse was fully automated in 1995 and is now operated remotely.

locations.title: Pakri Cliff
locations.description: Pakri Cliff formation located on the Pakri Peninsula. The bank is approximately 6 km long and up to 22 meters high, and consists of alternating layers of limestone and shale. The view from the cliff is amazing.

locations.title: Rummu Quarry
locations.description: Rummu quarry is a former limestone quarry with water that has a very bright blue color to it. The quarry was operational from the 1930s until the 1990s and was used to provide building materials for the Soviet Union. After the quarry was abandoned, it filled with water and became a popular spot for diving and swimming.

locations.title: Aila Backup Control Point
locations.description: This used to be a backup control point during the Soviet occupation. The area has the main command center building along with some minor structures in the vicinity. A rather small area but worth a check if in the area.

locations.title: Water Stonehenge
locations.description: The place does not have a name but it is basically a location with massive concrete blocks that form a sort of walkway onto the sea.

locations.title: Ülgase Valley
locations.description: The area of Ülgase has valleys with water in them and the unusual landscape provides interesting viewpoints. Some of the hills surrounding the valleys have trails on them.

locations.title: Jägala Suspension Bridge
locations.description: A wobbly foot-bridge to cross the river Jägala river. The bridge provides an excellent view of the surrounding area.

locations.title: Linnamäe Hydropower Plant
locations.description: A hydroelectric powerplant with the option to cross the river from on top of the structure. The crossing provides a good view of the flowing water and the nature around. This is an active facility.

locations.title: Jägala Waterfall
locations.description: Jägala waterfall is a natural wonder located on the Jägala River in northern Estonia. It is the largest waterfall in Estonia, with a height of approximately 8 meters and a width of over 50 meters. The waterfall is particularly impressive during the spring snowmelt or after heavy rains when the water flow is at its highest. The surrounding area is also popular for hiking, with several trails leading to and around the waterfall.

locations.title: Jõelähtme Waterfall
locations.description: Close to the Jägala Waterfall, this is a significantly smaller waterfall but it is a nice addition to check out when in the area.

locations.title: Maardu Limestone Quarry
locations.description: A vast quarry which is still active but this particular point has nothing going on and is accessible by anyone. It is not recommended to swim here but it does offer a nice view over the quarry.

locations.title: Majakivi
locations.description: Majakivi is a large erratic boulder measuring 15.1m in length, 11.0m in width, and 7.0m in height, making it the third largest boulder in Estonia. Majakivi has vertical cracks and is located in a marshy forest. It has been a protected natural monument since 1963 and is situated within the Lahemaa National Park. The Majakivi-Pikanõmme educational trail passes by the boulder.

locations.title: Pikanõmme Viewing Tower
locations.description: The Pikanõmme Viewing Tower is a 23m wooden structure built on top of the Pikanõmme hill, which rises 33m above sea level. The tower provides a great view over the nearby bog, which begins at the foot of the hill, as well as a view of the sea over the surrounding forest.

locations.title: Juminda Missile Base
locations.description: This is a former Soviet missile base located in the silent forests of Juminda. Many of the structures have declined into ruins or have been torn down but the area still provides some buildings to explore.

locations.title: Juminda Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is located on the western tip of the nature filled Lahemaa Peninsula. The area has two covered fire pits with grills, picnic tables, a woodshed, dry toilet, and trash containers.

locations.title: Tsitre Camping Ground
locations.description: Tsitre camping area is located in Lahemaa National Park and is a historic place with remnants of an old manor complex. The nearby area features a 1 km trail through the historic Tsitre manor park.. The camping area includes open and covered fire pits, observation tower, dry toilet, and waste bins. The area is easily accessible by car as well.

locations.title: Tsitre Viewing Platform
locations.description: Located in Lahemaa National Park the lookout tower offers stunning views of the Kolga Bay and the surrounding area. Visitors can see various species of seabirds, including gulls and cormorants, as well as migrating waterfowl during the spring and autumn. The nearby area features a 1 km trail through the historic Tsitre manor park.

locations.title: Muuksi Hill
locations.description: Muuksi Hill used to have an ancient hillfort that dates back to the Iron Age and is believed to have been built between the 7th and 9th centuries AD. Currently there is almost nothing of the fort remaining but the elevated ground provides a nice view around the area and there is a walkway that goes along the ledge of the hill.

locations.title: Turjekeldri Stream Cave
locations.description: Turjekeldri Stream Cave is situated beneath the Turjekeldri waterfall and has been naturally carved out by a spring. Access to the cave can be difficult as the main road leading to it is private and inaccessible. Visitors may need to approach the location from the northern side.

locations.title: Hara Military Base Ruins
locations.description: This used to be a military base that was most likely a part of the nearby Hara Submarine Base. Currently only ruins remain of the base, but if in the area to explore the Hara Submarine Base, then this location could be worth a check.

locations.title: Pärispea Urbex Village
locations.description: The history of this area's past use is unclear, but what remains today are over 10 empty buildings that make it an intriguing destination for urban explorers.

locations.title: Pärispea Research Institute
locations.description: This is a big well-preserved building that was established in 1953 and was used for research on ship defense against mines and torpedoes as well as research on ship concealment and protection. The location has a lot to see and is one of the more interesting places to check out.

locations.title: Purekkari Camping Ground
locations.description: This camping area is located on the northernmost tip of the Pärispea Peninsula in Estonia, surrounded by nature. The road leading to the tip of the peninsula is narrow and bumpy, making it unsuitable for large buses. The camping area includes open and covered fireplaces with grills, picnic tables under three shelters, a woodshed, a dry toilet, and trash bins.

locations.title: Purekkari Cape
locations.description: This is the northernmost point on the mainland of Estonia. The 1.5 km long rocky and boulder-rich peninsula ends with a small island, only accessible when the water is low and even though the area consists mostly of rocks and boulders, some trees and shrubs grow here as well. A one of a kind location which provides a great view of the surrounding scenery.

locations.title: Purekkari Urbex Camp
locations.description: Multiple camping shacks that have had their doors nailed shut, worth a visit for urban explorers.

locations.title: Purekkari Urbex House
locations.description: A random abandoned apartment building in the middle of the forest, not much to see but perhaps worth a check if passing by.

locations.title: Pärispea Radar Station
locations.description: The former Soviet radar station has a lot of buildings and half in ruins structures scattered around this estimated middle point. Some of the structures are a bit tricky to find, though it is an interesting area to check out.

locations.title: Vasaristi Waterfall
locations.description: Vasaristi waterfall is a beautiful cascading waterfall that has a considerable amount of water flowing through during any wetter season and is an excellent spot for photography or enjoying the view. It consists of three cascades with heights of 0.5m, 1.5m, and 1.6m in the direction of the flowing water, and is fed by Vasaristi stream.

locations.title: Nõmmeveski Canyon
locations.description: he canyon is a beautiful location with a river and a 16-17 meter wide waterfall that forms a 470-meter-long canyon. The waterfall is only 1.2 meters high but still provides stunning views, and the canyon adds a touch of drama and primordiality to the area. Additionally, there are remains of an old hydroelectric power station in the canyon that once supplied electricity to a nearby paper mill.

locations.title: Nõmmeveski Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the right bank of Valgejõgi in Lahemaa National Park. The site is suitable for hikers passing through RMK Oandu-Aegviidu-Ikla hiking trail, and it offers four covered fireplaces with grill grates, two shelters with tables and benches, two benches, two campfire sites, a woodshed, a shelter for firewood, dry toilets, and garbage cans.

locations.title: Joaveski Bridge
locations.description: The Suspension Bridge crosses over the Loobu River with a cascading waterfall at the same location. The Nõmmeveski Hydropower plant is nearby.

locations.title: Nõmmesveski Hydropower Plant
locations.description: Hydroelectric power plant was built in 1927 to meet the energy needs of the nearby Joaveski pulp mill. However, a fire destroyed the plant in 1964, and it has since become a historical landmark for visitors. The building has collapsed areas and entering it can be dangerous, requiring extreme caution.

locations.title: Kupu Missile Base
locations.description: This is a former missile base and the primary purpose of the base was to protect the airspace of Tallinn. The base now lies in ruins for the most part but it has a lot of small ruins around and a few half-intact structures as well. Worth a check.

locations.title: Saarte Bog Camping Ground
locations.description: This campground is a secluded and isolated location in the middle of deep forests near Pirita River. The road leading to the location is not accessible by car. The site offers a covered fireplace, a table, benches, a basic forest hut for shelter, a dry toilet, and an information board.

locations.title: Saula Blue Springs
locations.description: These blue springs are a group of three large springs located in Saula village. The total flow rate is 20-30 liters per second and the different colors of the springs are caused by the color of the underlying soil and the reflection of light from sand particles in the clear water. The springs are considered an archaeological monument and have been a site for offering sacrifices for thousands of years due to their supposed healing properties.

locations.title: Naissaare Lighthouse
locations.description: The lighthouse is located at the northern tip of the Naissaar Island. It is a red and white concrete tower with a height of 45 meters from the base and a light height of 48 meters above sea level. The first wooden lighthouses were built in 1788, and a stone lighthouse was built in 1805. The current tower was completed in 1960 and has been officially open to visitors since June 2019.

locations.title: Hara Submarine Base
locations.description: The Hara Submarine Base in Estonia was a unique military facility that served as a research and testing site for ship demagnetization to protect them from magnetic mines during World War II. The complex included a technical center in Hara harbor, laboratories, hydroacoustic and hydrodynamic testing centers, and a control ship for physical fields. Once an obscure place, it is now a popular destination for curious tourists, and although previously admission-free, there may now be a fee to enter.

locations.title: Leetse Ammo Storages
locations.description: There are a few intact ammo storage structures still standing around this point but most of these have fallen into disrepair. The buildings that do remain are located in the woods between the road and the coastline.

locations.title: Viimsi Upper Lighthouse
locations.description: A lighthouse that has been built on a hill called Lubja.The height of the red iron tower is 13 m and the elevation of the light is 59 m. The first wooden construction was built in 1914, and the current version was built in 1939.  The hill offers a good view over the surrounding area.

locations.title: Põhjakonna Stairs
locations.description: An illuminated stairway built on the limestone outcrop, which runs in a zigzag pattern up a steep hill, the other side follows the natural slope of the hill. During the Soviet era in the 1970s, the limestone outcrop was cordoned off with barbed wire because the authorities kept fuel tanks there.

locations.title: Pandju Islet
locations.description: A small island that has a narrow strip of land to get there from the mainland. The area of the island in 2012 was 0,87 hectares but it is constantly sinking. There are many sea birds around the island, who find this an attractive spot for nesting, because of that it is not permitted to go to the island from 14th of April to 31st of July. The name Pandju comes from Finnish and means the tools the Finns used for fishing or hunting birds.

locations.title: Rohuneeme Missile Base
locations.description: The missile base was a Soviet military installation on a 48-hectare area on the Viimsi peninsula, storing S-75 and S-300 missiles, 100mm anti-aircraft guns, and nuclear warheads in the 1970s. There are plenty of hangars and other similar installations to be found. The area is split into two locations, since some buildings are near the shore and some are a bit deeper in the forest.

locations.title: Rohuneeme Missile Base
locations.description: The missile base was a Soviet military installation on a 48-hectare area on the Viimsi peninsula, storing S-75 and S-300 missiles, 100mm anti-aircraft guns, and nuclear warheads in the 1970s. There are plenty of hangars and other similar installations to be found. The area is split into two locations, since some buildings are near the shore and some are a bit deeper in the forest.

locations.title: Sihiotsaalune Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the close to the beautiful southern shore of the Aegna Island, this is the only camping ground on the island. The campground offers fireplaces, sitting areas with tables and trash bins.

locations.title: Tädu Campfire Site
locations.description: Located in a pine forest the campfire site provides a fireplace, a firewood shelter, seating and an outhouse. It is at the end of the 3.5 km Tädu nature study trail.

locations.title: Saunakari Camping Ground
locations.description: Saunakari is a small and secluded official campfire site located near the northern tip of Naissaare. It features a covered seating area with tables and benches, a fire pit, a toilet, and a trash bin.

locations.title: Neeme Lighthouse
locations.description: This is a small lighthouse located on the tip of the Neeme peninsula. Just a bit onward from the lighthouse, the tip of the peninsula continues onto the sea providing a good view of the surrounding coastline.

locations.title: Kaberneeme Beach
locations.description: This is a beautiful sandy beach surrounded by woods. The water is usually warm during summer, because it is quite shallow.

locations.title: Kaberneeme Peninsula
locations.description: Being only 2 kilometers long, it is one of the tiniest on the North Coast of Estonia. On the west coast lies a sandy beach and on the east coast the Kaberneeme yachting harbour. The village was first mentioned in 1375. You can find a minor abandoned building at the very top of the peninsula.

locations.title: Kaberneeme Camping Ground
locations.description: The campground is located in the woods next to the Ristisoo Lake close to the Kaberneeme village. Featuring eight outdoor fireplaces, nine picnic tables with a shelter, two woodsheds and two dry toilets.

locations.title: Aegviidu
locations.description: Aegviidu is known as the hiking capital of Estonia and it's rich and diverse nature features vast forests, bogs, and rivers, making it perfect for outdoor activities such as hiking, skiing, cycling, camping, birdwatching or swimming in one of the forest lakes.

locations.title: Ännijärv
locations.description: This is a beautiful lake in the middle of the thick forests of Kõrvemaa. It is a great spot to take a break while hiking, to catch fish or to have a swim.

locations.title: Jussi Camping Ground
locations.description:
Jussi camping area is a small and private camping area located on the shore of Jussi
Väinjärv lake in the Kõrvemaa nature reserve. The camping area includes two separate
fireplaces, picnic tables with benches, dry toilets, firewood shelter, and a garbage bin. The
camping area is situated along the Jussi nature trail and the RMK hiking trail.

locations.title: Paukjärve Camping Ground
locations.description:
Paukjärve telkimisala is a spacious camping area located on the picturesque shore of the
Lake Paukjärv. The camping area features three separate fire pits, covered seating areas,
dry toilets, and rubbish bins. The campground is situated in a nature filled area and features
a tall lookout tower atop a nearby hill, providing breathtaking views of the lake and the bog
on the other side.

locations.title: Paukjärve Viewing Tower
locations.description: The Paukjärve observation tower is located on the Paukjärve esker. The 11-meter-high wooden observation tower offers a magnificent view of the North Kõrvemaa Nature Reserve. On one side, you can see Kõnnu Suursoo, and on the other side, there's Paukjärv.

locations.title: Kõnnu Suursoo Viewing Platform
locations.description:
The few storey lookout tower stands in the tranquil Kõnnu Suursoo bog, with a wooden
walkway leading through the bog and passing by the tower. This scenic spot is perfect for
enjoying the beauty of the surrounding nature.

locations.title: Järvi Pärnjärve Camping Ground
locations.description:
Järvi Pärnjärve campground is a fairly large area located next to Lake Pärnjärv, easily
accessible by car and suitable for events. It is situated along the Liiapeksi-Aegviidu hiking
trail and the Oandu-Aegviidu-Ikla hiking trail, and offers 4 open fireplaces with grill racks, 2
covered areas with tables and benches, 2 dry toilets, and 2 shelters for firewood. The nearby
Kõnnu Suursoo bog is also a great attraction.

locations.title: Linajärv
locations.description:
Lake Linajärv is a tranquil body of water located in the Aegviidu area, with a wooden dock
that provides a peaceful spot to enjoy the surroundings. The lake is situated near a hiking
trail, providing easy access to visitors. There are a lot of lakes called “Linajärv” in Estonia.

locations.title: Venemäe Camping Ground
locations.description:
The camping area is located in the dense forests of North Kõrvemaa and is suitable for
organizing larger events. The camping area features an open fireplace with a grill, a picnic
table with benches, a sheltered seating area, a dry toilet, and a waste bin. Additionally, the
multi-story lookout tower offers a magnificent view of the surrounding forest.

locations.title: Kõrvemaa Military Base
locations.description:
The Kõrvemaa military base used to be the largest training area in Estonia for Soviet forces,
but now it mainly consists of ruins and forest trails. While it is occasionally still used for
military training by Estonian forces, there may be restrictions in place during such times.

locations.title: Nelijärve
locations.description:
Nelijärve is a popular destination for tourists and locals alike due to its four adjacent lakes,
which provide various opportunities for recreation. The location marked on the map is
situated next to Purgatsi Lake, where visitors can rent boats for lake excursions or skis
during the winter months. The area is surrounded by a hilly landscape, and the Nelijärve
Holiday Center is located nearby, offering accommodation and a bar/cafe.

locations.title: Noku Camping Ground
locations.description:
Noku camping ground is located next to one of the most beautiful and well-known bogs in
Estonia - Kakerdaja bog. It is situated on the Kakerdaja nature trail and is named after the
former Nokko farm. There are two separate fireplaces with grill grates, shelters, picnic
tables, dry toilets, firewood storage, and waste bins.

locations.title: Kalajärve Camping Ground
locations.description:
The campground is located in a deep pine forest near the Kakerdaja study trail and next to
Lake Kalajärve. It features an open fireplace with a grill, a shelter with tables and benches,
two additional benches, a shelter for firewood, a dry toilet, and two garbage cans.

locations.title: Hiiesveski Camping Ground
locations.description:
The camping area is situated by the Paunküla reservoir and includes 3 sets of covered
tables and benches, 3 open fireplaces with grill racks, 2 dry toilets, a shelter for firewood,
and 3 trash cans. Very easy access with a car.

locations.title: Paunküla Camping Ground
locations.description:
Paunküla Camping Ground is located at the intersection of Paunküla hills hiking trails by the shore
of Suur-Kaksjärve lake. The site features an open fireplace with a grill, a shelter with table
and benches, a separate table, three benches, dry toilet, and a shelter for firewood.

locations.title: Naissaar
locations.description: Naissaar is a small island located off the coast of Estonia in the Gulf of Finland. The island has a rich history, having been inhabited by various civilizations over the centuries. It is a popular tourist destination, offering scenic hiking trails, historic landmarks, and picturesque beaches. Visitors can explore the island's diverse flora and fauna, including rare bird species, or its military history and abandoned Soviet-era structures.

locations.title: Viru Bog
locations.description:
Viru Bog is one of the best bogs in Estonia, located in Lahemaa with a landscape of forests
and bogs. The bog formed from a lake that was filled with sediment, and over time, the bog
expanded as the lake continued to fill. The name "Viru" comes from its location on the
eastern border of Harjumaa and near Virumaa. There is a boardwalk through the bog, with a
viewing tower in the center. The bog is estimated to be around 3,000 years old.

locations.title: Laitse Radio Station
locations.description:
The Laitse Radio Center Technical Building No. 1 is an abandoned building that was built in
1950, and it served as the main transmission complex for Estonian Radio's Central Wave
Station during the Cold War. The building was designed by the State Soviet Construction
and Assembly Trust No. 15 in Moscow, and is a well-preserved example of Stalinist
neoclassicism.

locations.title: Suurekivi Missile Base
locations.description:
Suurekivi Missile Base was a former missile base primarily used for air defense that now
mostly lies in ruins, with some remaining structures to explore. The name of the base is likely
derived from the nearby Suurkivi boulder, meaning "big rock" in Estonian.

locations.title: Tuhala Nõiakaev
locations.description:
Tuhala Witch's Well is a unique natural attraction, which periodically overflows with water,
creating an impressive natural phenomenon. The well has become a popular tourist
destination and is often associated with Estonian folklore and legends. It is recommended to
check the activity of the well before going, since the well can also go for years without
overflowing.

locations.title: Iru Military Ruins
locations.description: This location is a set of five bunkers located on a limestone cliff over the Pirita River. These bunkers are part of the Peeter Suure Merekindluse III coastal defence sector and were used during wartime. The bunkers are partially dug into the ground and are an interesting historical sight in the Pirita River valley nature reserve.

locations.title: Vääna-Jõesuu Nature Trails
locations.description: Vääna-Jõesuu is a settlement surrounded by pine forests, and this forested area cut off by the Vääna River provides many trails to enjoy. It's an excellent location for those seeking fresh air and nature walks.

locations.title: Vääna-Viti Bunker 1
locations.description: The Vääna-Viti Bunker is a fortification built between 1913-1917 for defense purposes. It is a part of a larger complex and features an underground bunker with thick concrete walls and ceilings and a narrow underground passage that connects with the other Vääna-Viti Bunker on the other side of the tunnel. The tunnels are usually moist and muddy, requiring waterproof footwear to traverse through. A flashlight is required.

locations.title: Vääna-Viti Bunker 2
locations.description: The Vääna-Viti Bunker is a fortification built between 1913-1917 for defense purposes. It is a part of a larger complex and features an underground bunker with thick concrete walls and ceilings and a narrow underground passage that connects with the other Vääna-Viti Bunker on the other side of the tunnel. The tunnels are usually moist and muddy, requiring waterproof footwear to traverse through. A flashlight is required.

locations.title: Muraste Beach
locations.description:
This is a sandy beach located near the settlement of Muraste. The beach is known for its
calm and peaceful atmosphere, making it a great place for swimming and sunbathing during
the summer months.

locations.title: Mustjõe Lookout Tower
locations.description:
The 9m high observation tower is located on a hillside along the Stroomi-Rocca al Mare
cycling path, with an absolute height of about 13m. Located in the Rocca al Mare area in
Tallinn, which is an important stopping and feeding area for protected birds, the lookout
tower provides a good viewing point for these birds.

locations.title: Keibu Camping Ground 1
locations.description: Keibu lõkkekoht I is a camping and resting place located on a beautiful sandy beach by Keibu Bay. It offers good swimming opportunities and amenities such as a dry toilet, two covered fireplaces, a covered bench-table, a table with benches, a woodshed, two trash cans, and two boardwalks to the sea.

locations.title: Keibu Camping Ground 2
locations.description: Keibu lõkkekoht II is a camping and resting place on a beautiful singing sand beach by Keibu Bay with good swimming opportunities. It offers one dry toilet, one covered fireplace, one roofed bench-table, a shelter for firewood, and one garbage bin.

locations.title: Vahiküla Joastik
locations.description: Vahiküla Joastik is a waterfall in Estonia that is fed by the Vääna River. It consists of three cascades, with the last one being 1.5 meters high. The waterfall is located about 100 meters from the nearby bridge on the main road, and the best way to reach it is by following the riverbank. Due to the Vääna River's average flow rate, the waterfall does not suffer from water shortage.

locations.title: Humala Command Center
locations.description: The Humala Command Center was a Soviet-era military base that includes hangars, radar hills, garages, a command center building, and tunnels dating back to World War I. The command center was built on top of the tunnel system. While there is much to see at this location, it is worth noting that the tunnel system is currently filled with water to knee-height.

locations.title: Humala Military Bunker 1
locations.description: The Humala Military Bunker is a partially underground concrete structure located near the Humala Command Center, which dates back to 1916. It is connected by a tunnel to another bunker located a short distance away. The tunnels are typically partially filled with water.

locations.title: Humala Military Bunker 2
locations.description: The Humala Military Bunker is a partially underground concrete structure located near the Humala Command Center, which dates back to 1916. It is connected by a tunnel to another bunker located a short distance away. The tunnels are typically partially filled with water.

locations.title: Järvi Pikkjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: The campground is located in the Põhja-Kõrvemaa Nature Reserve in Estonia. The area is surrounded by pine forests and there is a beautiful lake nearby. The camping area has fire pits, dry toilets, and a shelter for cooking. Visitors can enjoy hiking and cycling in the nearby trails, as well as fishing and swimming in the lake.

locations.title: Aegna Island
locations.description: Aegna is an island located in the Gulf of Finland, about 14 km south of Tallinn, Estonia. The island is known for its natural beauty, diverse wildlife, and historic military structures. Visitors can enjoy hiking trails, beaches, and camping on the island. The island was used as a military training area during Soviet times, and remnants of military installations can still be seen. Today, Aegna is a protected nature reserve, and visitors are asked to respect the environment and take care not to disturb the local wildlife.

locations.title: Aegna Artillery Battery
locations.description: The artillery battery is a remnant of Peter the Great's Naval Fortress on Aegna Island. It was built in 1915-1917 and was named Aleksander Nevski Battery. The 200-meter-long structure had two 12-inch guns in each tower. The battery rooms were located on two floors, with a deeper connection corridor. The battery was blown up in February 1918 and was later rebuilt but was again destroyed in August 1941.

locations.title: Põhjarand
locations.description: Suur Põhjarand is a wide sandy beach open to north winds, bordered to the south by pine forests. The beach can be accessed directly from the harbor along Tagama road, mostly surrounded by spruce forests. The distance from the harbor to the beach is about 1.7 km. From 2023 onwards, camping is no longer allowed here.

locations.title: Aegna Command Center
locations.description: This command center served as the central command point for Tallinn's coastal defense and was a massive iron and concrete structure. It was completed in 1927 on Eerikneeme and consisted of a two-story concrete bunker surrounded by a high wall made of field stones. The center's ruins still exist today, including the low cylindrical observation tower and the above-ground circular iron structure. The underground passages have partially collapsed.

locations.title: Lemmikneeme Kivikülv
locations.description: Lemmikneeme Kivikülv is a boulder field located on the island of Aegna. It consists of 23 larger and 10 smaller boulders, with the largest being Tulekivi, Kultusekivi, Nurgaauguga kivi, Teravaservaline kivi, and Mälestuskivi.

locations.title: Lemmiku Nina
locations.description: Lemmiku Nina is a peninsula at the edge of Aegna Island offers a peaceful coastline and plain area with a few trees, making it a great spot for a picnic or to simply enjoy the surroundings.

locations.title: Hülkari Resting Area
locations.description: The resting area is located on the southeast tip of Naissaar island and it is close to the border guard radar and offers a magnificent view of Tallinn Bay and the city of Tallinn. It is surrounded by wide and clean sand dunes, and the beach is suitable for swimming. The area has one large covered table with benches, one covered fireplace, a trash bin, and a dry toilet. However, camping is not allowed in the area.

locations.title: Astangu Tunnel 1 e1
locations.description: Astangu Ammo Storages are a complex of underground tunnels in Tallinn, that were built as a central depot for ammunition and supplies for the navy during World War I. The tunnels were excavated into the limestone bedrock and had a U-shaped design and the tunnels were connected by rail tracks. This tunnel was close to finished back in the day so it is easy to go through it.

locations.title: Astangu Tunnel 1 e2
locations.description: Astangu Ammo Storages are a complex of underground tunnels in Tallinn, that were built as a central depot for ammunition and supplies for the navy during World War I. The tunnels were excavated into the limestone bedrock and had a U-shaped design and the tunnels were connected by rail tracks. This tunnel was close to finished back in the day so it is easy to go through it.

locations.title: Astangu Tunnel 3 e2
locations.description: Astangu Ammo Storages are a complex of underground tunnels in Tallinn, that were built as a central depot for ammunition and supplies for the navy during World War I. The tunnels were excavated into the limestone bedrock and had a U-shaped design and the tunnels were connected by rail tracks. This is in a good condition and you can enter the tunnel from the two open entrances and go thorugh it with ease.

locations.title: Astangu Tunnel 2
locations.description: Astangu Ammo Storages are a complex of underground tunnels in Tallinn, that were built as a central depot for ammunition and supplies for the navy during World War I. The tunnels were excavated into the limestone bedrock and had a U-shaped design and the tunnels were connected by rail tracks. Sometimes the tunnel is filled with a bit of water and the second entrance of this tunnel has collapsed.

locations.title: Astangu Tunnel 3 e1
locations.description: Astangu Ammo Storages are a complex of underground tunnels in Tallinn, that were built as a central depot for ammunition and supplies for the navy during World War I. The tunnels were excavated into the limestone bedrock and had a U-shaped design and the tunnels were connected by rail tracks. This is in a good condition and you can enter the tunnel from the two open entrances and go thorugh it with ease.

locations.title: Astangu Tunnel 5 e2
locations.description: Astangu Ammo Storages are a complex of underground tunnels in Tallinn, that were built as a central depot for ammunition and supplies for the navy during World War I. The tunnels were excavated into the limestone bedrock and had a U-shaped design and the tunnels were connected by rail tracks. This tunnel was never close to being finished so it is not recommended to go through it because it might be dangerous.

locations.title: Astangu Tunnel 4
locations.description: Astangu Ammo Storages are a complex of underground tunnels in Tallinn, that were built as a central depot for ammunition and supplies for the navy during World War I. The tunnels were excavated into the limestone bedrock and had a U-shaped design and the tunnels were connected by rail tracks. A huge hole in the ground which is just a part of an old tunnel that has collapsed. Entering this tunnel is not recommended and it is not certain if it is still possible to go through the tunnels as cave-ins might have occured since the cave was last checked. The cave also has a lot of clay within, so shoes and clothes might get dirty.

locations.title: Astangu Tunnel 5 e1
locations.description: Astangu Ammo Storages are a complex of underground tunnels in Tallinn, that were built as a central depot for ammunition and supplies for the navy during World War I. The tunnels were excavated into the limestone bedrock and had a U-shaped design and the tunnels were connected by rail tracks. This tunnel was never close to being finished so it is not recommended to go through it because it might be dangerous.

locations.title: Kalana Military Battery
locations.description: This is a 17m tall concrete tower that was built around the year 1940 and it was used by the military for co-ordinating units. The tower is in a relatively good state.

locations.title: Paradise Beach
locations.description: Paradise Beach is a beautiful sandy beach located on the western coast of the Estonian island of Hiiumaa. As its name suggests, it is a picturesque and idyllic location, with crystal-clear waters, soft white sand, and stunning views of the sea and surrounding forests.

locations.title: Kalana Radar Station
locations.description: This radar station was built in the 1960s as a part of the near by Kalana Military Campus. This structure is in a relative good state.

locations.title: Ristna Lighthouse
locations.description: Ristna Lighthouse is an historic tower on the western coast of the Estonian island of Hiiumaa. Built in 1874, it stands 29 meters tall and has become a popular tourist attraction in recent years, offering stunning views of the surrounding coastline and sea.

locations.title: Hirmuste Camping Ground
locations.description: Hirmuste Camping Ground is a popular camping site located on the western coast of the Estonian island of Hiiumaa. Surrounded by beautiful forests and offering easy access to the sea, the camping ground is a popular destination for nature lovers and outdoor enthusiasts.

locations.title: Kaleste Camping Ground
locations.description: A popular camping site on the northeastern coast of Hiiumaa island in Estonia. It offers access to beautiful forests, the sea, and a range of amenities, making it an ideal base for exploring the island's attractions.

locations.title: Kõpu Lighthouse
locations.description: Kõpu Lighthouse is a historic lighthouse located on the Estonian island of Hiiumaa in the Baltic Sea. Built in 1531, it is one of the oldest lighthouses in the world and has been in continuous use for over 450 years. The lighthouse tower stands 36 meters tall and has a unique, tapered shape that has become an iconic symbol of the island.

locations.title: Kõpu Boulder
locations.description: It is the largest boulder on the island, weighing around 75 tonnes and measuring 5 meters in height. The boulder is located near the village of Kõpu and is a popular tourist attraction. Visitors can climb to the top of the boulder for views of the surrounding countryside and nearby Kõpu Lighthouse.

locations.title: Kalurikoja Camping Hut
locations.description: Kalurikoja Metsaonn is a cozy cabin located in the forested area near the village of Kärdla on the Estonian island of Hiiumaa. It offers visitors a chance to experience the island's natural beauty and tranquility, with easy access to hiking trails, the sea, and nearby amenities. The cabin is equipped with basic facilities, including a wood-burning stove, beds, and cooking equipment, making it an ideal spot for camping, hiking, or simply enjoying the peaceful surroundings.

locations.title: Mägipä Camping Ground
locations.description: A scenic camping area on Hiiumaa's northern coast, surrounded by lush forests and with easy access to the sea. It offers basic facilities, including fire pits, picnic tables, and outdoor toilets, making it an ideal spot for outdoor activities and a chance to experience the island's natural beauty.

locations.title: Palli Camping Ground
locations.description: A peaceful camping area on the Estonian island of Hiiumaa, offering visitors easy access to the nearby beach and natural attractions. The camping ground provides basic facilities, including fire pits, picnic tables, and outdoor toilets, making it an ideal spot for camping, hiking, or simply enjoying the island's tranquil surroundings.

locations.title: Palli Lookout Tower
locations.description: A medium sized lookout tower next to the Palli Camping Ground. It offers a nice view of the surrounding sea and forest.

locations.title: Luidja Camping Ground
locations.description: A peaceful and scenic camping area located on the western coast of the Estonian island of Hiiumaa. It offers visitors easy access to the nearby Luidja Beach and natural attractions, as well as basic facilities including fire pits, picnic tables, and outdoor toilets.

locations.title: Luidja Beach
locations.description: Luidja Beach is a beautiful sandy beach located on the western coast of the Estonian island of Saaremaa. It is a popular spot for swimming, sunbathing, and enjoying the natural surroundings, with easy access to nearby hiking trails and birdwatching opportunities.

locations.title: Vanajõe Camping Ground
locations.description: Vanajõe Camping Ground is a cozy and scenic camping area located in the heart of a picturesque valley in central Estonia. It offers visitors basic facilities for outdoor cooking and dining, as well as easy access to nearby hiking trails and natural attraction

locations.title: Leemeti Camping Ground
locations.description: Leemeti Lõkkekoht is a scenic camping area located in the heart of the forest on the Estonian island of Hiiumaa. It offers visitors basic facilities for outdoor cooking and dining, as well as access to nearby hiking trails, birdwatching spots, and other natural attractions.

locations.title: Laasimetsa Camping Ground
locations.description: Laasimetsa Lõkkekoht is a beautiful and secluded camping area located in the heart of the forest on the Estonian island of Hiiumaa. It offers visitors basic facilities for outdoor cooking and dining, as well as access to nearby hiking trails, fishing spots, and other natural attractions. The camping ground is an ideal spot for a peaceful retreat in the heart of the island's stunning countryside.

locations.title: Tihu Camping Hut
locations.description: A small camping hut on the side of the Tihu hiking trail. There are some basic ammenities such as an outhouse and a fireplace within the hut but it is not allowed to make a fire outside of the hut.

locations.title: Tihu Lake Lookout Platform
locations.description: A small platform at the edge of the Lake Tihu providing an excellent view of the lake. The way there is along the Tihu hiking trail which is partially a wooden walkway because of the humid environment.

locations.title: Metsapere Camping Hut
locations.description: A nice little camping hut located next to a pond in a forest that is rich in mushrooms and berries. The hut does not have a fireplace inside of it but it is still good as a shelter.

locations.title: Sõru Lighthouse
locations.description: The Sõru Lighthouse is located on the northern coast of the island of Hiiumaa, and was built in 1960 to replace an older lighthouse that was destroyed during World War II. Visitors can climb to the top of the lighthouse for a stunning view of the surrounding area, and the nearby Sõru Maritime Museum offers a fascinating look at the history of the lighthouse and the region's seafaring traditions.

locations.title: Liivalauka Beach
locations.description: Liivalauka Beach is a sandy beach located on the north shore of the island of Hiiumaa in Estonia, and it is known for its clean and clear water, as well as its picturesque surroundings. The beach is a popular spot for swimming, sunbathing, and other outdoor activities.

locations.title: Tohvri Military Battery
locations.description: This military battery was a coastal artillery battery built by the Soviet Union during the Cold War, located on the southern coast of Hiiumaa. It was completed in 1960 and was operational until the collapse of the Soviet Union in the early 1990s. The site is now abandoned and partially destroyed, but some of the bunkers and other structures are still standing and can be visited by tourists.

locations.title: Viscosa Industry Building
locations.description: The Viscosa silk factory buildings were built in 1913 in Kõrgessaare, Hiiumaa. The factory was in operation until 1940, and today the buildings are used as a cultural center and exhibition space.

locations.title: Ninaotsa Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the northern coast of Hiiumaa, the location is a popular spot for camping and enjoying nature, with amenities such as tent sites, fire pits, and dry toilets. The area is known for its scenic views of the Baltic Sea and opportunities for hiking, fishing, and birdwatching.

locations.title: Ungru Boulder
locations.description: Also known as Ungru Manor Stone, is a large boulder located in Hiiumaa, Estonia, which is believed to have been placed there by the ice sheet during the last glacial period. It is a popular tourist attraction and is surrounded by legends and stories.

locations.title: Kõrgessaare Manor Park
locations.description: A nature filled park with an interesting mossy alley of trees and a curved stone wall. Good for an easy walk.

locations.title: Kõrgessaare Lookout Tower
locations.description: An old boarder guard lookout tower built in the 80s. In very good state. A good view to the sea and the Matsalu nature park on the mainland. In vicinity are also a couple of abandoned military buildings.

locations.title: Wooden Eiffel Tower
locations.description: A popular tourist attraction built in 2007 by a local man, it stands at 31 meters. There is an adventure park and some interesting statues next to it, this area is sometimes called the Hiiumaa Disneyland.

locations.title: Kõrgessaare Port
locations.description: A pier near the Kõrgessaare lookout tower and Kõrgessaare lower and higher lighthouse. A nice view from the most northern point of the island.

locations.title: Kurisu Sinkhole
locations.description: This sinkhole has a diameter of about 32 metres and it is 6.2 metres deep. When it is dry, you can see a limestone bedrock and a little opening to a cave at the bottom. During spring, the crater fills with water and looks like a little lake.

locations.title: Kapasto Camping Hut
locations.description: Located in the heart of Hiiumaa, on the site of a former forest ranger station. It offers a peaceful and quiet retreat away from the hustle and bustle of civilization. The cabin is simple and rustic, with no electricity or running water, but it does come equipped with a wood-burning stove and basic kitchen equipment.

locations.title: Kaibaldi Sands
locations.description: The sand dunes are an important natural and historical site, known for its unique vegetation and sand formations. It has been designated as a nature reserve and protected area due to its ecological and cultural significance. The site is open to visitors who can explore the dunes and learn about the area's natural and cultural history.

locations.title: Mudaste Port
locations.description: Mudaste Port is near a small fishing village on the northwestern coast of the island of Saaremaa in Estonia. The port has a long history, with evidence of human activity dating back to the Iron Age. Today, the port is primarily used by local fishermen and offers visitors a glimpse into traditional coastal life in Estonia.

locations.title: Mangu Beach
locations.description: A sandy beach located on the northern coast of Hiiumaa, the second-largest island of Estonia. The beach is known for its beautiful natural scenery and calm waters, making it a popular spot for swimming, sunbathing, and relaxing.

locations.title: Ristimägi
locations.description: Ristimägi is a hill located in the middle of Hiiumaa island in Estonia, standing at 68 meters tall. It is considered a sacred site by the locals and features a unique landscape with natural ponds, ancient burial mounds and a trail leading to the hill's peak, offering a panoramic view of the surrounding forests and farmland.

locations.title: Tahkuna Lighthouse
locations.description: Tahkuna Lighthouse is a prominent lighthouse located on the northernmost point of Hiiumaa island, in Estonia. The lighthouse was first lit in 1875 and stands at a height of 43 meters, making it one of the highest in the country.

locations.title: Tahkuna Military Defenses
locations.description: Around this location there are over 10 buildings that remain from the World War 2 era that have been abandoned for decades. A lot of these structures are somewhat hidden but the location should be roughly the middle point of the area.

locations.title: Tahkuna Military Ruins
locations.description: There are a lot of coastal defence batteries here which were built before the first World War. The condition of these coastal defence structures is not the best since after the war the occupying forces blew up some of these buildings so that they could not be used later on.

locations.title: Tahkuna Radar Hill
locations.description: This hill had a radar station that was built in the beginning of the 1980s which was used by the near by command center.

locations.title: Tahkuna Command Center
locations.description: This command center goes through 2 stories and has a lot of rooms to explore and is said to be one of the largest of it's kind. The command center was built along with the near by radar hill and artillery batteries during the cold war.

locations.title: Lõimastu Artillery Battery 1
locations.description: This is one of the two parts of the coastal artillery battery nr.316. The two batteries connect to each other through a 400m underground tunnel. It is one of the most recently structures from the soviet era.

locations.title: Lõimastu Artillery Battery 2
locations.description: This is one of the two parts of the coastal artillery battery nr.316. The two batteries connect to each other through a 400m underground tunnel. It is one of the most recently structures from the soviet era.

locations.title: Lussuliiva Beach
locations.description: Lussuliiva Beach is a beach located in the northern part of Hiiumaa island in Estonia, known for its sandy dunes and shallow waters. It is a popular destination for swimming, sunbathing, and nature walks.

locations.title: Tõrvanina Camping Ground
locations.description: Tõrvanina camping ground is located under the pine trees along Tõrvanina beach, offering visitors beautiful sands and a sea with a gradual depth. The camping area is equipped with seven covered fire pits and tables, two dry toilets, three wood sheds, and trash containers in the large parking lot.

locations.title: Autobaasi Camping Ground
locations.description: Autobaasi lõkkekoht is a campfire site located in the Hiiumaa region of Estonia. The site offers fire pits, a covered picnic table, and wood for visitors to enjoy while camping and exploring the surrounding forest. Close by is the Kärdla hiking trail.

locations.title: Kärdla Beach
locations.description: Kärdla Beach is a sandy beach located in the town of Kärdla on the island of Hiiumaa in Estonia. It is a popular spot for swimming and sunbathing in the summer, and there are facilities available such as a beach bar and a playground.

locations.title: Kukka Boulder
locations.description: This large glacial erratic boulder is located on the northern coast of Hiiumaa island in Estonia. It is a popular natural landmark and tourist destination known for its unique shape and size.

locations.title: Kukka Field Boulder
locations.description: This is a large erratic boulder located in the village of Kukka on the island of Hiiumaa, Estonia. The boulder is approximately 7 meters long, 4 meters wide, and 3 meters high and is believed to have been brought to the area by glaciers during the last Ice Age.

locations.title: Säärenina Camping Ground
locations.description: A small juniper-covered peninsula on the north eastern shore of Hiiumaa, offering visitors a stone beach, sea views from a viewing platform, and three covered fireplaces, two covered picnic tables, one table with benches, an outdoor toilet, a shelter for firewood, and a great place to enjoy the natural surroundings.

locations.title: Kuri Church Ruins
locations.description: The Kuri Church Ruins is a historic site located on the island of Hiiumaa in Estonia. The ruins of the old church serve as a reminder of the centuries-old religious heritage and cultural history of the island. The site is open to the public for exploration and offers a glimpse into the past with its well-preserved walls and stunning views of the surrounding area.

locations.title: Kallaste Cliff
