locations.description: Kallaste Cliff is a 400-meter-long and up to 10-meter-high limestone cliff located in Hiiumaa. The cliff is covered with an old pine forest and a forest road runs along the edge of the cliff.

locations.title: Sarve Camping Ground
locations.description: The Sarve Camping Ground offers a picturesque natural setting, with a sandy beach, dunes, and a pine forest. The site features several amenities, including picnic tables, fire pits, dry toilets, and a designated area for barbecues. Additionally, visitors can enjoy activities such as swimming, fishing, and hiking on nearby trails.

locations.title: Vaemla Manor Ruins
locations.description: Vaemla Manor ruins are a set of historical ruins located in the Hiiumaa Island in Estonia. The ruins of the 16th-century manor house are located on a small hill surrounded by a park, and it's a popular tourist attraction for visitors who are interested in history and architecture.

locations.title: Kassari Beach
locations.description: This white sandy beach is known for its picturesque views and shallow waters, making it a popular spot for swimming and sunbathing during the summer months. The area also offers hiking trails, bird watching opportunities, and a lighthouse to explore.

locations.title: Sääretirbi Camping Ground
locations.description: A camping area located on the southern shore of Hiiumaa, Estonia. The area is situated on a narrow landmass, Sääre Tirp, which stretches out into the sea, and is surrounded by beautiful scenery and wildlife. The camping area offers visitors a chance to enjoy the great outdoors with facilities such as fire pits, picnic tables, and outhouses available for use.

locations.title: Sääre Tirp
locations.description: Sääre Tirp is a narrow peninsula that stretches into the sea and offers beautiful views of the surrounding landscape. The area is popular for hiking and birdwatching, as well as fishing and swimming in the summer.

locations.title: Orjaku Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located on the island of Hiiumaa in Estonia, overlooking the beautiful bay of Orjaku. It's a popular spot for birdwatching, especially during the migration periods. The tower stands at 10 meters high, and there are several viewing platforms at different levels, providing visitors with excellent views of the surrounding landscape and birdlife.

locations.title: Lehtma Bunker 1
locations.description: The reinforced concrete "dotts" or bunkers were built in 1940-1941, but were not used much for military purposes since the enemy arrived from a different direction than expected. The defensive line was built on the eastern and northern coasts of Hiiumaa, but the German landing occurred in October 1941 on the southern coast. The Sarve-Lehtma defensive line's "dotts" are heavy machine gun positions, uniform in type and meant for 2-3 fighters, although there are minor differences in construction (size, number of firing ports, shape and arrangement of tambours, etc.)

locations.title: Kalana Military Campus
locations.description: This was a campus built for military purposes in 1941 as an addition to the previously built structures near by. During the 1960s a radar station was built for surveilance and later on in the 80s they started with additional buildings but those were never completed.

locations.title: Uljaste Camping Ground
locations.description: The campsite offers a beautiful natural environment for camping and hiking. Visitors can enjoy the sandy beach, go fishing, or take a walk in the nearby forest. The camping area includes fire pits, camping tables, and dry toilets.

locations.title: Purtse Fish Industry
locations.description: The factory was established in 1906 and was the largest producer of canned fish in Estonia during the Soviet era. After the collapse of the Soviet Union, the factory went bankrupt and has been abandoned ever since. The buildings and facilities, including a large smokestack and a number of industrial machines, have fallen into disrepair and are popular with urban explorers and photographers.

locations.title: Saka Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located on the Saka Cliff next to the Saka manor. The lookout tower used to be a coast guard station decades ago and now after being renovated, it provides a great lookout point over the sea for anyone in the area.

locations.title: Aidu-Nõmme Camping Ground
locations.description: The camp ground is situated in the Aidu quarry park, which is known for its unique landscape of colorful cliffs and crystal-clear lakes. The camping site offers several fireplaces, a dry toilet, and a shelter for protection from the weather.

locations.title: Kohtla-Nõmme Ash Hill
locations.description: It is composed of over 30 meters of ash and oil shale waste that were piled up during the mining process. Today, the hill offers a stunning panoramic view of the surrounding landscape and is a popular destination for hiking and sightseeing.

locations.title: Aidu Quarry Raft
locations.description: This is a Raft in the middle of the Aidu quarry and it can be used to cross a gap of water. The raft can be moved by pulling a rope attached to the other side.

locations.title: Varessaare Camping Hut
locations.description: Located next to the Muraka bog the camping hut can be used as a camping shelter or a place for hikers to rest. The cabin is basic and equipped with simple amenities, including a fireplace, a stove, and bunk beds. The surrounding forest offers a peaceful and natural setting for visitors to enjoy.

locations.title: Kauksi Lookout Tower
locations.description: Situated on a high sand dune overlooking Lake Peipus, which is the fourth largest lake in Europe. The platform offers panoramic views of the lake.

locations.title: Kauksi Camping Ground
locations.description: It is said to be the biggest official camping ground in Estonia situated on the sandy northern shore of lake Peipsi. With a lot of fireplaces, covered sitting areas, outhouses and even wells for pumping up clean water.

locations.title: Kauksi Beach
locations.description: Kauksi Beach is located on the eastern coast of Estonia, in Ida-Viru County. It is a popular sandy beach with a length of 3.5 km, bordered by a pine forest. The water in the sea is shallow.

locations.title: Uusküla Camping Ground
locations.description: This camping ground is just a few hundred meters from the lake of Peipsi, far enough to avoid the strong winds and close enough for an easy access to the beach. The site offers ammenities like fireplaces, wells, outhouses and sitting areas.

locations.title: Alajõe Beach
locations.description: The beach is known for its beautiful sandy coastline and is a popular spot for swimming, sunbathing, and other water activities. The area is surrounded by forests and is home to several bird species, making it a great destination for nature lovers.

locations.title: Karjamaa Camping Ground
locations.description: Just a 100 meters from the beautiful shoreline of Peipsi, the camping ground has a sitting areas, fireplaces an outhouse and a well.

locations.title: Vasknarva Castle Ruins
locations.description: Vasknarva Castle was initially built in the 14th century and now the ruins stand near the Narva River and the border between Estonia and Russia. The ruins of the castle offer a glimpse into medieval history and the surrounding area is known for its picturesque landscape and unique flora and fauna.

locations.title: Puhatu Camping Ground
locations.description: Puhatu lõkkekoht is a popular camping and picnic area located in the Puhatu Nature Reserve in Ida-Virumaa. The site features fire pits, firewood, and dry toilets, and is surrounded by beautiful forested areas and hiking trails.

locations.title: Kõrtsikraavi Camping Ground
locations.description: Located next to the waterway Kõrtsikraav, it is a quiet camping area surrounded by forest. The camping ground has a covered sitting area, a fireplace and an outhouse.

locations.title: Rüütli Camping Ground
locations.description: Located in the Muraka nature reserve, which is home to a diverse landscape and many rare species of plants, animals, and birds. The campsite offers a small hut, a covered sitting area, a fireplace and an outhouse.

locations.title: Kotka Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located in the Muraka Nature Reserve and offers stunning views of the surrounding forests, bogs and marshes. The tower is 25 meters high and provides visitors with a bird's eye view of the area, which is known for its diverse wildlife, including rare bird species like the golden eagle and black stork.

locations.title: Agusalu Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated in the Agusalu nature reserve, which is characterized by a mix of marshes and long, narrow and high sandy ridges. The site offers a covered sitting area, a fireplace and an outhouse.

locations.title: Jõuga Järvede Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of the Pesujärve lake. It's a popular swimming spot with a boardwalk leading to the lake and a comfortable swimming dock for easy access to the water. The site offers multiple fireplaces, covered sitting areas, a well and an outhouse.

locations.title: Selisoo Camping Ground
locations.description: Close to the Seli Bog and hiking trail, this campground is a good place for staying the night or having a picnic. The site features covered sitting areas, fireplaces and an outhouse.

locations.title: Saka Missile Base
locations.description: Located on the Saka Cliff, the missile base was built in the year 1968 and was abandoned in the year 1993. The area has a lot of different building remains and those are mostly in ruins but it is still an interesting place to visit for a glimpse into the past.

locations.title: Ratva Pipe Spring
locations.description: This is a series of natural springs located in the small village of Ratva. The water from these springs is known for its high iron content, which gives it a distinctive reddish-brown color. People have been visiting the Ratva springs for centuries, believing in their healing properties, and the area has been designated as a nature reserve. Visitors can see the natural beauty of the springs and even drink the water, which is said to be good for your health.

locations.title: Konsu Camping Ground
locations.description: Konsu Camping Ground is located on the shore of the Konsu Lake which is a good spot for fishing or swimming. The camping ground has a lot of space for setting up tents, multiple fireplaces and sitting areas and 2 outhouses.

locations.title: Martiska Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated next to the Martiska Lake, it is a good peaceful spot for camping or picnicing. The site offers picnic tables, fireplaces and an outhouse.

locations.title: Rääkjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: The campground is situated on the shore of Rääkjärve Lake and provides a picturesque location for a picnic or camping trip. Visitors can enjoy fishing and swimming in the lake, as well as hiking in the surrounding forest. The site has fireplaces, covered sitting areas and outhouses.

locations.title: Liivjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Liivjärve Camping Ground is a popular outdoor recreation spot located near the beautiful Liivjärve lake in Estonia. Visitors can enjoy a range of activities such as swimming, hiking, and fishing, as well as relax and cook food on the provided fire pits. The site also includes an outhouse and covered sitting spots.

locations.title: Viivikonna
locations.description: Viivikonna is a former mining settlement in the northeastern part of Estonia. The settlement was founded in the early 20th century and was abandoned in the 1990s after the closure of the local mine. Nowadays, Viivikonna is mostly abandoned and has become a popular destination for urban explorers and photographers. The area offers a unique glimpse into the region's industrial history and the remnants of the former mining town.

locations.title: Narva River Canyon
locations.description: This is an interesting natural addition to see in the city of Narva. The canyon was made by the Narva River that has cut through the limestone soil over time.

locations.title: Narva Castle
locations.description: Narva Castle is a medieval fortress located in Narva, Estonia, on the banks of the Narva River, which marks the border between Estonia and Russia. The castle was founded in the 13th century by the Danes and rebuilt in the 14th century by the Livonian Order. Today it houses a museum and is a popular tourist attraction.

locations.title: Narva Promenade
locations.description: The promenade runs along the Narva River and offers scenic views of the river, the Narva Castle, and the Ivangorod Fortress on the Russian side of the border. The promenade is a popular spot for locals and visitors to go for a walk, run, or bike ride.

locations.title: Tõrvajõe Waterfall
locations.description: This waterfall starts from the river Tõrvajõgi and flows into the Tõrvajõe canyon. The steepest drop is 2,5 meters in height and the best time to visit would be during a wet period, since during the dry period the waterfall might not have enough water to properly flow.

locations.title: Narva-Jõesuu Beach
locations.description: A popular holiday destination situated on the Gulf of Finland in Northeastern Estonia. The beach is known for its long and wide stretch of soft sand, and there are many amenities on site, including restaurants, cafes, and shops. The surrounding area is also popular for its lush pine forests and numerous hiking trails.

locations.title: Karjaoru Waterfall
locations.description: Located in Saka village, in Ida-Viru County, the waterfall is fed by water from the Ontika main ditch and consists of two levels with a total height of 12 meters, making it the third highest waterfall in Estonia.

locations.title: Utria Cliff
locations.description: Utria Cliff is a 24-38 meter-high limestone cliff located in the northern part of Estonia, in Ida-Viru County. The cliff, which was formed during the Ordovician period, offers a scenic view of the surrounding landscape and is also home to a variety of rare plants and animals, making it a popular destination for nature enthusiasts.

locations.title: Mummassaare Camping Ground
locations.description: Mummassaare campfire site is located just a few dozen meters from the seaside. In the 19th century, the seaside was a summer destination for the nobility and merchants from St. Petersburg and Narva. The campground has an outhouse, a roofed sitting area and a fireplace.

locations.title: Sinimäe Lookout Tower
locations.description: The Sinimäe Lookout Tower is located in the Vaivara Landscape Reserve on Pargimägi Hill. The wooden tower is 29.5 meters high with 3 observation platforms, the highest one at 25.5 meters offering a panoramic view of the surrounding area, including the sea and the city of Sillamäe.

locations.title: Sillamäe Mere Puiestee
locations.description: The Sillamäe staircase and seaside boulevard are two symbols of Sillamäe that reflect the ideology of Stalinist neoclassicism. Architecture was a propaganda tool during the Soviet era, through which the authorities communicated with the people. The central square's staircase was adorned with sculptures and live palms to create a complete design, while the seaside boulevard features symmetrically designed buildings in Stalinist neoclassical style.

locations.title: Sillamäe Promenade
locations.description: The Sillamäe Beach Promenade connects the historical town center with the seaside, providing entertainment and activities for the whole family. The promenade is an attractive venue for sports and cultural events, as well as for those who enjoy a beach vacation. It features a seven-meter high observation tower, wooden terraces and platforms, ball courts, playgrounds, fire pits, an outdoor gym, seven fountains, and an adventure trail with the Robinson Bridge over the Sõtke River.

locations.title: Langevoja Fireplace
locations.description: The campfire site is located next to the Langevoja waterfall, and while having a picnic there, visitors can listen to and watch the multi-level waterfalls, which are particularly powerful during high tide in early spring.

locations.title: Saka Waterfall
locations.description: Saka Waterfall is located in Ida-Viru County, on the grounds of the former Saka manor from which it gets its name. The waterfall is made up of three large (6.0 m, 2.0 m, and 7.5 m) and several smaller (up to 1 m) cascades that drop about 48 meters from the Saka cliff to the sea. The waterfall is one of the highest in Estonia, with a 22.8-meter drop, ranking it second.

locations.title: Hõbeallika Cave
locations.description: Hõbeallika Cave is located in Toila-Oru Park, Ida-Viru County, Estonia. The triangular cave opening is 1.7 meters wide and 1.4 meters high, while the length of the cave extends up to 6 meters. The cave features a mixture of different rocks, including sandstone, quartz, and siltstone, and is renowned for its clear and pure water from the Hõbeallika spring.

locations.title: Toila Beach
locations.description: The area is known for its soft sandy beaches and clear waters. The beach is situated in the small town of Toila and features various amenities such as changing rooms, showers, a workout ground and a playground. Visitors can also enjoy stunning views of the nearby Toila Oru Park and the Gulf of Finland.

locations.title: Valaste Waterfall
locations.description: Valaste Waterfall is the highest waterfall in Estonia, located on the Ontika limestone cliff. It drops 30 meters into the Gulf of Finland and is surrounded by scenic views of the Baltic Sea. The area is accessible via a wooden staircase and is a popular destination for nature lovers and photographers.

locations.title: Valaste Artillery Battery
locations.description: Built in 1944, the site was part of a coastal defense system during World War II and served as an artillery battery to defend the Soviet Union against a potential naval invasion. Today, the site is a popular tourist attraction known for its striking coastal cliffs and scenic views of the Gulf of Finland.

locations.title: Kakerdaja Bog
locations.description: A well known bog in Estonia which has a well maintained wooden walkway through the bog. In the middle of the bog there is a big lake with no fish.

locations.title: Kakerdi Lake
locations.description: The lake which is in the middle of the Kakerdaja bog. Just follow the wooden walkway to get here. The water has a low mineral content, so it does not have much living in it.

locations.title: Reinevere Missile Base
locations.description: The missile base, that was built in the 1960s , has been abandoned since the 1990s. After the base was abandoned, there have been some lootings of the area and some buildings have been removed and others have decayed on their own, but there is still plenty to see.

locations.title: Reinevere Command Station
locations.description: This used to be the command station for the Reinevere Missile base to the west. Some buildings remain intact and it is worth a check out.

locations.title: Türi Beach
locations.description: Located next to an artificial lake in Estonia, created in the 1950s by damming the River Pärnu. It was intended for regulating water flow and irrigation but is now a popular recreational area for swimming, boating, fishing, and hiking.

locations.title: Napu Camping Ground
locations.description: This is a small and modest campfire site located by the Jägala River. The site includes an open fireplace with a grill, a table with three benches, a rubbish bin, a dry toilet and a shelter for firewood.

locations.title: Matsimäe Quarry Camping Ground
locations.description: The campground is located near a lake formed in an old quarry in Järvamaa. The site includes a covered fireplace with a grill, a shelter with benches and a table, two additional benches, a woodshed that can also be used for overnight stays, a dry toilet, and a garbage bin.

locations.title: Seli Bog Lookout Tower
locations.description: This is a small 5 meters tall lookout tower in the middle of the Seli bog right next to the multiple bog lakes. It is not very tall but it still provides a decent view of the surrounding bog.

locations.title: Paide Beach
locations.description: This artificial lake offers a sandy beach area for swimming and sunbathing, as well as a playground and a beach volleyball court. The beach is free to use and is supervised during the summer months.

locations.title: Matsimäe Pühajärve Camping Ground
locations.description: This is a spacious camping area located on the shore of a 5.5-hectare lake in Järvamaa. The lake is suitable for swimming, and the area has plenty of space for sunbathing. The campsite features covered and open fireplaces, a picnic area, seating benches, a dry toilet, a woodshed, parking space, garbage cans, and swimming bridges.

locations.title: Seli Bog
locations.description: Seli Bog is located in Järva County, covering an area of approximately 9,500 hectares. The bog is known for its diverse flora and fauna, including rare and protected species, and offers visitors a chance to hike and explore the unique landscape by using the wooden walkway of the Simisalu-Matsimäe hiking trail.

locations.title: Simisalu Lookout Tower
locations.description: The tower is 18 meters high and can be climbed via a staircase. From the top of the tower, visitors can enjoy a beautiful view of the surrounding landscape, including the nearby village and its traditional farmsteads. There is also a fireplace with a sitting area next to the tower for having a picnic.

locations.title: Kodru Bog Lookout Tower
locations.description: In the middle of the Kodru bog is a two-storey lookout tower. The tower is not very tall but it still provides a beautiful view of the surrounding bog.

locations.title: Kodru Bog
locations.description: Kodru Bog is a large peat bog located in the Järva County, surrounded by forested islands and with an irregularly-shaped lake, Vibujärv, at its center. The peat layer of the bog can reach up to 8.5 meters in thickness. The area features a 5 km hiking trail known as the Kodru Bog Trail or Tammsaare-Järva-Madise Nature Trail.

locations.title: Kurisoo Camping Ground
locations.description: This campground is located in a large spruce forest near Aravete village and usually it is a quiet site to camp at. It features an open fireplace with a grill, a large shelter with benches, a woodshed, a garbage bin, and a dry toile

locations.title: Tamme Camping Ground
locations.description: This is a simple camping ground located near the former Tamme forest ranger's farmhouse in Järvamaa. It features an open fireplace with a grill rack, a shelter with table and benches, two benches, a dry toilet, a trash can, and a shelter for firewood.

locations.title: Kirikumäe Camping Ground
locations.description: Kirikumäe Camping site is located on the banks of the Põltsamaa River in Järvamaa. There is the Purskav Spring along the way to the site. The campfire site features an open fireplace with a grill, an information board, and two benches.

locations.title: Purskava Allika Camping Ground
locations.description: This is a camping site located on a small island formed by the redirection of the Oostriku River in Järvamaa. The site is known for its natural spring and the water is safe to drink. The site offers an open fire pit with a grill rack, a table and benches, a shelter, a compost toilet, and a wrought-iron funnel for the spring.

locations.title: Purskav Spring
locations.description: The spurting spring is a man-made artesian well-type spring located in Järva County. It was formed in the 1980s during the mapping of the Oostriku River when geologists accidentally drilled into a water vein.

locations.title: Sopa Spring
locations.description: Sopa Spring is the deepest spring in Estonia, located in the Endla Nature Reserve in Järva County. The spring has a funnel-like shape and is surrounded by decaying peat with a thickness of nearly 3 meters, followed by bedrock. The spring funnel itself is 4.8 meters deep, 6 meters wide, and 3 meters long.

locations.title: Oostriku Jõe Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is located next to the Oostrik river. The site features an open fireplace with a grill, a shelter with a table and benches, seating areas, and a dry toilet. Visitors can also observe the convergence of Oostriku River, Norra Stream, and Vao main ditch.

locations.title: Norra Manor Ruins
locations.description: Norra Manor in Estonia was first mentioned in 1569 and was owned by the Knorring family from the 17th century until 1908. The classicist main building had two floors with nature-themed paintings in the interior. The manor was used as a school until 1971 and was later left abandoned.

locations.title: Laimetsa Manor Ruins
locations.description: Laimetsa Manor Ruins is located in Järva County and dates back to the 17th century. The manor had a fire in the 1980s and since then it has been abandoned. The ruins of the manor house and the beautiful surrounding park offer a glimpse into the history and culture of the area.

locations.title: Metsavenna Camping Ground
locations.description: Metsavenna lõkkekoht in Sakala Recreation Area offers a chance to experience the life of people who were forced to live in the forest during the war. The site features an open fireplace with a grill, a shelter with seating, and a woodshed. There is also a lean-to shelter that can accommodate up to 3 people for overnight stays.

locations.title: Kabelimäe Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the former ridges of the Baltic Ice Lake in the Sakala recreation area. The campfire site can be a lifesaver for those who have planned to stay at the nearby Saeveski forest hut but for some reason couldn't fit there. The campfire site features an open fireplace with a grill, a sheltered table with benches, a shelter for firewood, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Saeveski Camping Hut
locations.description: The forest hut is a spacious two-storey house and is suitable for larger hiking groups. Do not expect too many amenities, but the peacefulness of the nearby forest compensates for the lack of electricity and running water. The hut features a woodshed, an open fireplace with a grill grate, and a parking area. Inside the hut, there is a stove with basic cooking utensils, and the second floor has sleeping platforms.

locations.title: Võlingi Spring
locations.description: Võlingi spring is a natural spring, which is a popular destination for nature lovers and hikers who come to enjoy the peaceful surroundings and crystal clear water. The area around the spring is well-maintained, with picnic tables and benches available for visitors to use. The water from Võlingi allikas is said to have healing properties and is known for its purity. Võlingi Spring discharges 220 liters of water per second.

locations.title: Rava Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated next to the Rava lake, the simple camping ground has a fireplace, a seat and an outhouse. There is a small beach on the other side of the lake.

locations.title: Võlingi Camping Ground
locations.description: Located at the beginning of the Võlingi springs hiking trail, the campground has a fireplace and a sitting area with a raincover.

locations.title: Raadna Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground located on the shore of Lake Peipus has lakeside pine stands and patches of sandy beach between tall reeds, you can find bilberries and chanterelles. There are 10 campfire sites, 10 shelters, 4 dry toilets and a water well. No firewood provided at the campsite.

locations.title: Mustvee Beach
locations.description: A nice beach on the shore of Lake Peipus. There is a pier, sandy beach and nice nature around.

locations.title: Kõrgekalda Camping Ground
locations.description: The campfire site is on the high bank of the picturesque Kääpa River and in the immediate vicinity of the Koseveski cycling trail (20 km). There is a picnic table with shelter, fire ring with barbecue grill, benches, firewood shed, and a dry toilet. You will have a spectacular view over the Kääpa River and there are diverse forests with bilberries an different mushrooms around.

locations.title: Kaiu Järve Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is situated on the shore of Lake Kaiu, which is surrounded by a forest of alternating terrain, rich in wild berries and mushrooms. There is a picnic table with shelter, an outdoor fireplace, a woodshed with sleeping loft and a composting toilet. In the vicinity are the Kaiu cycling trail (45 km) and Peraküla-Aegviidu-Ähijärve hiking route.

locations.title: Kukeseene Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground, located on the shore of Lake Kaiu, is suitable for hikers who like to walk in the forest of alternating terrain, rich in wild berries and mushrooms. It has a picnic table with shelter, an outhouse, a fireplace and a firewood shed with a sleeping option on the second floor.

locations.title: Kalamehe Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the northern shore of Lake Kaiu, right next to the Tammeluha campfire site, the camping ground has a picnic table with shelter, outdoor fireplace, woodshed with sleeping loft and a composting toilet. There is versatile terrain of the Kaiu karnes, Lake Kaiu and Tammeluha floodplain and Pedassaar, with forests rich in wild berries and mushrooms.

locations.title: Papi Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of Lake Saare and next to the captivating study trail that runs around the lake, the camping ground has a picnic table with shelter, an outdoor fireplace, 2 fire rings with a barbecue grill, a firewood shelter and a composting toilet.

locations.title: Sihi Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is under the tall pine forest on a versatile terrain next to the Lake Saare. There is a picnic table with shelter, outdoor fireplace, benches, firewood shelter and composting toilet. Nearby is the Lake Saare 3.5 km study trail.

locations.title: Altmetsa Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located on the Kirna study trail, the 14 m four-storey lookout tower at Altmetsa farmstead provides a great view of the meadows and floodplain forest of the Pedja river. Nearby is the majestic oak tree Musutamm and in several places you can see signs of wild boar and beaver activity. Camping available at the nearby Altmetsa Camping Ground.

locations.title: Altmetsa Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is on the lands of the old Altmetsa farm, 1.5 km from the Kirna study trail. There is an observation tower at the camping ground, which offers a good view of the Pedja river and flooded meadow. There is a campfire site with grill rest, benches and a dry toilet. The Kirna study trail can be impassable during the flood and gun shots can often be heard – this is because of the shooting practice of the Estonian Defense League.

locations.title: Madisemäe Lookout Tower
locations.description: Built in 1952, the 36 m iron lookout tower was a part of the Soviet army bomber force polygon and was in use until 1992. Currently it is not in use, but it is possible to climb up at one’s own discretion. In the surrounding forest, it is still possible to find many training bombs, basements of the old military buildings, and a small cabin. It is difficult to reach, since there is no actual road but dense thickets and soft watery soil.

locations.title: Siimusti Camping Ground
locations.description: Located in the varied Siimusti Kurista Landscape Protection Area, the camping ground is very popular with its hiking and walking trails. It has a table and benches with shelter, outdoor fireplace, firewood shelter (firewood provided) and a dry toilet. Nearby is the Siimusti hill fort and forest landscapes with varied terrain.

locations.title: Laiuse Castle Ruins
locations.description: uilt in the late 14th century, it was the first defensive structure in Estonia to be fitted with firearms, the cannon towers were added in the mid-15th century. The fortress crumbled in 1559, but was restored in 1700. Currently it is in ruins, but enough is preserved to be an interesting sight.

locations.title: Vana Paadisadama Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the end of the Lake Endla hiking trail, the camping ground is the site of a former boat harbor with a platform for watching the lake. It has a fire ring, observation platform, 2 benches and a toilet. No firewood provided at the camping ground. Nearby is also the Lake Endla bird observation tower and the Kaasikjärve bog.

locations.title: Sinijärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of Lake Sinijärv, there is a fire ring, a shelter and an outhouse. Nearby are Räägi canal, pool-andweir fish ladder and protected orchid species. The camping ground is closed for visitors from 01 April to 01 July.

locations.title: Männikjärve Lookout Tower
locations.description: The 7 m tower is located at the 7.3 km Männikjärve bog study trail and it is possible to see the entire 210-hectare Lake Männikjärv bog and the more than 120 pools located in the bog from there.

locations.title: Linajärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Located at Lake Linajärv, there is a campfire ring, picnic table and wooden pier on the lake. Nearby are the 2.2 km Männikjärve hiking trail and the 7.3 km Männikjärve bog study trail with lots of interesting vegetation.

locations.title: Hundissaare Camping Ground
locations.description: A fairly modest camping ground located at a pond, with a table and benches with shelter, a fire ring with grill, firewood shelter and a dry toilet, meant as a stop on the Peraküla-Aegviidu-Ähijärve hiking route. In the vicinity, there are ancient forests with plenty of mushrooms.

locations.title: Laius Hill
locations.description: The height of the hill from sea level is 145,4 meters. On the top of the hill is the Laiuse Siniallikas, which is known in folklore to cure many ailments.

locations.title: Kuremaa Beach
locations.description: There is a 250-meter stretch of sand, a café, areas for ball games and a caravan park. It is also possible to camp there.

locations.title: Põltsamaa Palace
locations.description: Constructed on the banks of Põltsamaa river in 1272, it was founded as a purely defensive crusader fortress, it has been repeatedly pillaged throughout history. Currently only ruins remain of the main buildings.

locations.title: Tammeluha Camping Ground
locations.description: This is one of the five campfire sites around Kaiu Lakes. The site is situated on a land strip between the lakes, near the Tammeluha hiking trail leading to Pedassaare. It features a covered table, fire pit, benches, firewood shelter, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Tooma Camping Ground
locations.description: Tooma campfire site is located in the center of Tooma village, on the shore of Lake Linajärv in Jõgeva County. It serves as the starting point for hiking trails in Männikjärve bog and as the village square. The site features an information board, an outdoor stage with 20 benches, an open fireplace with a grill, 6 benches, a table, and an outhouse.

locations.title: Rabasaare
locations.description: An abandoned village which was built in the 1950s for peat mining purposes and a bit after that the village started declining and now remains abandoned. Most buildings are open but have signs stating that you shouldn't enter due to possible collapsings happening.

locations.title: Põima Commando
locations.description:
As part of the 14th Air Defense Division during the Cold War era, the division consisted of air defense missile units, radio technical units, and fighter aviation units, and had a high level of air defense capability. The Põima center was formed in 1960 and consisted of one brigade with six radio technical battalions.
Now the some of the structures remain but it a lot of it is in a bad state.

locations.title: Imastu Missile Base
locations.description:
Imastu rocket base was a secret military installation built by the Soviet army in Estonia. It was used to store and launch ground-to-air missiles. When the military left, the rocket launch pads and other equipment were sold or looted.
Now mostly just ruins remain.

locations.title: Kadila Missile Base
locations.description: Kadila and Rohu missile bases were among the largest and most secret military objects in Estonia during the Soviet era, serving as launch sites for nuclear missiles with a range of 2000 km. Today, they are overgrown with forest, but remnants of the giant concrete hangars, launch pads, fuel depots, and barbed wire fences can still be seen. The bases were abandoned in the 1980s with the introduction of new missile technology.

locations.title: Saartneem
locations.description: An island off the coast of the Käsmu peninsula. The island can sometimes be accessed on foot when the water levels are low, otherwise it is a few hundred meters off the coast.

locations.title: Palganeem
locations.description: At the tip of the Käsmu peninsula with a small abandoned building. There is a wide view over the sea from this location.

locations.title: Võsu Camping Ground
locations.description: Võsu camping site is located in Lahemaa National Park, close to the Võsu river and the sea, making it a suitable stop for hikers on the RMK Oandu-Aegviidu-Ikla trail. The site offers sitting areas, covered and open fire pits, an outhouse and a woodshed.

locations.title: Võsu Beach
locations.description: Võsu Beach is a sandy beach located in the small town of Võsu. The beach is known for its picturesque surroundings, shallow waters, and family-friendly atmosphere.

locations.title: Ojakivi
locations.description: Ojakivi, also known as Sagadi kivi or Võsu rändrahn, is a massive glacial erratic boulder. It measures 11.6 meters in length, 10 meters in width, and 6 meters in height, with a circumference of 33 meters.

locations.title: Lobi Missile base 1
locations.description: The Lobi missile base was a Soviet military base, which was used to store and launch nuclear missiles during the Cold War era. The decommissioning began in 1989 after the Soviet Union collapsed.

locations.title: Lobi Missile base 2
locations.description: The Lobi missile base was a Soviet military base, which was used to store and launch nuclear missiles during the Cold War era. The decommissioning began in 1989 after the Soviet Union collapsed.

locations.title: Lobi Cape
locations.description: The tip of the Lobi peninsula with a nice open view to the sea.

locations.title: Ulkkari Island
locations.description: A small island that with time has connected with the nearby peninsula. It offers a picturesque view of the surrounding sea.

locations.title: Älvi Island
locations.description: Älvi island is a small island connected to land by a thin strip of rocks. The island has a bit of forest on it as well.

locations.title: Kuueristi Resting Spot
locations.description: Kuueristi Resting Spot is a perfect spot located in Lahemaa National Park, where visitors can enjoy the beauty of the forest, listen to birds singing and in the autumn season, it's worth bringing along a mushroom basket to pick some. The site has a sitting area as well.

locations.title: Vergi Beach
locations.description: Vergi Beach is located in the village of Vergi and it is known for its beautiful scenery and calm atmosphere, making it a popular spot for swimming, sunbathing, and enjoying the seaside.

locations.title: Altja Suspension Bridge (double removed)
locations.description: The bridge is a pedestrian suspension bridge located in Lahemaa National Park and The bridge spans over the Altja River and provides a scenic view of the surrounding forest and river.

locations.title: Altja Beach (double removed)
locations.description: A quiet beach next to a few boat houses near the Altja Village.

locations.title: Mustoja Beach
locations.description: Mustoja rand is a beautiful sandy beach located on the northern coast of Estonia. It is situated in a picturesque area surrounded by pine forests and offers visitors opportunities for swimming, sunbathing, and hiking on nearby nature trails.

locations.title: Mustoja Camping Ground
locations.description: The easternmost campfire site of the Lahemaa National Park in Lääne-Virumaa situated next to the Mustoja Stream. The site offers 2 covered fire pits, 1 open fire pit with a grill, 2 shelters, a lean-to, a woodshed, a dry toilet, and garbage bin.

locations.title: Vainupea Beach
locations.description: A well maintained beach with some clothes changing booths, a wooden walkway and trash cans for keeping clean. The beach is surrounded by pine forests and offers a peaceful and relaxing atmosphere for visitors.

locations.title: Vainupea Lighthouse
locations.description: Vainupea Lighthouse is a 17-meter tall white conical tower  at the Vainupea Beach that was built in 1871. A nice trail goes right past the lighthouse.

locations.title: Munasaar
locations.description: "Egg Island" in translation is a small island off the coast of the Letipea area. It does not have a lot to see but it is an interesting land formation.

locations.title: Ehalkivi
locations.description: Ehalkivi is the largest erratic boulder in Estonia, located on a sandy beach on Letipea Peninsula. It is a 16.5 m long, 14.3 m wide, 7.6 m high and weighs around 2500 tons with a circumference of 49.6 meters, and has been protected as a natural monument since 1937. According to local legends, Ehalkivi is believed to have been a throwing rock of the legendary Estonian hero Kalevipoeg.

locations.title: Kunda Beach
locations.description: Kunda Beach is a sandy beach that is known for its clean and shallow waters and offers facilities such as changing rooms, showers, and a playground.

locations.title: Kunda Cement Factory
locations.description: This used to be a cement factory that worked from 1870 to ca 1990. The buildings here are in ruins but it is an interesting place to check out since there are quite a lot of building still standing.

locations.title: Kutsala-Kaukvere Missile base
locations.description: This used to be a S-125 missile base during the soviet times but now it remains in ruins.

locations.title: Rutja Airfield
locations.description: The former Rutja Airfield is a former soviet time airfield that now remains used by the Estonian forces for training. There are a few buildings remaining on the premises but mostly there is just a long runway to see. The location might be occupied by military forces from time to time, restricted access in such case.

locations.title: Toolse Fortress Ruins
locations.description: This is a castle that was build in the 14th century and it was abandoned in the 18th century after the war "Põhjasõda" took place. Now it remains a historical place to see with beautiful surroundings.

locations.title: Karepa Beach
locations.description: This beach is known for it's shallow sea and sandy beach. It has been a popular summer destination since the late 19th century when several noble families built their summer cottages there.

locations.title: Rutja Coast Guard Station
locations.description: This former coast guard station is just a small structure on the beach. It is a nice little addition to the nearby Eisma Beach

locations.title: Eisma Beach
locations.description: A quiet sandy beach near the Eisma village.

locations.title: Mägede Camping Ground
locations.description: Located by a lake formed in an old sand quarry. There are three private campfire spots with grilling facilities, picnic tables, benches, and garbage bins, as well as two dry toilets and wood storage shelters.

locations.title: Põima Military Campus
locations.description: A former military campus that only has a few buildings standing, a lot of the buildings have been torn down.

locations.title: Leivaahjumäe Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the RMK Penijõe-Aegviidu-Kauksi hiking trail and Neeruti nature trail in Lääne-Virumaa. Hikers can rest and cook meals by the fire, using the covered campfire spot, picnic tables with shelter, and wood storage shelter, as well as a dry toilet. Nearby are the Neeruti lakes.

locations.title: Eesjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Located in the Neeruti Landscape Protection Area, offering visitors a chance to experience diverse natural surroundings and observe wildlife. The site features two open campfire spots, three picnic tables with shelters, a covered shelter, and a dry toilet for visitors' convenience.

locations.title: Rakvere Castle
locations.description: Rakvere Castle is a medieval fortress located in the town of Rakvere. Built in the 13th century, the castle has a rich history and has been renovated and restored several times. Today, it serves as a museum and tourist attraction, offering visitors a glimpse into Estonia's medieval past.

locations.title: Mardihansu Camping Ground
locations.description: Located in the Porkuni Landscape Reserve between two lakes and along the RMK Penijõe-Aegviidu-Kauksi hiking trail. The site features a covered campfire spot, a shelter with a table and benches, a lean-to shelter, firewood shelter, a dry toilet, and a waste bin for visitors' convenience. It's a great place for hikers to rest and enjoy the natural beauty of the area.

locations.title: Rohu Missile Base
locations.description: Kadila and Rohu missile bases were among the largest and most secret military objects in Estonia during the Soviet era, serving as launch sites for nuclear missiles with a range of 2000 km. Today, they are overgrown with forest, but remnants of the giant concrete hangars, launch pads, fuel depots, and barbed wire fences can still be seen. The bases were abandoned in the 1980s with the introduction of new missile technology.

locations.title: Emumäe Lookout Tower
locations.description: Emumägi is the highest peak in the Pandivere Highlands and Northern Estonia, with an elevation of 166.5 meters above sea level and 80 meters from the base. The mountain is home to an observation tower with 115 steps that leads to a height of 21.5 meters above ground level, offering visitors stunning panoramic views of the surrounding area for up to 30 kilometers on a clear day.

locations.title: Tudu Camping Hut
locations.description: Tudu Forest Hut is located in the picturesque Tudu bog lake in the deep forests of Alutaguse. The former hunting lodge provides a pleasant retreat and an excellent swimming spot in summer, equipped with a campfire site, grill rack, dry toilet, benches, and sleeping platforms. The trail leading to the hut is also accessible for wheelchair users and visually impaired individuals.

locations.title: Äntu Sinijärve Camping Ground
locations.description: The Äntu Sinijärv camping site is located 7 km from Väike-Maarja along Tartu Road, next to the Sinijärv Lake. The site features a small parking lot, a covered picnic area, benches, a fire pit with a grill, and a pyramid-shaped shelter for up to 7 people. Swimming is allowed only in Valgejärv Lake, and there is a nearby hiking trail with a viewing platform and access to Tehisjärv Lake.

locations.title: Emumäe Camping Ground
locations.description: A camping ground near the Emumäe hill with a sitting area and fireplaces.

locations.title: Altja Boulder
locations.description: A boulder on the Altja Hiking trail which is 4m tall and around 19m circumference.

locations.title: Punasoo Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the Seljamäe nature trail in Lääne-Virumaa. The site offers a dry toilet, covered seating area, and a pavilion for hikers to rest and prepare food while taking in the scenic views of the area's pine forests, bogs, and wildlife.

locations.title: Lepaaugu Camping Ground
locations.description: The campground is a popular destination for fishing and swimming during the spring and summer months, thanks to the small lake nearby. The site features two covered fire pits, a dry toilet, two covered picnic tables and a shelter for firewood.

locations.title: Nõva Beach
locations.description: This is a beautiful beach located in Nõva village. The sandy beach stretches for several kilometers and is surrounded by pine forests and coastal meadows. It's a popular spot for swimming, sunbathing, and picnicking during the summer months. The beach is relatively secluded and offers a peaceful and tranquil setting for visitors to enjoy.

locations.title: Peraküla Camping Ground
locations.description: Peraküla camping area in Läänemaa is located near the famous singing sands and clean sea of the Peraküla beach. The camping area has 16 covered fireplaces, 16 picnic tables, 4 covered picnic tables, 4 dry toilets (1 of them accessible for disabled people). The coastal sea is suitable for surfers and visitors can also enjoy a 4.4 km nature trail along the beach.

locations.title: Toatsejärve Rest-Stop
locations.description: Toatsejärve recreation area is located on the northern shore of a picturesque forest lake and is a favorite spot for early swimmers due to its warm water. It is also home to a protected leech species and rare plants.

locations.title: Allikjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Allikajärve is a secluded campfire site located on the beautiful shore of Allikajärve lake. The lake is known as "Dance Lake" and is popular among fishermen. However, the lake is not very suitable for swimming due to its muddy bottom. The campfire site includes one covered fire pit, one shelter with benches, an accessible dry toilet, a shelter for firewood, and a trash bin.

locations.title: Spitham Recon Station
locations.description: The reconnaissance station was built in 1915 and the main goal of the station was to locate German battleships and to intercept their communications. The location is in an average condition currently.

locations.title: Peraküla Beach
locations.description: The unspoiled surroundings and sparse population attract nature lovers, beachgoers and surfers. Peraküla beach offers several kilometers of clean sandy beach. The nearby Peraküla camping area provides fireplaces, dry toilets, and a parking lot for up to 200 cars, while the nearby forest is a great place to pick blueberries, cranberries, and mushrooms.

locations.title: Uuejõe Sauna Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the bend of Uuejõgi River, Uuejõe Sauna Lõkkekoht offers a private and serene environment with the nearby sea and diverse vegetation. There are two covered fire pits, a dry toilet, two picnic tables, and a shelter for firewood. The Liivanõmme study trail is also accessible from here.

locations.title: Uuejõe Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping site is a tranquil spot located on the shore of the overgrown Flyvae Lake and Uuejõe river, protected from sea winds. During spring and summer, the constant chirping of the reed warbler can be heard from the nearby reeds. The site offers a covered campfire area, a sheltered bench, a viewing platform, a dry toilet, and a shelter for firewood.

locations.title: Uuejõe Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located next to the Uuejõe Camping Ground, the 2 storey lookout tower provides a nice view over the nearby Flyvae lake that has been partially overgrown.

locations.title: Spitham Radar Station
locations.description: The radar station that was used by the Soviet Union is now in disrepair. Most of the structures are in a bad state but the radar hill itself is still an interesting location to visit.

locations.title: Põõsaspea Peninsula
locations.description: This location is the most northwestern point of mainland Estonia, not including any islands. The locations provides a wide view over the Gulf of Finland.

locations.title: Dirham Artillery Battery
locations.description: This is one of the few remnants of the time when Germany was occupying Estonia before the first World War. This used to be an artillery battery which was abandoned in 1917.

locations.title: Rooslepa Beach
locations.description: This is a quiet sandy beach on the west coast of Estonia with a shallow and partially rocky seabed.

locations.title: Veski Karjääri Resting Stop
locations.description: The location features former Soviet-era sand quarries that have been taken under protection with no mining activities taking place. The quarries are filled with water and serve as a habitat for the protected midwife toad. The surrounding area is also home to the regrowth of nature after a forest fire in 2008, and the site offers 30-meter high sand dunes from the time of the Litorina Sea.

locations.title: Veskijärve Camping Ground
locations.description: The camground next to the Lake Veskijärv, offers views of interesting landforms that were exposed after a forest fire in 2008. The camping ground has fireplaces, covered sitting areas and an outhouse. The lake is good for fishing, but it is not possible to fish from the shore. Visitors can also take a 4 km hike through the bog to Tänavjärve.

locations.title: Elbiku Beach
locations.description: Elbiku or Roosta beach is one of the best sandy beaches in Northwest Estonia. The beach has an 80-space car park, boardwalks for wheelchair users and dressing cabins.

locations.title: Valgejärve Viewing Platform
locations.description: The Valgejärve hiking trail leads to a six-meter-high observation tower located next to the lake, offering a beautiful view of the chalky eastern shore of the lake and the streams flowing into the lake.

locations.title: Suur-Nõmmküla Missile Base
locations.description: Located near the western coast, the Soviet era military base used to be one of the missile bases constructed for the cold war. Now the area is slowly decaying and most of it is in ruins.

locations.title: Saare Lookout Tower 1
locations.description: The tower is locafted next to the lake Sutlepa Meri. The lake is a nesting area for a lot of migratory birds and the tower provides a good lookout point for these species. The lake also has good fishing opportunities.

locations.title: Saare Lookout Tower 2
locations.description: The tower is locafted next to the lake Sutlepa Meri. The lake is a nesting area for a lot of migratory birds and the tower provides a good lookout point for these species. The lake also has good fishing opportunities.

locations.title: Marimetsa Lookout Tower
locations.description: In the middle of the Marimetsa bog is a 7,6m high lookout tower to provide a good view over the surrounding bog that is both rich in species and a diverse landscape. Here is also the end of the marimetsa hiking trail.

locations.title: Marimetsa Campfire Ground
locations.description: The campfire ground is located near the beautiful Marimetsa bog. The site does not have a lot to offer but it does have a covered sitting area and a fireplace for a nice picnic.

locations.title: Rannajõe Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located in an extensive floodplain, offers a unique experience during spring when the floodplain is submerged by the high tide. Thousands of geese, swans, and ducks stop here during their migration, while several waders and passerines nest in the area during summer. The four-meter-high observation tower and the 110 cm wide boardwalk leading to the viewing platform are also accessible to wheelchair users.

locations.title: Haeska Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located on the northern coast of the Matsalu Bay, which is known for its exceptional birdwatching opportunities. The tower offers the best view of the surrounding coastal meadows and the shallow bay, which serves as a favorite spot for migratory birds. The area is a popular destination for people interested in birdwatching, as it provides an opportunity to observe a diverse range of avian species in their natural habitat.

locations.title: Rohuküla Missile Base
locations.description: This former Soviet missile base now remains as mostly ruins but there should still be plenty to explore.

locations.title: Nõmme Missile Base
locations.description: This missile base was built in the year 1962 and was operating until the early 1990s. The base has been looted and the buildings are not in a good state but the area is worth a visit.

locations.title: Kiltsi Fuel Depot
locations.description: This used to be a fuel storage for the Kiltsi Airfield but now these buildings stand empty. The site is in an average condition and some of the structures have been destroyed.

locations.title: Ungru Castle Ruins
locations.description: Ungru manor, located in Kiltsi village, Estonia, is a neobaroque-style building that was built between 1893 and 1896 by Ewald von Ungern-Sternberg, modeled after the Merseburg castle in Germany. However, due to an unfortunate love story, the interior was never completed, and the building remained empty. It is now in ruins due to damage sustained during World War II.

locations.title: Kiltsi Former Airfield
locations.description: Built by the former Soviet Union army, covers an area of about 800 hectares with concrete runways that are 2500m long, and there used to be 28 hangars for planes. It was initially built in 1939 and used until 1956, then reconstructed in 1969. Since the departure of Soviet forces, the airfield has deteriorated, leaving behind old aircraft hangars, barracks, and runways. The airfield is now part of the Estonian Defense League's Western District. Note that permission is required to enter the airfield territory which can be optained from Lahe tänav 17 on workdays from 9-16.

locations.title: Kiltsi Manor Ruins
locations.description: Kiltsi Manor is a former manor located in Kiltsi village. The manor was built in the 18th century and was owned by several Baltic German families throughout its history. Today, the main building of the manor is in ruins, but several outbuildings, including a granary and a stable, have been restored and are used for various cultural events.

locations.title: Paralepa Beach
locations.description: Paralepa beach is one of the warmest sandy beaches in Estonia with outdoor toilets, changing cabins, a children's playground and a volleyball court.

locations.title: Haapsalu Castle
locations.description: Haapsalu Castle is a medieval castle located in the town of Haapsalu, Estonia. Originally built in the 13th century, the castle was used as a fortress until the 17th century and is now a popular tourist attraction and cultural center hosting various events and exhibits. It is considered to be one of the best-preserved medieval castles in Estonia.

locations.title: Tagalahe Lookout Tower
locations.description: Haapsalu Tagalaht is an exciting birdwatching spot for most of the year, and you don't need to leave the city to experience it. You can visit the 15-meter-high birdwatching tower located on the promenade, which provides a complete overview of bird life between Haapsalu and Noarootsi.

locations.title: Vasikaholmi Beach
locations.description: Vasikaholmi beach is Haapsalu's second official swimming spot in addition to Paralepa beach. The beach has dressing cabins, a toilet and clean water for swimming.

locations.title: Vormsi Lighthouse
locations.description: The lighthouse located on the island of Vormsi in Estonia. It was built in 1864 and is the oldest still-functioning cast iron lighthouse in the country. The lighthouse is 38 meters tall.

locations.title: Parunikivi
locations.description: Parunikivi is a protected natural landmark located in Hullo village, Vormsi parish. The rock, made of boulder clay, is covered with lichen and is also an important historical monument as it bears a German inscription in memory of Otto Friedrich Fromhold von Stackelberg.

locations.title: Muinasküla Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the Meiekose hiking trail at the confluence of the Tõramaa and Raudna rivers in Pärnu County. The site offers the opportunity to have a picnic, camp, and fish. It features an open fireplace with a grill, shelter for wood, a dry toilet, and a mowed area for camping.

locations.title: Meiekose Tamme Camping Hut
locations.description: The campground is located by the Raudna River under a large oak tree. Visitors can have a picnic, camp, and stay overnight in the forest hut. The hut includes a fireplace, grill grate, shelter, and dry toilet. However, there is no heating system in the forest hut.

locations.title: Meiekose Camping Ground
locations.description: Meiekose Camping Ground is located on the beautiful bank of the Raudna River in Pärnumaa, Estonia. It offers an open fireplace with a grill, a shelter for firewood, a dry toilet, and a picnic area with a canopy-covered table and benches. Visitors can enjoy a picnic and spend a longer time camping at the site.

locations.title: Tori-Jõesuu Bridge
locations.description: Jõesuu village in Pärnu County is named after the rivers, where Navesti River flows into Pärnu River. Jõesuu suspension bridge, which is the longest suspension bridge in Pärnu County, crosses over Pärnu River. The bridge is 67.5 meters long and was built in 1975.

locations.title: Tori Põrgu Cave
locations.description: Tori põrgu is a mysterious cave which was eroded over centuries by underground water in the Devonian sandstone cliff along the Pärnu river. The cave entrance was a 6-meter-wide opening, and it was said to have been 32 meters long. However, the cave collapsed in 1908, and the entrance in 1974. Today, visitors can no longer enter the cave, but stairs lead to the site for a closer view. The cave has been the subject of many legends and myths over the years.

locations.title: Riisa Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located in the middle of the Riisa bog, the 5-meters tall tower provides a great lookout point over the flat bog landscape. The lookout tower is located on the Riisa hiking trail.

locations.title: Kellissaare Camping Ground
locations.description: Kellissaare camping ground is located by a lake that formed in a former sand quarry. The site features two fireplaces, benches, a covered table-bench, shelter for firewood, a dry toilet, two trash cans, and offers a serene environment for nature enthusiasts to swim and enjoy the outdoors.

locations.title: Soontagana Lookout Tower
locations.description: The 15-meters tall lookout tower provides an excellent view over the surrounding bog scenery as well as the historic structures nearby. The tower is located on the Soontagana bog island which is home to ruins of two old farms with a history spanning over 22 generations, and the area is also rich in cultural heritage such as old homesteads, stone walls, and cellars.

locations.title: Soontagana Camping Ground
locations.description: Soontagana camping area is located on the Soontagana bog island which is home to ruins of two old farms with a history spanning over 22 generations, and the area is also rich in cultural heritage such as old homesteads, stone walls, and cellars. The camping area features a covered fireplace with a grill, shelter for firewood, forest hut, gazebo, toilets, observation tower, and a small boat.

locations.title: Rumba Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping site is a small spot located on the edge of a mixed spruce and pine forest, away from human settlements, in Pärnumaa. The campfire site has a covered fireplace with a grill, one sheltered table-bench, four low benches around the fire pit, a dry toilet, and a shelter for firewood. The RMK Penijõe-Aegviidu-Kauksi hiking trail passes by the site.

locations.title: Kloostri Lookout Tower
locations.description: The 16-meter tower offers views of the Kassari River floodplain, where flocks of ducks, geese, swans, and sandpipers stop during the spring floods, while storks, eagles, and sometimes elks and deer can be seen in the area during the summer.

locations.title: Penijõe Lookout Tower
locations.description: The Penijõe viewing tower in Pärnumaa offers a view of the 2,000-hectare reed bed that is one of the most interesting habitats in the Matsalu National Park, with the possibility of hearing and seeing various bird species. The tower is located on the left bank of Penijõe, on the edge of the reed bed.

locations.title: Suitsu Camping Hut
locations.description: The forest hut is located at the end of the Suitsu hiking trail in Pärnumaa. It is a restored hay barn that offers a good place for a small break to listen to birdsong or for overnight stays, with wooden benches inside, and the nearest dry toilet is located 700m away in the Suitsu recreation area.

locations.title: Suitsu Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located on the left bank of the Suitsu River in the Matsalu National Park in Pärnumaa. The tower is 21 meters high and offers a view of the unique Matsalu landscape, with its reed beds, coastal meadows, and Matsalu Bay on the horizon. The forest to the west of the tower was once a wooded meadow, which has now grown over.

locations.title: Keemu Lookout Tower
locations.description: The lookout tower is located on the southern coast of Matsalu Bay in Pärnumaa. The 8-meter-high tower provides a view of the central bay islands and the low inner bay covered with reeds and coastal meadows, offering excellent nesting opportunities for various bird species.

locations.title: Salevere Salumägi
locations.description: Salevere Salumägi is a highland that was formed by the continental glacier and the Baltic Sea. The steep limestone cliff on the seaside is around 6000-5000 years old, and the area used to be an island 4000-6000 years ago. Salevere Salumägi offers a panoramic view of the Topi Bay and Matsalu Bay estuary, and there is a Silmaallika spring at the foot of the cliff. The surrounding forest is home to a diverse range of plants and animals, including various bird species and snakes. Salevere Salumägi is a popular hiking destination and is especially beautiful during the spring bloom.

locations.title: Virtsu Manor
locations.description: Virtsu Manor was a powerful stone castle built on a narrow peninsula in the 15th century, which was destroyed in a battle in 1534. Later, a baroque-style manor was built on the mainland, including a wooden main house, a symmetrical complex with a walled French-style park, and various outbuildings. The manor was burned down by  in 1917 and only some of the outbuildings remain today.

locations.title: Vanaluubi Rest Area
locations.description: Located near Virtsu, right next to the sea which has a rocky seabed. The resting area has a covered sitting area, a grill and an observation tower. Good for a picnic or a rest stop.

locations.title: Puhtu Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located on the southern shore of the nature filled Puhtulaid, the 5-meter lookout tower provides a wonderful view over the sea and the surrounding area.

locations.title: Tuhu Bog Lookout Tower
locations.description: The 5-meter tall unique viewing tower called TREPP, which was created in collaboration with the Estonian Academy of Arts. The tower offers visitors a panoramic view of the bog landscape, while a short boardwalk trail leads through the different stages of bog development.

locations.title: Kaubaru Camping Hut
locations.description: Located in the Nigula nature reserve in Pärnumaa. The camping hut provides accommodation for hikers who want to explore the surrounding area. The simplistic cabin includes four sleeping platforms, a fireplace, a table-bench set, a well, an outdoor toilet and a woodshed. The hut is free to use for anyone.

locations.title: Nedrema Puisniidu Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground offers visitors an opportunity to experience the beauty and biodiversity of the natural landscape, including many protected plant species. The site provides covered fireplace, dry toilet, three tables with benches, and firewood storage.

locations.title: Matsiranna Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping area is located in the western part of Pärnu County and is famous for its natural and beautiful sandy Matsi beach. The camping area has covered tables and benches, covered fireplaces, and toilets, but campfires must be made using personal firewood. The beach is surrounded by a beautiful pine forest and there are plenty of meadows and juniper groves in the area.

locations.title: Sutiranna Beach
locations.description: This is a small grass covered beach with a stone filled shoreline. The beach has a clothing booth and a sitting area.

locations.title: Kavaru Lookout Platform
locations.description: Located next to the Kavaru port in the Pärnu bay area, the platform provides a nice picturesque view over the surrounding area inspite of it's small elevation.

locations.title: Liu Missile Base
locations.description: The former missile base located near the northern shore of the Pärnu bay, provides a look into how well these types of structures fare with time. Most of the buildings are in ruins and there is a lot of overgrowth.

locations.title: Lindi Lookout Tower
locations.description: Lindi Lookout tower, located near the Pärnu-Tõstamaa highway, provides a view of the Lindi nature reserve. The reserve is home to Lindi bog, which has several small picturesque lakes, diverse plant and bird species, and over 20 different types of forests. The tower also has a covered rest area for visitors to take a break.

locations.title: Valgeranna Lookout Tower
locations.description: Valgeranna lookout tower is a 9-meter high circular tower with three viewing platforms. From the top of the tower, visitors can admire the beautiful natural surroundings, watch swimmers, and observe adventurers in the nearby adventure park. The tower is easily accessible via a beautiful promenade leading directly to the seaside.

locations.title: Valgeranna Beach
locations.description: Valgerand is a popular beach and recreational area and it is named after its long white sandy shoreline that stretches towards the south. The beach can be accessed through a lit promenade, and a viewing tower is also located in the recreational area.

locations.title: Vana-Pärnu Lookout Tower
locations.description: The 8-meter-high birdwatching tower is located on the border of Vana-Pärnu meadow, between the river and the sea, offering great views of birds in the coastal reedbeds, meadows, and the sea. Throughout the year, visitors can spot over 100 different bird species. The tower also provides a fascinating view of the Pärnu River estuary, the piers, and the long sandy beach.

locations.title: Pärnu Beach
locations.description: Pärnu rand is a popular beach with its soft sand, shallow water, and numerous amenities, Pärnu beach is a beloved destination for both locals and tourists. The beach stretches for nearly 4 kilometers along the Pärnu Bay, offering plenty of space for sunbathing, swimming, and other water activities.

locations.title: May Lookout Tower
locations.description: The two birdwatching towers (May and Raeküla) built on the Pärnu beach meadow near the sea are great places to observe birds and admire the beach meadow and the sea. Both towers are unique rectangular structures made of strong wooden lattices, which prevent them from swaying during birdwatching. The 8-meter high towers have information stands with drawings of birds and protected plant species in the beach meadow.

locations.title: Raeküla Lookout Tower
locations.description: The two birdwatching towers (May and Raeküla) built on the Pärnu beach meadow near the sea are great places to observe birds and admire the beach meadow and the sea. Both towers are unique rectangular structures made of strong wooden lattices, which prevent them from swaying during birdwatching. The 8-meter high towers have information stands with drawings of birds and protected plant species in the beach meadow.

locations.title: Reiu Beach
locations.description: Reiu Beach, located about 10 km from the city of Pärnu, is a popular destination for summer vacationers. The beach features warm and shallow water, making it ideal for swimming and picnicking. Visitors can reach the beach from Pärnu via a well-lit pedestrian and bike path as well as a car.

locations.title: Katku Camping Ground
locations.description: This is a good stopping point located on the edge of large coniferous forests and the protected Katkusoo (Rüütli bog) in Pärnumaa, where with some luck, you can even spot a capercaillie. It's an opportunity to rest, make a fire, or have a meal. The site features a covered fireplace with a grill, benches, a dry toilet, an information board, and a firewood stand.

locations.title: Kopra Camping Ground
locations.description: The Kopra campfire site is located on a small island in the middle of a drainage ditch, which can be accessed by crossing a bridge. It is a suitable place for hikers to take a break or stay overnight. It is located on the RMK Oandu-Ikla hiking trail, and the Kopra-Ikla (74 km) section of the Pärnumaa Recreation Area starts from there. The site has a covered fire pit with a grill, a table and bench, and a woodshed.

locations.title: Allikukivi Caves
locations.description: The Allikukivi Caves are located on the slope of the Allikukivi ancient valley near the town of Kilingi-Nõmme in Estonia. These large underground caves were discovered in 1961, and were carved into the sandstone by the flow of groundwater. The cave system is 33 meters long and up to 2.7 meters high. At the bottom of the caves, groundwater accumulates and emerges as a small spring at the foot of the valley. Visitors are encouraged to bring a flashlight to explore this mysterious place. According to legend, a wealthy farm once stood on this spot, but it sank into the ground as punishment for the family's failure to give alms to a beggar during a wedding celebration.

locations.title: Kilingi-Nõmme Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping site is located along the RMK Oandu-Ikla hiking trail and the Kilingi-Nõmme forest trail, offering hikers a great opportunity to rest and prepare a meal over a fire. The wooden sheltered fireplace provides shelter in case of rainy weather, and there are benches on the edges of the shelter. Additionally, there is a woodshed and a trash can on the site.

locations.title: Rae Lake Lookout Tower
locations.description: The lookout tower is located next to the Rae lake and it is easy to reach from the Rae Lake hiking trail. The modest tower provides a nice view over the nature around.

locations.title: Rae Lake Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated by the Rae Lake, offering visitors a chance to enjoy a peaceful forest retreat and engage in activities such as swimming, fishing or hiking on the Rae Lake hiking trail. The camping area features amenities such as a swimming pier, covered fireplaces with grill racks, picnic tables with roofs, firewood sheds, outdoor toilets, a water well, and a covered shelter. It also serves as a good stopover point for those hiking along the RMK Oandu-Ikla hiking trail.

locations.title: Laiksaare Metsaonn
locations.description: Laiksaare camping hut is located on the Laiksaare nature trail, which introduces visitors to the forested wetlands of Pärnumaa. It's an excellent spot for bird watching and listening to birdsong while on the trail. The hut also serves as an overnight accommodation for hikers on the RMK hiking trail between Kopra and Ikla (74 km). It features a covered fire pit, three sleeping and seating platforms, and an outdoor bench.

locations.title: Rannametsa Lookout Tower
locations.description: The Rannametsa hiking trail leads through a bright pine forest and reaches Tornimägi, where an 18-meter high observation tower offers stunning views of the Häädemeeste beach and the Gulf of Pärnu on one side, and the Tolkuse bog on the other. From Tornimägi, the trail descends to the Tolkuse bog.

locations.title: Rannametsa Dunes
locations.description: Rannametsa dunes are the highest dunes in Estonia, located in Pärnu county in western Estonia, between the Baltic Sea and Tolkuse bog. The dunes include Tõotusemägi (40 meters above sea level), Tornimägi (34 meters), and Varsamägi.

locations.title: Tõotusemäe Camping Ground
locations.description: This camping ground is located in the Luitemaa Nature Reserve in Pärnumaa. Near the site is the Rannametsa-Tolkuse nature study trail, which introduces visitors to the coastal dune landscape and bog areas. The site features a covered fireplace with a grill grate, two wooden benches, a shelter for firewood, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Priivitsa Camping Ground
locations.description: This campground in Pärnumaa is an ideal spot for camping and beach holiday. Located near Priivitsa stream, the sea shore, and Kabli nature trail, it offers covered seating areas, a covered fire pit with a grill, firewood storage, and a toilet.

locations.title: Kabli Lookout Tower
locations.description: A lookout lookout tower near the beach next to a bird research station. You can witness a lot of different bird species here during the right season and there is a study trail starting from near the tower.

locations.title: Kabli Beach
locations.description: The beach features a meandering promenade along the sandy shoreline and beach meadow, as well as picnic areas and a playground. In addition to beach recreation, Kabli offers a change of scenery for nature lovers, with Kabli Bird Station and the Kabli Nature Study Trail located just a few hundred meters from the beach. There are also nearby camping areas for those who wish to stay longer.

locations.title: Lemme Camping Ground
locations.description: Lemme camping area is a popular place for vacationers, both locals and foreigners, located by the sea and surrounded by a pine forest in the Pärnumaa recreational area. The area offers campsites, covered picnic tables, a well, and toilets.

locations.title: Mehikoorma Church Ruins
locations.description: A quarry-stone and brick church built in 1896, now in ruins since 1944. The old tower is still in pretty good shape and offers an interesting view.

locations.title: Valgesoo Lookout Tower
locations.description: This 24-meters tall lookout tower provides an incredible view over the beautiful Valgesoo bog. The Valgesoo hiking trail starts from here as well.

locations.title: Tuurapera Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated on the bank of the Võhandu River, it has a table with shelter, woodshed, a fire ring with barbecue grill, an outdoor fireplace, and dry toilet. There are bountiful mushroom picking forests all around.

locations.title: Kaljupealse Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the banks of the Võhandu River, in a beautiful pine forest, it offers a beautiful view of the Võhandu River and sand outcrops. It has a roofed table and benches, a roofed grill, and firewood. Nearby is an unmarked hiking trail where you can enjoy beautiful nature or pick berries and mushrooms.

locations.title: Uku Cavern
locations.description: Located in the landscape reserve of the Võhandu River Valley, it is a sandstone cave next to Sõjatare Cave and Väike-Sõjatare Cave. According to legend, the mother of the devil used to knit in the cave. Sõjatare is said to be an ancient sanctuary. Behind it, there is a tunnel with an iron door, where two devils come out once a year.

locations.title: Valgjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated at Lake Valgjärve, it has an outdoor fire place, woodshed (equipped with firewood), and dry toilet. It is located on the RMK Perakülä-Aegviidu-Ähijärve Hiking Route.

locations.title: Liipsaare Lookout Tower
locations.description: An 11 m high tower providing a wonderful view of the Meenikunno bog landscape. There is an outdoor fireplace near the tower, and it is located on the Meenikunno hiking trail.
