locations.title: Liipsaare Camping Hut
locations.description: Situated near the Meenikunno bog and hiking trail, the hut has a stove and bunk beds, a fire ring with a barbecue grill, a picnic table, woodshed, dry toilet, and the Liipsaare Lookout Tower is also nearby.

locations.title: Ootsipalu Valley Giants
locations.description: The location for many grand and magnificent trees, including the tallest spruce (48.6 m) and pine (46.6 m) in Estonia. They are quite close to each other and are more than 200 years old, and from 2016, the pine tree is actually the tallest pine in the world.

locations.title: Mustjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated at Lake Mustjärve, it has two fire rings with barbecue grills, two outdoor fireplaces, three picnic tables with shelter, a woodshed, and a dry toilet. Water can be taken from the lake, and the firewood is provided. Nearby is the Meenikunno hiking trail. Tent camping is allowed only in a designated enclosed area.

locations.title: Leevi Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the bank of the Võhandu River, it has a fire ring with barbecue grill, picnic table with shelter, woodshed with a sleeping loft, and dry toilet. The water can be taken from the Võhandu river, and firewood is provided. The site is on the RMK Peraküla-Aegviidu-Ähijärve Hiking Route.

locations.title: Koolmajärve Campfire Site
locations.description: Situated at The Lake Koolmajärve in a pine forest, the campground includes a fire ring with a grill, firewood shelter, and dry toilet. It has been stated that tents are not allowed at this site.

locations.title: Kooraste Suurjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Located in the Kiidjärve-Kooraste Recreation Area and Kooraste cycling trail, the camping ground has an open fire pit with grill grate, table-bench with roof, benches, shelter of firewood and dry toilet. In the vicinity you can find many mushrooms and bilberries.

locations.title: Kooraste Kõverjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: A nice lakeside site on the Kooraste cycling trail, where you can catch fish, make a campfire and camp. There is an outdoor fireplace, a fire ring with a grill, benches, and a dry toilet. Firewood is provided. Nearby you can find bilberries and mushrooms.

locations.title: Jõksi Lake Camping Ground
locations.description: A nice lakeside spot very close to the small town of Kanepi, with an outdoor fireplace with benches and firewood shelter, picnic table with shelter and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Erastvere Park Camping Ground
locations.description: Located by Lake Kiidjärve and the 38 km Kooraste cycling trail, the camping ground has two covered campfire sites with benches, a shelter for firewood and a dry toilet. You can walk around the park and discover the graveyard of the Ungern-Sternberg family, founded on the hill in the Erastvere oak stand in 1883, which is maintained in good condition. The old red brick building at the lake is used as a place of worship.

locations.title: Palojärve Camping Ground 1
locations.description: Located on the shore of the lake Palo in a pine forest, the roads and trails surrounding the camping area are also suitable for longer walks. It includes seven outdoor fireplaces with benches and firewood, tables with a shelter, two woodsheds (one with loft sleeping possibility), two dry toilets.

locations.title: Palojärve Camping Ground 2
locations.description: The camping ground is located on the shore of Lake palo in a pine forests, which are perfect for easy walks. There are two outdoor fireplaces, a table with a shelter, benches, a woodshed, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Voorepalu Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the historic Tartu-Pihkva highway, it is possible to make campfire, grill, camp and spend the night in a wooden barn. Across the road, in an extremely varied landscape, runs the 0.5 km long Voorepalu Põrgurada trail, which is suitable for a pleasant walk. The surrounding beautiful forests also offer good opportunities for walking on forest paths, picking berries and mushrooms in the autumn.

locations.title: Ahja Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is a rest stop on the hiking route near Ahja-Võnnu Road and has an outdoor fireplace, table and benches, firewood shelter (firewood is provided) and a dry toilet. There is no water nearby.

locations.title: Laari Camping Ground
locations.description: Located in a pine forest by Tartu-Põlva road, offering a nice place to stay for forest recreation. The camping ground has an outdoor fireplace, benches, a table with shelter, a dry toilet, and a woodshed, and it is where the Laari forestry study trail starts. Other hiking trails close by are Kiidjärve Wood Ants Trail and Taevaskodade hiking trail.

locations.title: Kiidjärve Beach
locations.description: Kiidjärve Beach has a sandy beach and two swimming bridges. The Kiidjärve Manor Park, which has buildings from the second half of the 18th century, is located nearby. Although the manor house was destroyed in a fire in 1950, some smaller buildings have been preserved. Kiidjärve Water Mill, Roiupalu study trail, Valgesoo bog study trail and watchtower, and Large Taevaskoda sandstone outcrop are all within 3.5 km of the beach.

locations.title: Kiidjärve Water Mill
locations.description: Kiidjärve Water Mill used to be one of the largest and most powerful mills in Estonia, and it was built in 1914. The saw mill, which formed a complex with the flour mill and was destroyed in a fire in 1962, was located on the other side of the Ahja River. The mill was a part of the historic Kiidjärve Manor and operated until 1979. Nowadays, the mill is privately owned and can be viewed from the outside only.

locations.title: Sõnajala Camping Ground
locations.description: Located at the Sõnajala sandstone outcrop and offers a wonderful view of the Ahja river. The camping ground has an outdoor fireplace, table with shelter, a woodshed (firewood provided), a dry toilet, and railings over the steep slopes. The RMK Kiidjärve Visitor Centre is the closest place to get drinking water, located 0.65 km away. Sõnajala Camping Ground is located on the Kiidjärve-Taevaskoja-Kiidjärve hiking trail and Peraküla-Aegviidu-Ähijärve hiking route, and the Roiupalu study trail is located on the opposite side of the river.

locations.title: Metsavendade Bunker
locations.description: Located 400 metres south of Suur Taevaskoda Cliff and is the location of the last bunker of Jaan Roots, the notorious leader of Forest Brothers in Võrumaa, and his group of followers. Nowadays, only a large hole remains where the bunker once was.

locations.title: Oosemägi Cavern
locations.description: Located in the land protection area of the primeval valley of the Ahja river, and it is in good condition despite the lower 4 meters remaining under the water of the dam lake. It is mostly visited by boat tourists, and on the opposite bank of Oosemägi Cavern, there is a campfire site with a beautiful view of the outcrop. It is one of the biggest caves in the region, but it has poor access.

locations.title: Saesaare Hydropower Plant
locations.description: Located on the Ahja River, and it was built in 1949.

locations.title: Taevaskoja Neitsikoobas
locations.description:
Taevaskoja Neitsikoobas (Virgin Cave) is a large cave in the Väike Taevaskoda Cliff created by water from the springs. It is called Neitsikoobas (Virgin Cave) and is over 3 metres high at the outlet. The spring that flows out of this cave yields approximately 2.5 litres of water per second. Historians believe that the cliffs have been named Heaven’s Hall (Taevaskoda) because Estonians believing in the Taara God used to hold their meetings there. The legend however says that the Vanapagan (Old Devil) had been so
enchanted by the place’s beauty, that he had built various rooms underground here and named
them the Heaven’s Halls.

locations.title: Taevaskoja Emaläte
locations.description: From the depths of the cliff cave originates this spring with its cold and clear water. The average streaming volume is between 4 and 4.5 litres per second. Emaläte is the largest Taevaskoja spring and probably feeds the other springs in the area, hence the motherly reference.

locations.title: Salakuulaja Kivi
locations.description: Salakuulaja kivi (Secret Listener's Boulder) is big boulder in the river in Taevaskoja. According to legend, this boulder is a secret listener, who snuck into the river to spy on a war council and was turned to stone because of the cold river water.

locations.title: Suur Taevaskoda
locations.description: The sandstone outcrops and the ancient river form the most beautiful part of the Ahja River landscape reserve. Legends speak of caves, secret passageways and of demons. A walk from the Saesaare dam to the Small Taevaskoda outcrop, the Emaläte spring, the Neitsikoobas cave and the Large Taevaskoda outcrop, which rises 22.5 metres above the river, is worth undertaking in every season. One of the most beautiful hiking experiences available in Estonia.

locations.title: Laho Camping Hut
locations.description: Located on the shore of Lake Laho, the forest hut has no heating, but has a fire ring with barbecue grill, picnic table with shelter, woodshed and dry toilet.

locations.title: Pahkla Military Base
locations.description: Pahkla Missile Base was a missile base for air defense rockets, located near Pahkla village. It was built in 1973 and had four command centers, each with three launchers for S-200 missiles and two radar stations. The base covered approximately 195 hectares and had barracks, housing, and technical facilities. Several buildings have been demolished and some equipment has been removed.

locations.title: Kõnnu Lake
locations.description: Kõnnu Lake is a circular lake located in the Seli or Kõnnu bog in Rabivere landscape protection area. It is a typical bog lake with a water surface of approximately 4 ha and a depth of around 4 m. The lake is accessible via a trail that is part of the RMK Penijõe-Aegviidu-Kauksi hiking trail.

locations.title: Varbola Camping Ground
locations.description: Varbola camping area is located near the ruins of a castle in Raplamaa. The area offers several shelters, open fireplaces with grills, covered fireplaces, water supply (from a well), and 4 covered picnic tables with benches.

locations.title: Sooniste Manor
locations.description: Sooniste Manor was built in the 17th century and was owned by the von Mohrenschildt family in the early 20th century. The current neoclassical appearance of the main building dates back to its reconstruction after a fire in 1905. The manor is currently privately owned, and some of the surrounding buildings have been preserved or renovated, while others are in ruins.

locations.title: Õmma Camping Hut
locations.description: Õmma forest cabin is a secluded cabin with a viewing tower located in the midst of wild nature. There are no marked paths leading to the cabin, making it a true adventure for nature enthusiasts. The cabin features a dry toilet and is ideal for those seeking an immersive nature experience.

locations.title: Maidla Manor
locations.description: Maidla Manor is one of five Estonian manors with the same name, founded in 1631. The small baroque one-story building with a semi-circular roof was constructed in the late 18th century, later modified with classical elements in the 19th century. It was later used as a school until a fire in 1985 left it in ruins, with only the walls remaining.

locations.title: Mukri Bog Lookout Tower
locations.description: The Mukri bog hiking trail features an 18-meter high observation tower located next to Lake Eidapere that provides a wonderful view of the entire bog.

locations.title: Mukri Camping Ground
locations.description: Mukri Camping Ground is located on the picturesque edge of Mukri bog at the beginning of the Mukri nature trail. The site features an open fire pit with a grill, a shelter with a table and two benches, a dry toilet, a trash bin, and three additional benches for seating.

locations.title: Järvakandi Manor Ruins
locations.description: The Järvakandi Manor in Estonia was built in the 1770s and underwent a major renovation in the 1820s to become one of the finest examples of neoclassical architecture in the country. Despite several subsequent renovations and restorations, the manor house now lies in ruins, with only the overgrown walls remaining.

locations.title: Loosalu Camping Ground
locations.description: The Loosalu campfire site is located in the northeastern corner of the Loosalu bog. The site is situated by the Loosalu-Paluküla study trail, which leads onto the bog. The site features a large shelter with benches and tables, an open fire pit, seating, a wood shelter, a dry toilet, a garbage bin, and an information board.

locations.title: Pahkla Boulder
locations.description: Pahkla Suurkivi is a giant boulder made of bogstone in Rapla County, Kohila Parish, Estonia. The rock is rectangular with almost vertical sides and measures 10.1 meters in length, 9.7 meters in width, and 4.3 meters in height. The origin of the rock's name remains unknown, but it is said that a Pahkla estate owner used to organize dance parties on top of the boulder for his guests.

locations.title: Rabivere Camping Ground
locations.description: The Rabivere Campfire Site is a simple camping area in the forest near a hiking trail. It has a shelter with tables and benches, a covered fireplace with a grill, a dry toilet, a shelter for firewood, and a trash can.

locations.title: Haimre Park Pavilion
locations.description: In the park of Haimre Manor, by the pond, stands a neo-Gothic style pavilion known as the Mohammedan Chapel, built in the late 18th century reportedly by Alexander Uexküll, son of the manor owner, for his Muslim lover.

locations.title: Tillniidu Camping Ground
locations.description: This camping site is a small and secluded spot in the forests of Raplamaa, Estonia. Visitors must bring their own water as there is no water source nearby. The site features an open fireplace with benches, a sheltered area with tables and benches, a shelter for firewood, a dry toilet, and a trash can. The campfire site is located along the RMK Oandu-Ikla hiking trail.

locations.title: Üügu Cliff
locations.description: Üügu Cliff is a 300m long and impressive cliff located on the northeast coast of Muhu Island in Estonia. Despite being slightly away from the current coastline, it was strongly eroded and has large cave-like indentations in the rock. It is considered the largest and most beautiful of the cliffs in Muhu.

locations.title: Piiri Missile Base
locations.description: Piiri Missile Base was a Soviet military base located in Piiri state forest on the island of Muhu. It was built in 1963 and had a missile division with officers and soldiers, electric power station, shelters, launch pads, and storage for S-75 missiles and nuclear warheads. Today, the site still has remnants of the missile storage, garages, command post ruins, launch pads, and bunkers.

locations.title: Kübassaare Lookout Tower
locations.description: The Kübassaare Lookout Tower is a tall metal tower that provides a view of the surrounding area and a little bit of sea.

locations.title: Maasi Castle Ruins
locations.description: Maasi Castle was built in the Middle Ages as a stronghold by Saaremaa islanders under duress from the Livonian Order. It became a secure fortress until it was destroyed by the Danes. Today, the 8-meter-high walls that survived the destruction are a popular sightseeing attraction.

locations.title: Audla Manor Ruins
locations.description: Audla Manor was first owned by Jacob Swart in 1464 and later by the Vietinghoff and Buhrmeister families until it was expropriated in 1919. The manor house, built in 1805, is a one-story classical building with a high first floor and three oval rooms, and is currently in private ownership and in a state of disrepair.

locations.title: Koigi Bog Lookout Tower
locations.description: A 9-meter high observation tower located near the trail of Saaremaa's largest bog provides a beautiful view of the Koigi bog.

locations.title: Kallemäe Missile Base
locations.description: Kallemäe S-75 Missile division was stationed there in 1962 and the base was likely built in 1959. In 1970, additional land was allocated to the unit and a nuclear missile facility called "Granit" was likely built there. The base was dissolved in 1991 and later taken over by the Estonian Defense League in 1993. Since then, the area has mainly been subjected to looting and vandalism.

locations.title: Laidunina Lighthouse
locations.description:
The Laidunina Lighthouse is a former lighthouse and landmark located on the Kahtla islet in Saaremaa.
The height of the red brick tower from the base is 24 meters (79 feet) and from sea level is 27 meters (89 feet). It was built in 1907 and was used as a lighthouse until 1924.

locations.title: Koigi Bog Viewing Platform
locations.description: The Koigi Bog Viewing Platform is situated in the scenic Koigi Bog, on the shore of Lake Pitkjärv. It offers a great vantage point to view the lake and is a part of the wooden hiking trail that winds through the bog.

locations.title: Triigi Camping Ground
locations.description: Triigi camping area is a seaside camping area located near Triigi Harbor, where ferries travel between Saaremaa and Hiiumaa. It has 4 covered fireplaces, 4 roofed bench tables, an information board, 2 dry toilets, and 2 shelters for firewood.

locations.title: Meiuste Camping Ground
locations.description: Meiuste camping area is located in a beautiful pine forest on the Upsu Bay coast in Saaremaa. When looking towards the sea, Hiiumaa can be seen on the left and the Triigi Peninsula on the right. The camping area has 11 covered fireplaces, 11 picnic tables with roofs, 4 double dry toilets, including 1 wheelchair accessible toilet, 4 woodsheds, and 4 garbage sheds.

locations.title: Soela Camping Ground
locations.description: Soela Camping Ground is situated near the larger Meiuse camping area, surrounded by a beautiful pine forest on the Upsu Bay coast in Saaremaa. It offers covered fireplaces, a picnic table with a roof, and a dry toilet for camping enthusiasts.

locations.title: Tuhkana Beach
locations.description: Tuhkana Beach on the north coast of Saaremaa and is known for its wide sandy shore and deep water. It's a good spot for swimming and experiencing waves during windy days. The beach also has a volleyball court and nearby forests for mushroom and berry picking.

locations.title: Tuhkana Camping Ground
locations.description: Tuhkana Camping Area is a quiet resting place near the picturesque and water-lily-rich Tuhkana Lake in Saaremaa. The camping area is located behind a beautiful white sand dune with swimming opportunities just 300 meters away. The surroundings offer good berry and mushroom forests. The campsite has an information board, a picnic table, a dry toilet, and two covered fireplaces with a roof, as well as a woodshed.

locations.title: Valjala Hill Fort Ruins
locations.description: This was a circular ring fort located in Saare County in the village of Valjala. It was built on a natural hill that was approximately 5-8 meters high. Valjala maalinn was the strongest fortress of the ancient Saaremaa people and was not conquered during the siege of 1227 by the 20,000-strong crusader army. The hill fort's defense relied mainly on stone walls, and its construction dates back approximately 800 years.

locations.title: Kaali Meteor Crater
locations.description:
The Kaali meteorite impact occurred around 7500-7600 years ago on the already inhabited
island of Saaremaa, Estonia. The meteorite caused massive destruction, with the largest
fragment creating a 110-meter-wide and 22-meter-deep crater and eight smaller craters. The
area surrounding the Kaali lake was later enclosed with a massive stone wall, and the
discovery of numerous animal bones suggests it was an ancient sacrificial site.

locations.title: Panga Cliff
locations.description:
Panga cliff is the highest mainland cliff in Western Estonia and the islands. It is an extensive
Silurian outcrop that originates from Gotland Island in Sweden, runs through the Baltic Sea
and rises to the surface on the western coast of Saaremaa. The maximum height of the cliff
is 21.3 m and its length is about 2.5 km. Panga cliff rises like an exposed wall directly from
the beach, and there is an ancient sacrificial site at the highest point where offerings were
made to the sea in the past.

locations.title: Jaagarahu Port
locations.description:
Jaagarahu harbor was a harbor located in the western part of Saaremaa near Kihelkonna
Bay in Jaagarahu. It was established in the 1920s and was used to export limestone from a
nearby quarry. During World War II, the harbor was used to deport Saaremaa islanders. The
harbor was destroyed in 1969 after a severe storm.

locations.title: Tammese Missile Base
locations.description:
Tammese Missile Base is a former military site located in the western side of Saaremaa,
Estonia. It was used as a storage and maintenance facility for S-125 missiles during the
Soviet era. Today, the base is abandoned and offers a unique opportunity for urban
exploration, as it contains a variety of structures, including bunkers, launch pads, and other
military installations.

locations.title: Kiipsaare Lighthouse
locations.description:
The lighthouse was completed in 1933 and is 26 meters high. Due to the changing coastline,
the lighthouse is now located in the sea and is an interesting sight to see. Accessible only by foot, the journey from the closest
parking lot to the lighthouse is about 5 km.

locations.title: Kuriku Cliff
locations.description:
This is a steep limestone cliff formation on the northwestern coast of Saaremaa island,
Estonia, known for its unique and picturesque landscape. The cliff, which is about 10 meters
high and 1.3 km long, is a popular tourist attraction.

locations.title: Undva Cliff
locations.description:
Undva Cliff is located at the northern tip of the Tagamõisa peninsula in Saaremaa. The bank
is about 350 meters long and its height is 2.5 meters. The bank is not very tall but it provides
a scenic view.

locations.title: Harilaiu Camping Ground
locations.description:
Harilaiu peninsula is a part of the Vilsandi National Park located on the northwest coast of
Saaremaa. It features beautiful sandy and pebble beaches and dunes, and the constantly
changing coastline shaped by the sea. The camping area is located by Laialepa Lake, which
is accessible via a 4 km hiking trail, and has basic facilities such as a woodshed, a covered
fireplace, a bench-table, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Ninase Lookout Tower
locations.description:
Ninase cliff is located on the northern coast of Saaremaa and is known for its stunning
natural scenery. Visitors can admire the nature from the Ninase observation tower, which is
located near the parking lot.

locations.title: Tagaranna Missile Base
locations.description:
The former Soviet military complex in Tagaranna village, Saaremaa island, housed a S-125
surface-to-air missile division which was formed in 1964. The complex consisted of several
buildings and missile launch positions and was in use until 1992 when the brigade was
disbanded. Currently, the buildings are unused and have fallen into disrepair, and the missile
launch positions have been used as quarries.

locations.title: Ninase Defense Battery
locations.description:
The Ninase Defence Battery was a 6-inch artillery battery built in 1917 in Tagaranna
peninsula, Saaremaa. It was quickly constructed and untested before the German invasion
in 1917. The battery provided weak resistance and surrendered. Today, its gun positions are
well preserved and accessible.

locations.title: Tagaranna Coast Guard Station
locations.description:
Tagaranna Coast Guard Station was a military installation located in Tagaranna village in
Estonia, built in the early 1980s during the Soviet era. It served as a radar station,
observation tower, barracks, and other facilities for the Soviet Coast Guard. The station was
named after Viktor Kingissepp. Following Estonia's restoration of independence in 1991, the
Tagaranna Coast Guard Station was abandoned.

locations.title: Undva Radar Station
locations.description:
Undva is located in the north-eastern part of the Tagamõisa peninsula in Saaremaa. In
August 1940, the Soviet authorities and local residents began building military base No. 32.
After World War II, the Soviet military built two 127mm artillery batteries there, one near
Suuriku cliff and the other at Undva point. The latter was later replaced with a newer coastal
defense battery, followed by the construction of a radio location station in the 1960s. Today,
four well-preserved concrete gun platforms, concrete hangars, and a building complex
remaining from various facilities still exist.

locations.title: Tiirimetsa Church Ruins
locations.description:
Tiirimetsa Church is a five-dome limestone church built in 1873. It was constructed by
Aleksei Kann and is one of 16 Orthodox churches built in Livonia and one in Estonia based
on the same design. The church was looted by Soviet soldiers in 1944 and left in ruins.

locations.title: Raketi Camping Ground
locations.description:
The former military territory of Karujärve has been turned into a sports and leisure area in
Saaremaa, Estonia. The area offers hiking and camping opportunities in the summer and
skiing, sledding, and other winter activities in the winter. The rocket camping area provides
two picnic tables, a dry toilet, a covered fireplace, and a woodshed for visitors.

locations.title: Odalätsi Spring
locations.description: Odalätsi Spring is a natural spring located in the midst of a forested dune and pine grove area in Saaremaa. The name "Odalätsi" comes from a legend that Vanapagan broke his spear there during a fight with Tõll and exclaimed "Oda läts!" ("The spear is broken!"). A 400-meter nature trail surrounds the springs, which are believed to have formed from the footsteps of Vanapagan.

locations.title: Karujärve Beach
locations.description:
Karujärv is a lake located in Saaremaa. According to a local legend, the lake got its name
from a fight between seven bears. The lake has five islands and a sandy beach which is
popular for swimming and camping during the summer. It is also the oldest lake in
Saaremaa, approximately 8000 years old, with a protected area of about 330 hectares and a
maximum depth of 6 meters.

locations.title: Oriküla Missile Base
locations.description:
Formerly a Soviet rocket base, Oriküla Command Center is now a collection of abandoned
buildings located in Karida village, 13 km from Kuressaare. The center was built in 1976 and
was intended to oversee rocket divisions in Saaremaa, Muhumaa, and partially in the Pärnu
region in the event of war. Today, the center consists of a concrete control center, garage,
radar hill, barracks ruins, and other unknown buildings.

locations.title: Rauna Lookout Tower
locations.description:
Rauna observation tower offers a view of the forested area and the western coast of
Saaremaa. It is 26 meters high and requires climbing 80 stairs to reach the top. On clear
days, it offers views of Vilsandi lighthouse, Kihelkonna church tower, and even Kuressaare
Castle, Sõrve Peninsula, and Kõpu lighthouse.

locations.title: Sõrve Lighthouse
locations.description:
The Sõrve lighthouse is located on the tip of the Sõrve Peninsula in Estonia. It was originally
built on a small island in 1646 but was later moved to the peninsula due to storms. The
current concrete lighthouse was erected in 1960 and stands at 52 meters tall. Visitors are
able to climb to the top of the lighthouse during the summer months.

locations.title: Stebeli Battery
locations.description:
During World War II, several battles took place in Sõrve between German and Soviet forces.
The road to the most powerful and modern coastal defense battery of the Soviet Armed
Forces on Saaremaa, the Stebeli battery, turns off from the Sääre-Kaugatuma road at the 3
km mark.

locations.title: Maantee Missile Base
locations.description:
The Maantee Missile was a military facility located in Maantee village. It was initially
established in 1940 as a coastal artillery battery, and later served as a missile division from
1960 to 1993. The base was part of the Soviet Union's air defense forces and was
responsible for operating S-75 surface-to-air missile systems. The base was
decommissioned in 1993, following Estonia's restoration of independence.

locations.title: Mändjala Beach
locations.description:
Mändjala Beach is the biggest and most popular sandy beach in Saare County, Estonia. It is
500 meters long and has warm and shallow water, making it a safe and enjoyable spot for
families. The beach is known for its relatively warm seawater, which is carried to the shore
by southern winds during the summer.

locations.title: Vooremägi
locations.description: Vooremägi is a hill that reaches 10–11 m above the mouth of Väike Emajõgi and provides a nice view over the area. The hill, which is made of layered sand and gravel, used to have a castle on top of it but almost nothing remains of that era . Due to its high relative height of about 50 meters and the forest surrounding it, it stands out from afar.

locations.title: Rõngu Missile Base
locations.description: Built in 1968, the missile base called “Gallipod”, was in use until 1985. Currently it is mostly not in use, but there is a wood factory in the old officers building.The campus has many separate buildings (including garages, depositories, bomb shelters) to discover, which are in quite good shape, minimal rubble.

locations.title: Liivamäe Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on a high sand outcrop in the primeval valley of Elva, this camping ground has a picturesque view of the surroundings opening up to the ancient valley. There are two picnic tables, a fireplace with a grillrest, benches, stairs, shelter for firewood and a dry toilet. The Elva river primeval valley hiking trail and Lake Viti hiking trail pass the camping ground.

locations.title: Vitipalu Lookout Tower
locations.description: The 20 m observation tower is located on one of the highest hills of the Vitipalu esker field in Elva Landscape Reserve and offers a beautiful view of the varied landscape of the surrounding area. There are several hiking opportunities around on the Vapramäe-Vellavere-Vitipalu trails.

locations.title: Jõeharu Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground located near Elva River between a forest and a meadow features one bigger and 2 smaller shelters, a fireplace with grillrest, benches and a shelter with firewood. The Fairy hiking trail starts from the campsite and the Elva river primeval hiking trail passes it. You can find diverse landscapes, the floodplain of Laguja, Tihase stream and beautiful pine forests.

locations.title: Illi Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is located in a beautiful pine forest near Elva and has 2 bigger and 5 smaller shelters, 5 fireplaces with grillrest, benches, firewood shelter and a dry toilet. The Elva river primeval valley hiking trail passes the camping area and you can find diverse landscapes all around.

locations.title: Pangodi Camping Ground 2
locations.description: The camping ground is situated in the Pangodi forest park next to the Pangodi Lake and features a picnic table with shelter, an outdoor fireplace, benches, a woodshed and a dry toilet. This is a little more secluded compared to the other Pangodi camping ground. The Pikksaare hiking trail (1 km) passes the camping ground.

locations.title: Pangodi Camping Ground 1
locations.description: Situated next to the Pangodi Lake, the camping ground has 3 outdoor fireplaces, a picnic table with shelter, 3 fire rings with a barbecue grill, benches, a woodshed, a dry toilet and a volleyball court. This is the busier of the two Pangodi camping grounds. It is possible to hike on the nearby Pikksaare hiking trail (1 km) and see the Lake Pangodi and the forest park.

locations.title: Palumägede Camping Ground
locations.description: A nice lakeside camping ground with a narrow strip of sandy beach on the lakeshore and the nearby hiking trail provides a fascinating walk across a versatile terrain. There is a picnic table with shelter, benches, an outdoor fireplace, a fire ring with a barbecue grill, a firewood shelter and a dry toilet. The camping ground is on the Palumägede hiking trail.

locations.title: Kurepalu Beach
locations.description: A nice little beach with a floating bridge, a pier and all the necessary facilities. The water in the lake gets deep quite quickly and the shore is rather steep. The beach is open to the south and therefore sunny the whole day.

locations.title: Kabina Beach
locations.description: The beach is located next to the former Kabina Sand Quarry and it is used for swimming, fishing and launching boats into the water.

locations.title: Kaagvere Manor
locations.description: Kaagvere manor and the later children’s special school was first established in 1544 and closed in 2009. There is a lot to discover, however, the building is in very poor condition. There are other buildings around the manor, such as the servant’s house, the brewery and the gardener’s house.

locations.title: Rõka Lookout Tower
locations.description: The lookout tower is 28-meters tall and provides a great view over the old and diverse Järvselja forest that surrounds the area. The Järvselja hiking trail passes close by to here.

locations.title: Ahvena Camping Ground
locations.description: A very simple camping ground, that can only be reached by boat. When you use it, make sure to bring your own camping stove (there is a base for the camping stove) or campfire material. There is a fire ring with a grillrest, benches and a boat mooring. Non-drinkable water can be taken from the river Emajõgi.

locations.title: Koosa Camping Hut
locations.description: Located at the crossing of the Emajõgi and Koosa rivers, the camping hut is only accessible by boat. There is a fire ring, a forest hut, benches and an outdoor toilet. Non-drinkable water can be taken from the river.

locations.title: Koha Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping site is located on the river Emajõgi and can only be reached by water. There is a fire ring with grillrest, benches, a base for a camping stove and a boat mooring. Non-drinkable water can be taken from the river. Be sure to bring your own camping stove or campfire material.

locations.title: Latika Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground, which is located on the river Emajõgi and can only be reached by water. There is a fire ring with a grillrest, benches, a base for a camping stove and a boat mooring. Non-drinkable water can be taken from the river. Be sure to bring your own camping stove or campfire material.

locations.title: Kantsi Campfire Ground
locations.description: This is a small campfire ground next to the River Emajõgi. The campfire site offers a covered sitting area and a firemaking area. It is not recommended for over-night camping.

locations.title: Kalevipoja Säng Hill
locations.description: The name of the prehistoric fortress hill means “the bed of the son of Kalev”. It is 10-15 m in height and bordered by the Alatskivi river. The earlier settlement of the castle belongs to the early metal age, but the main period of use has been the end of the 1st millennium and the beginning of the 2nd millennium. It probably played an important role in prehistorical wars.

locations.title: Alatskivi Lake Crossing
locations.description: This is a small pedestrian bridge in the middle of the Lake Alatskivi and it provides a nice view over the surrounding area. The location is good for photography or enjoying the scenery.

locations.title: Kallaste Sandstone Walls
locations.description: This is the longest Devonian sandstone outcrop in Estonia. It is about 930 metres long and the maximum height is 8 metres. When the water levels rise during high waters, the waves chip away the outcrop, creating many caves in the sandstone. The largest of these are more than 1.5 m high and up to 6 m deep. According to legends, an ancient and magical smith used to live in these caves.

locations.title: Kallaste Beach
locations.description: Located next to the Lake Peipsi in the Kallaste village, this sandy beach is a good place for swimming or enjoying the scenery of the lake. The settlement of Kallaste has two beaches, this is the quieter beach of the two.

locations.title: Kallaste Main Beach
locations.description: Located next to the Lake Peipsi in the Kallaste village, this is a sandy beach which is a good swimming spot or having a rest from a trip. The settlement of Kallaste has two beaches, this is usually the busier beach of the two.

locations.title: Kärgandi Camping Ground
locations.description: The campfire site is located in Tartumaa, on a beautiful clearing surrounded by diverse forests with varied terrain, including the Selgise mountains. The location is ideal for hiking and foraging for berries and mushrooms in the fall. The site includes a covered picnic area with tables and benches, an open fire pit with a grill, additional benches, a woodshed with sleeping facilities in the attic, and a composting toilet. The site is also accessible via the Selgise bike trail.

locations.title: Kukemetsa Camping Hut
locations.description: The camping hut is located next to a gravel forest road and nearby are the Selgise cycling trail and Peraküla-Aegviidu-Ähijärve hiking trail. It is surrounded by a forest rich in wild berries and mushrooms. The site offers a hexagonal hut with a fire ring and benches, a picnic table with shelter, a woodshed with a sleeping loft, and a composting toilet.

locations.title: Elistvere Lookout Tower
locations.description: Located on the 2 km Elistvere study trail inside of the Elistvere animal park, which has many information boards and observation platforms, nearby is the Elistvere campfire site. There is a boardwalk going up to the observation tower, where you can see various biocommunities and the bird-rich Lake Elistvere.

locations.title: Raigastvere Lookout Tower
locations.description: The 18 m lookout tower is located on the Raigastvere drumlin and offers a spectacular view to the Vooremaa area with its diverse landscape - drumlins, lakes and forests.

locations.title: Kukulinna Beach
locations.description: Located on the shore of Lake Saadjärv, the beach is quite interesting. It goes deep smoothly, similar to the sea. Due to that, the water is also quite warm. Nearby you can find the Kukulinna manor, which was established in 1553. The wooden main building, which was constructed at the end of the 19th century, was a rare example of a wooden Tudor building.

locations.title: Lähte Lookout Tower
locations.description: The Lähte Lookout Tower is a 15-meter tall tower situated on top of a hill, offering a stunning panoramic view of the surrounding area. Located next to the Lähte sports trails, visitors can enjoy hiking and other outdoor activities as well. The tower provides a breathtaking view of the surrounding forests, fields, and waterways, and on clear days, you can even see as far as the city of Tartu.

locations.title: Naela Camping Ground
locations.description: A campfire site on the right bank of the Pedja river and it can only be accessed from the river by boat.  The camping ground has a campfire site, a table and benches and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Tenno Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is the starting point and destination of Tüki cycling trails. A characteristic feature of this campsite is the interconnected dredged pond with bridges across. There are two table-benches with a canopy, an open campfire with a grillrest, a barn and a firewood shelter, dry toilet. The surrounding terrain is very versatile and nearby is a system of hollowed ponds.

locations.title: Tiksoja Camping Ground
locations.description: This camping area is close to the city of Tartu and has hiking trails nearby. The camping ground has two picnic tables with roofs, two outdoor fireplaces, benches and a dry toilet. It is recommended to bring your own barbecue charcoal and/or firewood.

locations.title: Emajõe Beach
locations.description: Located on the left bank of the Emajõgi River, the beach is a very popular place among locals. There is a sandy beach for sunbathing. On the city side of the beach, you will find the Lodjakoda, which operates as centre for building historical ships.

locations.title: Aruküla Caves
locations.description: The caves form a labyrinthic system of 1 m to 1.5 m high passages, which are separated by 1 m to 2 m thick pillars and cover about 80 m by 100 m. According to legends the underground passages led to the monastery of Kärkna and the manor of Raadi. The caves are of artifical origin, and were created to mine the white sand.

locations.title: Tartu Town Hall Square
locations.description: The trapeze-shaped central square of Tartu, the Town Hall Square is surrounded by classical buildings. In the 18th century, the square was the location of the most important market in the city - the Great Market. Since 1998, it has been adorned by the fountain known as the Kissing Students.

locations.title: Musumägi
locations.description: Locals call the artificial hill on the Toome Hill the Kissing Hill. It was built in the 19th century as part of the park's romantic design. Today, stairs lead to the Kissing Hill and at the top, there is a viewing platform with benches.

locations.title: Verevi Beach
locations.description: One of the best-known sights in the town of Elva is the lovely Lake Verevi with its warm waters. The beach gains additional value from a diving tower and beach volleyball courts. In the immediate vicinity, there is the Elva Sports Park with several illuminated jogging tracks and tennis courts.

locations.title: Piiri Camping Ground
locations.description: The campground is located along a dirt forest road, not far from a pond, making it an ideal resting spot for cyclists and berry pickers between the Kaiu and Selgise trails. The site features a sheltered table, two benches, an open fire pit with a grill rack, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Pedeli River Park
locations.description: A nice park in the middle of the city of Valga with some playgrounds etc. The Pedeli rive flows right in the middle of the park.

locations.title: Tehvandi Lookout Tower
locations.description: The height of the tower is 34 metres and it is 218 metres above sea level (second highest viewing platform in Estonia). The viewing platform is accessible through the tower's spiral staircase as well as by using a lift. You can see beautiful panoramic views of the Otepää Highlands. This requires a ticket costing 1-3 euros.

locations.title: Vilaski Missile Base
locations.description: Located in the Vilaski woods, this missile base was the only underground starting complex with four 37 metre deep missile bays in Estonia. The starting complex has been demolished and two of the missile bays have been filled with soil. The missile depot is still in use, as well as a few other buildings in the campus, others are disintegrating, but still offer quite an interesting view. There is a partially preserved passage between two of the missile bays, which is possible to access.

locations.title: Line of Defence
locations.description: Built in 1944, the northern part of the line ends at Lake Võrtsjärv and ran to the left bank of Väike Emajõgi. To build the defence line, the Germans used Russian prisoners of war and forcefully recruited the local population. The trenches and fire trenches have been partially restored, the area is used by the Valga Military Theme Park, which led the effort to restore the wartime appearance of the hill.

locations.title: Karula Pikkjärve Camping Ground
locations.description: The campfire site is located in a unique east-west oriented primeval valley by the lake Pikkjärv. It features 2 campfire places with grill rest, dry toilet and woodshed (firewood provided). Nearby you can enjoy the views of the narrow lake with deep valleys covered with old forest.

locations.title: Oore Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated by the picturesque Lake Aheru and is a former forest keeper's place, which now includes a complex of buildings such as a barn dwelling, bunk beds, and barns. It is also located along the Karula 50 km cycling trail. The camping ground offers convenient amenities like dry toilets, fire rings with benches, picnic tables, and firewood, making it a great location for outdoor enthusiasts to enjoy the surrounding nature.

locations.title: Alumati Camping Ground
locations.description: Located by a small forest lake, Lake Alumati, it is well hidden naturally and a bit difficult to find, because the hills conceal the campfire site and the lake, but definitely worth the search. It features a fire ring with benches, dry toilet, small pier and woodshed with sleeping loft. The 20 km Koiva cycling trail goes past near the campfire site.

locations.title: Kõrgeperve Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground is located near the Koiva river and because of that is constantly changing, as the bank is gradually worn away by the water. It features a fire ring with benches, dry toilet and a woodshed. There are multiple hiking trails that go past here.

locations.title: Tellingumäe Lookout Platform
locations.description: the 24 metre wooden observation tower offers a view on the flooded plains of the curvy Mustjõgi River and Latvia. At the foot of the Tellingumägi hill, you can go to the indicated Koiva 20 kilometre cycling trail or the 2 kilometre Koiva cultural heritage walking trail around the hill.

locations.title: Rattaradade Camping Ground
locations.description: This campfire site is the start for 4 bicycle trails (Koiva 20km trail, Karula 50km trail, Mõniste 55 km trail and Estonian-Latvian 60km trail) and 1 heritage trail (Koiva 2 km trail) and is easy to find and use. The camping ground features a picnic table with shelter and a campfire ring.

locations.title: Essemäe Linnajärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of a small forest lake Linnajärv, which is surrounded by a mire with a 80-year-old pine forest. It features 1 fire ring with benches, dry toilet, small pier and woodshed.

locations.title: Plaagi Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground features a fire ring, dry toilet, 2 benches, firewood shelter and waste sorting bin. It is on the long Karula hiking trail. In the vicinity are Lake Ähijärv and lots of old forests with rich biodiversity.

locations.title: Õdri Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of the little Lake Õdri and features a fire ring with grill, firewood shelter (firewood provided), 3 benches, dry toilet, waste sorting bin and shelter. About 300m from the campfire site runs the circular 36 kilometre long Karula hiking trail.

locations.title: Rebasemõisa Camping Ground
locations.description: Situated in the Rebasemõisa villiage. It features 3 benches, a fire ring, firewood shelter (firewood provided), dry toilet. The 7 kilometre Rebase off-roud track starts near the campfire site and also marks the start of the Karula hiking trail.

locations.title: Kogrejärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Due to renovation, access to the campfire site is difficult. It is on the shore of the small forest lake, which is surrounded by an 80-year-old mixed pine forest with numerous good places for gathering mushrooms and berries. It features 1 fire ring with benches, a dry toilet, a woodshed with a sleeping area under the roof and a small pier.

locations.title: Sangaste Missile Base
locations.description: An abandoned Soviet anti-aircraft missile base for S-75 type missiles. Buildings are mostly preserved, plenty of shelters, shooting ranges, earthen embankments and several other buildings to discover.

locations.title: Kiigemäe Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of Lake Pühajärv and near the Pühajärve hiking trail, the camping ground features 2 outdoor fireplaces, a dry toilet and a firewood shelter.

locations.title: Kooliranna Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of the Lake Pühajärv, suitable for camping and swimming and features an outdoor fireplace, a forest hut, a picnic table and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Harimäe Lookout Tower
locations.description: The height of the tower is 28 metres and the highest viewing platform is 24 metres high, it offers views of the Otepää mountains (highest in Estonia) and across the Väike Emajõgi valley to the Karula upland.

locations.title: Rooni Missile Base
locations.description: An old R-12 nuclear missile base, it is currently in a pretty bad shape. The only things still somewhat preserved are: 1 nuclear missile hangar and 4 starting complexes, overall around 20% still preserved.

locations.title: Uniküla Missile Base
locations.description: An old nuclear missile base, which is not in very good shape. Still somewhat preserved is the concrete road with the guard building. The hangars are in ruins, but there is still something to discover (around 20% preserved).

locations.title: Helme Caves
locations.description: Located in the Helme Manor Park, they are a very rare sight in Estonia. The caves have a white layer of sandstone walls standing 3 metres high, some of the better known caves have unfortunately collapsed though. The caves have a long history and over the years they have been dug out and used as shelters as needed. In ancient times the Helme caves were also known locally as the gateway to Hell.

locations.title: Koorküla Caves "Põrguhaud"
locations.description: Koorküla caves are located in the left slope of the Õhne ancient valley, opposite Koorküla park, and cover an area of about a quarter of a hectare. The cave system is carved in Burtnieki sandstone formation, with irregular funnel-shaped depressions on the surface, leading to small underground tunnels and a narrow cave that branches into several passages, most of which are collapsed.

locations.title: Koorküla Valgjärve Camping
locations.description: Located on the shore of Lake valgjärv, you can find beautiful sandy beaches there. The campground is bigger than the second Valgjärve Camping Ground and it features a campfire ring with benches, 2 outdoor fireplaces, 2 picnic tables with shelters, dry toilet, changing cabin and a woodshed (equipped with firewood). The second camping ground is 300 metres away.

locations.title: Koorküla Valgjärve Camping 2
locations.description: Located on the shore of Lake valgjärv, you can find beautiful sandy beaches there. The campfire site features a fire ring and benches, shelter for firewood (firewood provided) and camping space for 2 tents. The first camping ground is 300 metres away.

locations.title: Lasajärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of Lake Lasa, which is surrounded by a bog pine forest, the camping ground features a campfire ring with benches (firewood provided), picnic table with shelter, changing cabin and dry toilet. The lake is quite shallow, so you have to walk nearly 50 metres before it gets deep enough for swimming.

locations.title: Tündre Järve Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of the Lake Tündre, the camping ground features a shelter, a dry toilet, an outdoor fireplace, a woodshed and a wooden pier. The lake is rich in fish and the water is convenient for swimming.

locations.title: Tündre Suurekivi Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore of the Lake Tündre and surrounded by a dense spruce forest, the camping ground features a shelter, a dry toilet, an outdoor fireplace and a firewood shelter (firewood provided). The campfire site has got its name from the large rock on the lakeshore.

locations.title: Taagepera Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground features a shelter, an outhouse and 2 outdoor fireplaces (firewood provided). The Taagepera hiking trail starts and ends at the campfire site. In the vicinity is also the Taagepera Castle with its species-rich park and burial place for manor lords.

locations.title: Helme Castle Ruins
locations.description: The castle is thought to have been built in the first half of the 14th century, parts of its walls with their distinctive window openings remain standing today. According to legend, a young virgin was entombed within its walls in order to further strengthen it. The castle was destroyed in 1658 by the Swedes. In the valley below the castle is the Doctor's Spring, whose waters are said to cure seven different ailments.

locations.title: Väike-Munamäe Lookout Tower
locations.description: Väike-Munamäe Lookout Tower provides a panoramic view that extends over 50 km on a clear day from the top of the 207.5 m high hill. The absolute height of Väike Munamägi or Otepää Munamägi is over 80 meters. There is also a spring located on the northeast slope of the hill that has been considered the source of the Small Emajõgi River since ancient times.

locations.title: Pühajärve Beach
locations.description: At the shore of the beautiful Lake Pühajärve is a beach with clean sands, a pier, beach volleyball courts and gazebos. There are hiking trails in the Pühajärve park next to the lake.

locations.title: Harimäe Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground located in the middle of a thick forest features an outdoor fireplace, a dry toilet and a shelter. Next to the campground is the Harimäe viewing tower (28 metres high)where you can get a very scenic view of the Southern Estonian landscape.

locations.title: Angunina Camping Ground 2
locations.description: Next to the Lake Pühajärv, the camping ground features a covered fireplace (firewood provided), tables and benches, a wooden shed with a sleeping possibility and an outhouse. Nearby starts the Lake Pühajärv 14 kilometre hiking trail.

locations.title: Angunina Camping Ground 1
locations.description: Located on the shore of the Lake Pühajärv, the camping ground features an outdoor fireplace, a picnic table, a shelter and a dry toilet. Nearby starts the 3.5 kilometre Murrumetsa hiking trail and 14 kilometre Lake Pühajärve hiking trail and there is also the Angunina second campground.

locations.title: Metsniku Missile Base
locations.description: Soviet missile base, where the anti-aircraft missile nuclear warheads were kept. These days the buildings are partially used as an urban battle training ground, mostly looted, the condition of the main structures is generally satisfactory. Currently some of the area has been taken to be used as a shooting range but the rest should be fine.

locations.title: Tarvastu Castle Ruins
locations.description: A teutonic castle built in the 14th century. Only small relics still remain - castle walls, a fragment of a huge tower called dansker and the moat around the ruins.

locations.title: Tarvastu Lookout Tower
locations.description: A lookout tower in the Tarvastu polder (a dam which boarders a meadow on one side and Võrtsjärve lake on the other.  From the tower you can see the shores of Võrtsjärv. Near it are also some nature trails (Kivilõppe and Tarvastu).

locations.title: Rutu Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground features a tent camping area, outdoor fireplace, firewood shelter, picnic area and dry toilet. In the vicinity you can find the Rutu hill, which does not offer a panoramic view, but is a decent hike with nice forest paths.

locations.title: Teringi Camping Ground
locations.description: This camping ground has a large shelter at the campfire site and enclosed camping area for safe accommodation. It is located at the beginning of the 4.5 km Teringi study trail, which passes through a lot of different interesting terrain, including bog islands. 1.5 km away you can find a very clean bog lake, where you can go for a swim.

locations.title: Ruhijärve Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shore to the Ruhijärve lake, which features clean water, a sandy shoreline and many water-lilies in the summertime. The camping ground is equipped with a fireplace with benches, wooden pier and a dry toilet. It is equipped with firewood.

locations.title: Maimu Cave
locations.description: The cave is developed in an outcrop of yellowish-red sandstones. To get there, you have to walk through a farmhouse yard, which is allowed. According to legend, the devil lives in the cave.

locations.title: Hendrikhansu Cave
locations.description: A collection of caves and sandy outcrops. The largest outcrop is called the Hendrikhansu Hell, where it is not recommended to swear.

locations.title: Tuhalaane Church
locations.description: Initially built in 1870-1873 from field rocks, the church was used as a place of worship by people of the Apostolic-Orthodox religion. The church was abandoned after it had suffered extensive damage that was caused by a fire started by a lighting strike.

locations.title: Paistu Valley
locations.description: There is a hiking trail taking you through the sights of the valley. There are several outcrops, the largest is called Loodi Hell.

locations.title: Loodi Camping Ground
locations.description: Located on the shores of the reservoir behind the old Loodi manor, where the hiking trail leading to Paistu valley begins. The camping ground features a shelter, pincnic table, dry toilet, 1 campfire ring and 1 outdoor fireplace. In the vicinity you can see species-rich varied spruce and pine forests, steep slopes of the primeval valley and the sandstone outcrop called Loodi Hell.

locations.title: Sinialliku Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground features a Shelter, dry toilet, outdoor fireplace. The safe and separate camping area is big enough for a larger group. Nearby are Sinialliku springs, hill fort and Lake Sinialliku. A swimming place can be found at the foot of the hill fort.

locations.title: Läti Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground features an Observation tower, fire ring with barbecue grill, shelter, dry toilet and firewood shelter. Sights nearby are Läti flooded meadow, Halliste River, semi-natural habitats, traditional village landscape. Maximum number of tents allowed is 2.

locations.title: Tõramaa Camping Ground
locations.description: The site is situated in a forested area and is a popular spot for camping, picnicking, and enjoying nature. It has a fire ring, firewood shelter, and an outhouse for visitors.

locations.title: Tõramaa Lookout Tower
locations.description: The Lookout tower is located on the Tõramaa wooded meadow trail. On the other side of the meadow, there is the Tõramaa camping ground by river Halliste, where it is possible to swim. Since the observation tower has views to the Soomaa national park, it is possible to see several species of birds and even large animals.

locations.title: Kõrtsi Tõramaa Camping Ground
locations.description: the camping ground features a fire ring with a grill, a shelter with tables and benches, firewood shelter (firewood is provided), dry toilet, Wash- hand place and watering. The Beaver hiking trails starts nearby.

locations.title: Kuresoo Lookout Tower
locations.description: The tower is located on the Ingatsi study trail and you can see great views of the Kuresoo bog from here.

locations.title: Mulgi Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground features a fire ring with a grill, a firewood shelter (firewood provided) and dry toilet. The sights nearby are the emi-natural communities of the Mulgi meadow, ancient trees, and Lemmjõgi River.

locations.title: Oksa Küün Camping Hut
locations.description: The camping hut is an old hay barn. Also features a fire ring with a barbecue grill, a firewood shelter (firewood is provided) and a dry toilet. It is located in the Soomaa National Park, so it is possible to hike in the vicinity.

locations.title: Oksa Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground features a fire ring with a barbecue grill, a firewood shelter (firewood is provided)and a dry toilet. There is an old tavern with an old chimney just a walk away from the campsite. Oksa barn forest hut and hay barn forest hut are also close by.

locations.title: Oksa Ait Camping Hut
locations.description: This is an old granary, where it is possible to take shelter. It also features a fire ring with barbecue grill and firewood shelter (firewood is provided). The forest hut has no heating. The camping hut is located in the Soomaa National Park, so it is possible to hike in the vicinity.

locations.title: Hüpassaare Camping Ground
locations.description: The camping ground which is located in the beginning of the Hüpassaare study trail in Soomaa National Park, features a fire ring with barbecue grill, firewood shelter (firewood is provided), 2 dry toilets, shelter and waste bins. There is a nice wooded meadow in the vicinity.

locations.title: Labidakivi Camping Ground
locations.description: The campfire site amidst the pine forest and sand dunes features an outdoor fireplace, picnic table with shelter, firewood shelter (equipped with firewood) and dry toilet. Nearby Labidakivi is a protected erratic boulder, 3 m high, 6 m wide and 24.5 m in circumference. The eskers near the boulder are interesting hiking places. You can find many cloudberries, bilberries, cowberries and mushrooms.

locations.title: Luite Camping Ground
locations.description: The campfire site is on the historical Sakala Road, with a sandy dune ridge is a remnant of the Baltic Ice Lake. The site features a fire ring, picnic tables, shelter, firewood shelter (equipped with firewood) and a dry toilet. The camping ground is located on the Sakala hiking trail.

locations.title: Lõhavere Camping Ground
locations.description: Located in the Sakala recreation area, the camping ground features an outdoor fireplace, large shelter and a dry toilet. In the surrounds of the hill fort there is a outdoor fireplace, two picnic tables with shelters. There are two sets of stairs to the historical hill fort, and 60 m boardwalk leading to an unmarked forest path.

locations.title: Lõhavere Hill Fort
locations.description: The hill fort of Lõhavere was occupied by a local Estonian chieftain Lembitu during the Late Iron Age. The stronghold has been built on a moraine hill which rises 8 to 10 meters above the surrounding terrain. Encircling the top of the hill is a man-made sand wall, which was originally supported from inside by a 2.5 meter timber wall. There is a trail marked with arrows and a metal stair leads to the main gate of the hill fort.

locations.title: Parika Lookout Platform
locations.description: The observation tower is on the edge of the Parika bog, believed to be about 9,000 years old, which makes it one of Estonia's oldest bogs. and offers a view of the incredible bog landscape and the birds on the lake.

locations.title: Jõesuu Lookout Tower
locations.description: The 15 m high wooden Jõesuu observation tower is located near the Võrtsjärv visitor center at the beginning of the Emajõgi River. The tower offers a good view of Lake Võrtsjärv and the meadows of the Emajõgi River, offering the opportunity to watch a beautiful sunset as well as enjoy a foggy summer morning.

locations.title: Viljandi Beach
locations.description: Viljandi Beach is a popular recreational area located on the shore of Lake Viljandi in the city of Viljandi, Estonia. It offers a sandy beach, a swimming area, a playground, beach volleyball courts, and other amenities for visitors to enjoy.

locations.title: Viljandi Castle Ruins
locations.description: The stone castle started to appear in the place of the former Estonian stronghold in 1224 and got its final shape in the 16th century, but due to wars between Sweden, Poland and Russia, all that is left from the entire order castle today are just a few stone walls. In order to access the castle hills from town, a rope bridge was build from the Kaeuvumägi hill over the 13 m deep trench in 1931. From the ruins, you have the most beautiful view over Lake Viljandi.

locations.title: Lüübnitsa Lookout Tower
locations.description:
The Lüübnitsa Lookout Tower is a tall structure built on the southern end of the village of
Lüübnitsa. It offers a great view of the village and Kulkna (Kolpino) island across the bay.

locations.title: Laossina Beach
locations.description:
This is a quiet beach on the eastern shore of Estonia right next to the Lake Pihkva. It is a
sand and grass covered beach with sitting areas and clothing booths.

locations.title: Velna Lookout Tower
locations.description:
The Velna Lookout Tower is a 5-story tower near Estonia's eastern border, offering stunning
views of the surrounding pine forest and bog.

locations.title: Värska Camping Ground
locations.description:
Värska campfire site is a great vacation spot by the lake in Setomaa, Estonia. Visitors can
swim, play ball, hike, and ride bikes on the Laskevälja bike trail. The site has 3 covered and
1 open fireplace with a grill, tables and benches, a dry toilet, and a shelter for firewood.

locations.title: Poogandi Camping Ground
locations.description:
This is a peaceful campsite located in the middle of the Mustoja forest, by the Poogandi lake.
It is a great resting place for those traveling along the Laskevälja bike trail or the RMK
Peraküla-Aegviidu-Ähijärve hiking trail. The site offers an open fireplace with a grill, a shelter
for wood, a table-bench with a canopy, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Lõunalaagri Camping Ground
locations.description:
Lõunalaagri lõkkekoht is a campfire site located on the Lõunalaagri hiking trail by a reservoir.
It has covered fire pits, benches, a dry toilet, and a shelter for firewood. The surrounding
area is good for berry and mushroom picking.

locations.title: Piusa Camping Ground
locations.description:
The campfire site is located near the historic Piusa Caves in Võru County, Estonia. It
features a covered fireplace, tables and benches, a shelter for firewood, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Obinitsa Juudatare
locations.description:
This is a sandstone outcrop and cave located on the shore of Lake Obinitsa in Estonia,
which was carved by a stream. According to legend, the cave was inhabited by evil spirits
that prevented the construction of the Obinitsa church. Locals caught the spirits in the act
and with the help of a priest, rid themselves of the spirits' misdeeds.

locations.title: Meremäe Lookout Tower
locations.description:
The Meremäe Lookout Tower is located on Meremäe hill between Meremäe village and
Vastseliina castle, standing at 204 meters above sea level. The 20-meter tower offers
stunning views of Petseri Monastery's church domes and the beautiful Setomaa fields and
forests, regardless of the weather.

locations.title: Make Camping Ground
locations.description:
The campground is located in the southern part of Piusa River primeval valley landscape
reserve, next to the impressive Make or Möldri Wall. The shore here is 20 meters high, and
the height of the outcrop is 14 meters. The site has an open fireplace with a grill, benches,
and a shelter for firewood.

locations.title: Rebasemäe Camping Ground
locations.description:
Rebasmäe forest cabin is located near Rebasmäe creek. The cabin has a fireplace and
sleeping platforms, and there is an open fire pit with a grill next to it. Additionally, there is a
woodshed and a dry toilet nearby. Camping is also allowed at the site, and visitors can try
the spring water.

locations.title: Ilumetsa Meteor Craters
locations.description:
Ilumetsa is a group of meteoritic craters located in Võru County, Estonia. The largest of these
craters is Põrguhaud, which was formed approximately 6,600 years ago and has a diameter of
up to 80 meters and a depth of 12.5 meters. The crater is covered by a thick layer of peat and
there is a well-maintained boardwalk leading to it, with interesting wooden sculptures along the
way.

locations.title: Härma Walls
locations.description:
The locals call two high sandstone outcrops by the Piusa River the Härma walls. Härma
Upper Wall or Keldri Wall is the highest Devonian sandstone outcrop in Estonia, with a
height of up to 43 meters above the valley floor. The 150-meter-long cliff can be seen up to
19 meters high. Härma Lower or Kõlgusniidu Wall is a 20.5-meter-high protruding rock
formation and one of the most beautiful in the Piusa valley due to its vividly colored clay
layers and majestic pyramid-like shape. Camping and making a fire is possible on the
meadow by the river bend.

locations.title: Härma Camping Ground
locations.description:
The Härma Kõlgusniidu müür is a 20.5 meter-high cliff that protrudes into the Piusa River
valley, known for its colorful layers of clay and majestic pyramid-like shape. There is a nice
campsite by the river where you can set up a tent and make a campfire. The campsite

features an open fireplace with a grill, covered picnic tables and benches, a shelter for
firewood, and a toilet.

locations.title: Kirikumäe Camping Hut
locations.description:
The campsite is located by the beautiful Lake Kirikumäe, and it is a great stop for hikers on
the RMK Peraküla-Aegviidu-Ähijärve trail. The campsite offers a shelter made of thick logs
from Haanjamaa, and a platform for spending the night. There is one open fireplace with a
grill, one covered fireplace, benches, a sheltered wooden cabin with platforms for sleeping,
and a dry toilet. However, there is no fireplace in the wooden cabin.

locations.title: Suuremäe Camping Ground
locations.description:
This campsite is located near the Karula National Park Visitor Center in Ähijärve village.
From the camping area, you can go on a 4 km long Ähijärve hiking trail that starts from the
lake parking lot, or a 36 km long Karula hiking trail. The camping area has a parking lot, 2
dry toilets, an outdoor washing area with clean drinking water, 2 open fire pits, 1 covered fire
pit, 3 sets of table and benches, and 2 sets of table and benches with a shelter. There are
also 2 light poles with power outlets.

locations.title: Kavadi Lake Camping Ground
locations.description:
The campfire site is located in the protected Uue-Saaluse manor park, by the picturesque
Kavadi Lake with its jagged shoreline and many islands. The site offers two separate fire
pits, camping possibilities, and a swimming area. One of the fire pits is located on a hill with
a beautiful view of the lake. The site includes 2 open fire pits with grilling grates, a shelter
with a table and benches, 2 firewood shelters, benches, a changing room, a swimming
bridge, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Vaskna Camping Ground
locations.description:
The Vaskna lake campfire site is located by Vaskna lake, offering a beautiful view of the
dome landscape. Visitors can enjoy making campfires, camping, swimming, and
appreciating nature. The lake is 242 meters above sea level and has a maximum depth of 10
meters. The campfire site has two benches, a bench table, a shelter for firewood, a changing
room, an open fireplace with a grill rack, a dry toilet and a wooden garbage bin.

locations.title: Haanja Camping Ground
locations.description:
The campground is located near the Suur Munamägi hill in Estonia, the highest peak in the
Baltics. It is a suitable place for making a campfire, having a picnic, and camping while
enjoying the beautiful nature of the Haanja hilly landscape. The site includes benches, a
table, an open fireplace with a grill rack, a shelter for firewood, and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Rõuge Lookout Tower
locations.description:
The height of the Rõuge observation tower "Pesapuu" is 30 meters, and it resembles a tree
with two bird nests. The tower is not only eye-catching during the day but also at night when
special lights illuminate the trunk, "bird nests" serving as viewing platforms, and the "golden
egg" crowning the top of the tower.

locations.title: Vana-Nursi Manor
locations.description:
Vana-Nursi Manor was established in 1688 and the neorenaissance-style main building was
constructed in the 1860s. The manor complex was used as a Soviet missile base after World
War II and many of the outbuildings were destroyed. Today, the manor is abandoned and
unused.

locations.title: Hinni Canyon
locations.description:
This is a unique canyon located in the Haanja Nature Park in Estonia. It is a 15-20 meter
deep and 300-meter-long steep-walled valley with Devonian sandstone. The canyon
features nearly 200 meters of exposed sandstone walls in white and yellow tones, a fast-
flowing Enni stream, fallen trees, a small spring cave, and a narrowing path that creates a
primordial feeling.

locations.title: Tamula Beach
locations.description:
Tamula Lake beach in Võru is a modern and well-maintained sandy beach with dressing
rooms, beach volleyball courts, swimming piers and other amenities. In addition to the nice
beach, the lakeside offers a 800-meter-long beach promenade for a nice stroll.

locations.title: Kubija Lake
locations.description:
Kubija Lake is located on the outskirts of Võru city in the Kubja district. The lake has an area
of 15.1 hectares and a depth of barely 5 meters. It is relatively rich in fish species such as
bream, perch, pike, and some burbot and roach. The lake shore has dressing cabins, toilets,
a bridge, and a diving tower.

locations.title: Verijärve Camping Ground
locations.description:
The camping area with a campfire site and benches is located next to Verijärve lake and is
popular among the local residents. There is also a 1 km hiking trail nearby. The campfire site
has an open fire pit with a grill and a sheltered area for wood.

locations.title: Kautsi Camping Ground
locations.description:
Kautsi metsaonn is a gathering place for hunters and a rest stop for hikers. It is also a great
spot for enjoying the peacefulness of nature and is surrounded by good berry and mushroom
picking spots. The hexagonal cabin features benches, a fireplace, and there are additional
amenities such as a woodshed, dry toilet, and a fire pit with a grill.

locations.title: Karula Pikkjärve Camping Ground
locations.description:
Karula Pikkjärve Campfire Sites are located on the shore of a lake in a very peculiar east-
west oriented primeval valley. The lake shores are high and mostly sandy. It is a quiet place
for making a campfire, camping, and enjoying the beauty of the lake. Both campfire sites
have a covered fireplace with a grill rack, and in the second campfire site, you can also
camp in a tent. Both sites have a woodshed and a dry toilet.

locations.title: Veski Camping Ground
locations.description:
The Veski campfire site is located on the shore of the picturesque Lake Ähijärv. It provides
access to a 4 km long hiking trail around Lake Ähijärv and a 36 km long Karula hiking trail.
The site offers a covered fire ring, a shelter for firewood, a dry toilet, a trash bin, benches,
and a table-bench set with a canopy.

locations.title: Alakonnu Camping Ground
locations.description:
Alakonnu campfire site is located on the shore of Ähijärve. It's a great spot for both
holidaymakers and fishermen. In case of rainy weather, it's possible to make a fire under the
wooden shelter. The campfire site has a covered fireplace, a dry toilet, a trash bin, a shelter
for firewood, 3 benches, a table-bench set, and 1 additional shelter.

locations.title: Peräjärve Camping Ground
locations.description:
Located close to the Lake Peräjärv. From the campfire site you can head towards the 4 km-
long Peräjärve forest trail, the long Karula hiking trail, and the 38 km-long Karula cycling trail.
Camping at the campfire site is not possible, but there are designated camping areas nearby

at Ubajärv and Ähijärv. The campfire site features an open fire pit with a grill, a dry toilet, a
shelter for firewood, 4 benches, and a trash bin.

locations.title: Lõunatipi Camping Ground
locations.description:
Located on the banks of Peetri River at the southernmost point of the Republic of Estonia,
this is a good resting place for both the RMK Peraküla-Aegviidu-Ähijärve hiking trail and
local nature trail visitors. A forest hut provides more efficient shelter. There is an open fire pit
with a grill grate, a shelter for firewood, tables and benches, a forest hut, and a dry toilet.
There is no fireplace in the forest hut.

locations.title: Tursa Radio Station
locations.description: This used to be a radar station during the Soviet era which was used for surveillance. Currently the area stands in ruins with a few buildings left.

locations.title: Pähni Kõlakojad
locations.description:
The Forest Megaphones, also known as Metsa Kõlakojad, is an installation consisting of
three large wooden megaphones located in the forest of Estonia. Built in 2015 as a
collaboration between the Estonian Academy of Arts and the State Forest Management
Centre, these structures were designed to amplify the sounds of the surrounding forest and
provide a unique experience for visitors to connect with nature.

locations.title: Pähni Camping Ground
locations.description:
This is a hexagonal wooden hut located on the shore of Pähni reservoir. The Pähni nature
trail passes by the hut, which begins at the visitor center and is 1.5 km long. The hut has
circular benches and a covered fireplace with a grill. Outside, there are picnic benches under
a roof, a covered fireplace with a grill, an open fireplace with a grill, a woodshed with a
covered platform, and an outdoor toilet.

locations.title: Paidra Camping Ground
locations.description:
The campfire site with the possibility of camping is located by the Paidra Lake. It's a great
place to relax in a pine forest. There's 1 open fireplace with a grill, 3 covered fireplaces, a
woodshed with overnight accommodation, and 1 dry toilet.

locations.title: Kotka Crossing
locations.description: A small bridge to cross the Vääna river.

locations.title: Jussi Nõmm
locations.description: Jussi Nõmm, an area in Estonia, bears witness to a rich history and unique ecology. Shaped by the retreating glacier during the Ice Age, it evolved into a landscape marked by hillocks and valleys. The region features a distinctive heathland ecosystem, resilient to forest fires, and has a history as a Soviet military training ground known for infantry, artillery, and tank exercises. The hillocks and geological features offer a visually striking 150-hectare open area.

locations.title: Jussi Linajärv
locations.description: Linajärv, also known as Jussi Linajärv, is a semi-dystrophic lake situated approximately 12.5 km northeast of the settlement Aegviidu. The lake, located in the Pruunakõrv area, features a surface area of 5.5 hectares, a maximum depth of 9.7 meters, and clear, yellowish water with high visibility (4.5 meters). The lake is surrounded by tall, burned pine-covered eastern and western shores, while the northern and southern shores are marshy, with sandy areas on the northern shore. The lake is inhabited by perch, pike, and silver bream

locations.title: Jussi Kõverjärv
locations.description: Jussi Kõverjärv, the southernmost and most beautiful lake in the Jussi lake system, features a unique three-lobed shape. With a surface area of 6.8 hectares, a maximum depth of 7.2 meters, and an average depth of 3.6 meters, the lake has steep shores on the eastern and northwestern sides, while the rest has shallow peat banks.
