The trail also passes by the Witch's House and the Adventure Trail.
The Primeval Valley Trail begins and ends at the Tartumaa Health and Sports Center.

journeys.title: Viti Lake Hiking Trail
journeys.description: The Viti Lake Hiking Trail is a 2.5 km trail that begins at the parking lot on Vitipalu Road. It winds through the hilly terrain to reach the Viti Observation Tower. From there, it follows the Tartu Marathon trail for a few hundred meters and then leads to the Ürgoru Campfire Site, located above the Elva Primeval Valley's sandstone cliff. The trail then continues along the edge of the Primeval Valley before returning to the parking lot. Although the trail has both ascents and descents, it is suitable for hikers of all levels. The trail is marked with RMK directional signs and yellow paint markings on the trees.

journeys.title: Valgesoo Hiking Trail
journeys.description: Valgesoo Nature Study Trail is an excellent choice for learning about the development and vegetation of bogs and observing pine tree plantations. This circular trail is 1.8 km long and winds its way around Valgesoo Bog. On the trail, you can observe bog vegetation, the "Parunimännik" (Baron's Pine Forest), and the rich forest edge with broadleaf trees. The trail is accessible for wheelchairs and baby strollers along its entire length. At the beginning of the hiking trail, there is an observation tower and a campfire site.

journeys.title: Kiidjärve Hiking Trail
journeys.description: The 11.8 km long hiking trail starting from the Kiidjärve Visitor Centre runs along the picturesque banks of the Ahja River, offering great views of various sandstone outcrops (such as Sõnajalamägi, Palanumägi, Oosemägi, Laaritsamägi, Mõsumägi, and more). The trail follows one riverbank up to Saesaare Dam, crosses a bridge there, and returns along the other riverbank. There is a campfire site called Sõnajala along the trail where camping is allowed. The hiking trail at times runs along high riverbanks and features steep ascents and descents, so caution is advised.

journeys.title: Taevaskodade Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Taevaskoja Hiking Trail is located in the Ahja River Primeval Valley Landscape Protection Area. The trail starts at the Taevaskoja parking lot and follows a circular route along both banks of the Ahja River, featuring the eye-catching Little and Big Taevaskoja outcrops, spring caves, and picturesque views.
At the beginning of the hiking trail, there is an information board with a map of the trail and the recreational area. Throughout the trail, directional signs are provided in Estonian, English, and Russian. Visitors using wheelchairs or strollers can take an alternative route to Big Taevaskoja, which is 3 km in length.

journeys.title: Roiupalu Study Trail
journeys.description: Roiupalu Nature Trail (3.2 km) offers a diverse experience of South Estonian characteristics. Along the trail, you'll encounter the meandering Hatiku Stream, breathtaking views of the Ahja River and sandstone cliffs, the unique Linajärve bog, known as the "cork fir," and a record pine tree. The trail also features illustrations about small mammals. The route is marked with orange spots on tree trunks. It is of moderate difficulty, with some easy sections, but be aware of the potential for slippery slopes. This trail is suitable for walking only.

journeys.title: Tilleoru Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Tilleoru Hiking Trail (4.5 km) starts in Tilleoru and winds its way along riverbanks, through primeval forests and spruce groves, eventually leading to Merioone Spring. Most of the trail crosses private land.
The landscape protection area on the upper reaches of the Ahja River, between Tille and Möksi mills, aims to preserve the landscape integrity of the Ahja River, its deep primeval valley, tributary gullies, relic highlands, and forests growing on valley slopes – a true masterpiece of nature.
Merioone Spring, located within the Tilleoru Landscape Protection Area, is a natural spring known for its exceptionally pure water. According to folk wisdom, it has a connection to the sea and is considered a sacred place.

journeys.title: Meenikunno Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Meenikunno Hiking Trail is located in Meenikunno Landscape Reserve in Põlvamaa County, and is 5.8 km long. The trail is circular and consists of a 2.4 km-long boardwalk and a 3.4 km-long forest path. There are 13 information points along the trail, providing insights into the bog and forest ecosystems. At the Liipsaare end of the trail, there is an observation tower offering a beautiful view of the raised bog and several bog islets. Near the beginning of the trail, in a forested hummock area by the bog, you'll find the Liipsaare Forest Hut. At Kamarusmägi, there's the Päikeseloojangu Forest House, which can be used for overnight stays with prior arrangement. It offers a scenic view of the eastern part of the bog and the red hues of the setting sun in the western sky. In the center of the bog, there is a 4.7-hectare, relatively round-shaped Meenikunno Bog Lake (ombrotrophic lake).
Meenikunno Landscape Reserve, encompassing Meenikunno Bog, Lake Must and Lake Valg, and capercaillie habitats, aims to preserve the unique landscape, bog ecosystem, and habitats of protected species. Among the protected plants here are species like the yellow loosestrife, floating bur-reed, and several orchids. In terms of birds, in addition to the capercaillie, you can spot species like the ruff, common snipe, white-tailed eagle, and white-backed woodpecker.

journeys.title: Piusa Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Piusa Nature Centre is the starting point for a 1.4 km circular hiking trail that introduces you to the beautiful pine forest and offers the chance to stroll through the refreshing air of the pine woods. Along the trail, you'll find informative signs in Estonian and English that provide insights into protected plant and animal species.

The hiking trail begins and ends at Piusa Nature Centre, where you can purchase a ticket to explore the Piusa Caves. Visitors can also explore the Museum Cave, which is open to the public.

journeys.title: Piusa River Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Piusa River Primeval Valley Hiking Trail is a 15 km one-way trail that can be done as a return journey, totaling 30 km. It runs from the ruins of Vastseliina Castle to Lindorani or in the opposite direction.
The trail initially winds gently through the Vana-Vastseliina manor park and the meadows along the banks of the Piusa River. On the other side, the journey becomes more rugged, with some substantial height differences. Along the trail, you'll encounter old mill sites, isolated farms, and numerous sandstone outcrops (ruins). The highest of these, and also the highest in Estonia, is Härma Mäemine or Keldri Müür, which is a 43-meter-high and 150-meter-long sandstone wall.
Camping and a campfire site are available at Härma Alumine Müür.
Please note that the trail does not have additional lighting, so it's best suited for daytime hiking.

journeys.title: Kirikumäe Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Kirikumäe, a 3.2-kilometer hiking trail, winds around Kirikumäe Lake, locally known as Kerigumäe Lake. This is the best-known swimming spot in the area. On the southern shore of the lake, there's a bog, and the hiking trail crosses the bog, with boardwalks in place to help you navigate the wetter sections. Despite the boardwalks, it's still possible to encounter wet areas, so be prepared.
The trail is equipped with informational boards, campfire sites, and a forest cabin. You can also camp at the Kirikumäe Camping Hut.

journeys.title: Kisejärve Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Kisejärve Landscape Reserve features a 6-kilometer hiking trail that follows the old horse paths and runs along the edges of the swamp. It's advisable to wear waterproof footwear, especially in wet areas.
While hiking on this trail, you can explore the lake-rich landscape, valuable forest and swamp ecosystems, and even observe the activities of beavers.
Good to know: The hiking trail offers a camping area, resting spots, and a picnic area.

journeys.title: Vällamäe Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Vällamäe is a 2.2-kilometer hiking trail where you can experience impressive elevation changes, which are unique in Estonia.
Vällamägi is the second-highest hill in Estonia covered with primeval forest, featuring steep slopes. Its elevation above sea level is 303.9 meters, but if you measure from the base to the summit, it's a relative height of 84 meters, making Vällamägi one of the highest points in Estonia in terms of relative height.
The hill's slopes are very steep in some places, with angles ranging from 35 to 40 degrees. Since 1992, the forests on Vällamägi have been designated as a genetic reserve for spruce trees.

journeys.title: Rõuge Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Rõuge Primeval Valley Hiking Trail (10 km)
One-way hiking trail (10 km one way, 20 km round trip) descends from the swing in Rõuge Primeval Valley, passes a small pond, and goes along the spring-rich slope of the valley. Two water pumps operate on the shore of Liinjärv. The water pump is a "perpetual motion machine" that uses only water energy and can pump water to heights of tens of meters. The trail continues along the village road between Liinjärv and Valgjärv, offering views towards Ööbikuorg (Nightingale Valley) and Linnamäe. It then runs along the southwestern shore of Rõuge Suurjärv – the deepest lake in Estonia. After that, it continues along a chain of lakes (Ratasjärv, Tõugjärv, Kahrila Lake) to Hinni Canyon. This section of the trail can be narrow and challenging in places.

journeys.title: Luhasoo Nature Trail
journeys.description: This 5km long trail located in Võrumaa, Haanja-Karula Recreation Area Luhasoo, the only bog nature reserve in Võrumaa, is interesting because it allows you to see all stages of bog formation: from lowland bog to raised bog. To explore Luhasoo, there's a 5-kilometer nature trail with a boardwalk that makes walking in the wet lowland and bog areas easier. The trail passes by a bog lake and leads to a mineral mound where you'll find a cozy forest cabin. You can also read interesting information about nature from the Estonian and English informational boards along the trail.

journeys.title: Paganamaa Study Trail
journeys.description: You can explore these trails on foot, with lengths of either 4 or 4.7 kilometers. All trails start from the Paganamaa campfire site. The highlight of Paganamaa is the chain of lakes in the Piiroru Piiroja area: Kikkajärv, Sarapuujärv, Liivajärv, and Mudajärv. All these lakes are divided by the Estonian-Latvian state border. The area gets its name from the ancient belief that it is shaped like giant footsteps in the land, resulting from the melting of ice patches in the depressions left by the retreating glaciers. These depressions, known as "vanapagana jäljehaud," have diameters ranging from 50 to 100 meters and depths of 15 to 20 meters. The slopes of the depressions can reach angles of up to 25 degrees. In the past, the surroundings of these depressions were supposedly open and the elongated hollows were said to resemble giant footprints when viewed from higher ground.

journeys.title: Ähijärve Hiking Trail
journeys.description: The 4km trail begins at the shores of Lake Ähijärv, the largest lake in Karula National Park with a surface area of 176 hectares. Ähijärve Teerada introduces the local nature, history, and folklore, following former roads used for different purposes: meadow roads, lakeside paths, bike trails, forest roads, winter roads, and horse trails. The trail features 15 information boards, 2 boardwalk sections, a bridge, and is marked with wooden signposts and color markings on the trees.

journeys.title: Verijärve Hiking Trail
journeys.description: A 1 km long hiking trail runs along the shore of Lake Verijärv and through the pine forest surrounding it. This trail provides a short yet informative hiking experience. Along the trail, there are information boards that introduce various plants, birds, mammals, and other natural phenomena in the area. About a few hundred meters from the trail's start, there is an information point and parking area located on the Vastseliina highway. Here, visitors can learn about the protected areas in Võrumaa and find maps of the county's protected areas and hiking trails.

journeys.title: Kütioru Hiking Trail
journeys.description: The 9.6 km long hiking trail starts near the Kütioru Ski Resort's parking area, marked with directional signs. This trail offers a diverse and challenging terrain with steep slopes, making it necessary to allocate four to five hours for the hike. The trail is designed to provide hikers with an excellent overview of Kütioru, the largest and most majestic primeval valley in Estonia. Along the way, hikers can explore old mill sites and other cultural heritage objects. The forested slopes of Kütioru feature over a century-old spruce trees and impressively tall and thick birch trees.

journeys.title: Koigi Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The 4.8-kilometer hiking trail in Saaremaa, Estonia's largest bog, offers a wonderful opportunity to explore this unique natural landscape. The trail includes paths made of wood chips and boardwalks, leading you around Pikkjärv, a picturesque lake within the bog. Near the lake, you'll find a scenic viewing platform, ideal for taking a break and enjoying a small picnic. Additionally, there's a 9-meter-high observation tower along the trail that provides stunning views of the beautiful bog. During the autumn, lingonberries grow by the side of the trail.
Fun fact: According to local folklore, Naistejärv (Lake of Women) was formed from the tears of women, including Piret, the wife of the legendary Estonian hero Suur Tõll. Koigi Lake, on the other hand, is believed to have emerged where the lords of Kareda and Koigi manors had an argument, and the whole manor sank into the lake's depths.

journeys.title: Viieristi Hiking Trail
journeys.description: The 1km nature trail starts along the historical Sõrve post road, at the 35th kilometer of the Kuressaare-Sõrve highway. This educational trail offers insights into the Viieristi nature reserve located in the Sõrve uplands, showcasing old-growth forests and local cultural heritage. The picturesque trail leads across forested dunes, through ancient natural woodlands, and reaches the Kõrgema magic spring. The estimated time needed to complete the trail is around 30-50 minutes.

journeys.title: Allikasoo Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Allikasoo nature trail, starting from the Viidumäe nature reserve center's parking lot, descends along up to an 18-meter escarpment. While hiking through Allikasoo, you'll learn about the unique environment of the bog and how it differs from regular wetlands. You'll also discover the appearance of the Antsülusjärv lakeshore terrace and the former seabed. Along the trail and in the forest, you'll find many protected peatland plants, including the Saaremaa broomrape (Rhinanthus osiliensis), which grows only in Saaremaa.
The trail is marked with directional signs and blue marks on the trees.

journeys.title: Audaku Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Audaku nature trail is located in the Viidumäe Nature Reserve, passing through characteristic landscapes of the Western part of Saaremaa, including various forest types. You can traverse a species-rich lowland swamp on a boardwalk. The trail also includes Upsi's wooded meadow.
The trail is equipped with information boards and is 2.8 kilometers in length. It starts at the Viidumäe Nature Reserve Visitor Center parking lot.
The Audaku hiking trail may be wet in lower-lying areas during rainy periods.

journeys.title: Harilaiu Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Harilaiu hiking trails offer a glimpse into the diverse and untouched coastal nature of Harilaiu Peninsula, which has been protected for its interesting fauna, flora, and geology since 1924.

You'll experience a variety of coastal landscapes – low horizons, expansive views, the wind, golden sandy beaches, and large waves. The trails start from the RMK Harilaiu recreation site parking lot. There are two options:

A longer, 10 km marked trail traverses the narrow neck of Harilaiu Peninsula and heads northwest towards Kiipsaare Lighthouse. From there, it follows coastal dunes to Kelba Cliff before returning to the trailhead.

A shorter, 6 km trail branches off from the longer route and heads towards Haabade Bay in the middle of the peninsula, eventually rejoining the longer trail in the south. Along the way, you can rest and camp by Laialepa Bay.

Both trails provide an opportunity to experience the unique coastal nature of Harilaiu Peninsula in Vilsandi National Park.

journeys.title: Vilsandi Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Vilsandi Hiking Trails offer an opportunity to explore the beautiful Vilsandi Island, reachable by boat from Papisaare Harbor. There are two hiking trails on the island, and they both start from the observation tower in the center of Vilsandi Island.
The 6 km educational trail takes you along the southern shore, passing by coral reefs to reach the lighthouse and then continues through the northern coast's forested areas. These trails provide an opportunity to experience the unique and diverse natural beauty of Vilsandi Island.

journeys.title: Vesiloo Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Vesiloo Hiking Trail is situated on Suur-Vilsandi Island, which can be accessed by boat from Papisaare Harbor. These trails start from the observation tower in the center of Vilsandi Island and offer various options for hikers.
If you head north (for 8 km), you'll encounter the biology station, a complex monitoring area, and then continue your hike through the water to reach Vesiloo Island. This trail provides an opportunity to explore the diverse ecosystems and research areas found on the island.

journeys.title: Abula Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Abula Nature Trail is a delightful coastal hiking trail that provides a unique opportunity to explore the seashore and its adjacent pine forests. Located on the east coast of Tagalaht in the Koorunõmme Nature Reserve, this 11.2 km long trail can be accessed from the Pidula-Veere road, starting at the Abula camping area.
The trail offers picturesque views of the coastline and pine forests, providing a suitable habitat for various plant and animal species. The hike takes approximately 4-5 hours to complete at a relaxed pace. Along the trail, hikers can learn about the geological history and land use of the region, discover the flora and fauna, and observe signs of wildlife activity in the forested areas. Tagalaht is an important wintering place for waterfowl, making it a significant area for birdwatching during the colder months.
Additionally, the nature trail features remnants from the World War I and II periods, such as trenches, stone-loaded gun emplacements, and other military structures. These historical objects add a unique dimension to the hiking experience, offering insights into the region's past.

journeys.title: Rebastemäe Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Rebastemäe Hiking Trail is located in the oldest part of Hiiumaa and all of Western Estonia, on the Kõpu Peninsula. This area was once a small island in the middle of the open sea about 8000 years ago. The trail offers a variety of unique forests, each influenced by different light and moisture conditions, due to its position on a sandy ridge.
Hikers will encounter various types of forests along the trail, including coastal heath woodlands, species-rich deciduous forests on the slopes, and natural old-growth spruce forests impacted by past storms. The ecosystems of these forests have been shaped by the specific environmental conditions of the sandy ridge, allowing for a rich diversity of plant and animal species. Some of the noteworthy species you may come across include the evergreen juniper, common cuckoo-flower.
The trail is home to various animal species as well. The name "Rebastemägi" (Fox Hill) originates from the foxes that inhabit the sandy dunes and lagoons in the area. Kaplimägi, the second-highest mountain on Hiiumaa with an elevation of 63.5 meters, holds a chapel with a history rooted in local folklore, offering a cultural and historical perspective.
This nature trail features numerous stairs and two viewing platforms that allow hikers to enjoy scenic views of the sea. If time is limited, you can visit the Rebastemäe viewing platform for panoramic vistas, which overlook the forests toward Saaremaa.
The trail can be accessed via all modes of transportation, and it is a circular path that spans 1.5 kilometers with an elevation difference of 20 meters. There are eight information boards along the trail, providing information in both Estonian and English. Wooden directional signs and blue trail markings guide you along the way. The trail can be completed in approximately one hour and remains relatively dry, making it accessible year-round.

journeys.title: Tihu Study Trail
journeys.description:
Tihu Nature Trail is a one-way trail that leads through the dune valleys to Hiiumaa's largest lake. Along the trail, you will find informative signs that help you understand the process of peat formation. Towards the end of the trail, there is a 200-meter boardwalk that leads to a viewing platform overlooking Tihu Lake. At the viewing platform, you can take a break and enjoy the interesting birdlife and wetland scenery, savor the peace and the sounds of the surrounding nature. In summer, you can admire water lilies, while in autumn, you can pick berries and mushrooms along the trail.
If you wish to spend more time immersed in nature, you will find a cozy forest cabin along the trail where you can stop and rest.

journeys.title: Kärdla Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Kärdla Hiking Trail is a 4-kilometer trail located in the forests near Kärdla on Hiiumaa Island. It is suitable for a leisurely walk or for immersing yourself in nature. The trail is marked with color-coded markings and directional signs. It takes you through various forest ecosystems and around small salt patches. You can enjoy this hike in any season, but please be aware that there are wetter areas on the trail, so it's a good idea to wear waterproof footwear, especially during wetter seasons. There are four footbridges along the trail to help you cross ditches. It takes about an hour to complete the trail at a relaxed pace.
Please note that the Kärdla Hiking Trail is not intended for cycling or motorized vehicles.

journeys.title: Leemeti Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Leemeti Hiking Trail is situated amidst the Leemeti hills and offers an invigorating experience with its uphill and downhill segments. The trail can be completed as a 2-kilometer or 3-kilometer loop, making it suitable for various fitness levels. Whether you're exploring on skis during the winter or on foot during the summer, Leemeti Hiking Trail allows you to immerse yourself in the natural beauty of the area.
As you traverse the trail, you'll encounter diverse forest landscapes, postglacial sand dunes, wet hollows, and various types of forests. In the fall, you can also pick berries and mushrooms along the trail.
The trail is well-marked and is suitable for competitive events. After a rewarding hike or a session of sports, you can enjoy a picnic at Leemeti Forest Hut, which includes a fire pit and seating areas. If needed, the hut also provides overnight accommodation options.

journeys.title: Rumpo Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Rumpo Hiking Trail (6.7 km) is located in the unique landscape of Rumpo Peninsula on the island of Vormsi, which is part of the Vormsi Landscape Protection Area. The peninsula is known for its diverse juniper landscapes of varying density and age, and it hosts rare lichen communities, including the unique habitat of the Arctic Reindeer Lichen. These lichen areas are sensitive to trampling, so care must be taken when exploring the region.
The trail leads to the tip of the peninsula, and along the way, you'll find platforms with stunning views of Hullo and Sviby Bays and the surrounding islands. This trail is mostly an out-and-back route, with a small detour in the central part of the trail for added variety. Visitors will also notice livestock such as cattle, sheep, and horses that graze the peninsula's landscape.

journeys.title: Allika Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
Allika Hiking Trail is a 2 km long trail located in the relatively wet and swampy area of the Vormsi Landscape Protection Area. The trail's main attractions include Suurallikas (the Big Spring) and Raviallikas (the Healing Spring), and you can enjoy views of Prästviigi Lake. The trail is equipped with directional signs and information boards. It consists of dirt paths, boardwalks, and footbridges in some parts. During wet periods, the trail can be muddy and challenging to navigate.
Appropriate footwear for this trail includes rubber boots. There is also a viewing platform with a 2.5-meter-high wooden structure along the trail for taking in scenic views.

journeys.title: Huitbergi Hiking Trail
journeys.description:
The Huitberg Nature Trail (3 km) on the island of Vormsi in Läänemaa, Estonia, takes you through a fascinating landscape on Vormsi Island, one of the most captivating places on the island. This trail leads you to Huitberg, a limestone ridge in the middle of the island, covered with spruce forest.
The ridge is more than 400 million years old and was formed from the remains of tiny sea creatures that lived in a tropical shallow sea during the Ordovician period. You can find fossils in the limestone outcrops, and protected ferns have found a home on the limestone hollows.
The trail is circular, marked with wooden signs, and it offers a unique experience as you explore this ancient landscape on Vormsi Island.

journeys.title: Marimetsa Hiking Trail
journeys.description: The 9 km round-trip trail, starting and ending at the Marimetsa parking area, offers an excellent opportunity to explore various habitats. The path begins on a dirt road, following a forest path, continuing through meadows, then along the banks of the Marimetsa River, and ultimately reaching the edge of the Kureselja Hill bog. From there, a boardwalk leads through lowland, transition, and high bog ridges. Wooden signposts mark the way.

journeys.title: Saare Study Trail
journeys.description: In the Silma Nature Reserve, the primary attraction is the wetlands, especially the reed beds. The Sutlepa Sea is a remnant of geological processes, separated from the sea, with extensive reed fields along its shores. Here, you can listen to the song of warblers and reed crickets, as well as the eerie calls of the bittern, resembling the sound of blowing into a bottle. You can experience all of this on the 1 km round-trip trail.
